title: Fix Python 3.11/3.12 compatibility: update stale deps, fix install docs, repair SKU bug
body:
Pinned dependencies (`numpy==1.24.3`, `pandas==2.0.1`, stale Azure SDK pins) blocked installation on Python 3.11+ and caused silent runtime failures. Install instructions also pointed to the wrong `requirements.txt` path.

## `requirements.txt`
- `numpy==1.24.3` → `>=1.26.0,<3.0.0` (1.24.x tops out at Python 3.11)
- `pandas==2.0.1` → `>=2.1.0,<3.0.0` (2.0.x tops out at Python 3.11)
- `azure-core==1.26.4` → `>=1.32.0,<2.0.0`
- `azure-identity==1.12.0` → `>=1.19.0,<2.0.0`
- `azure-mgmt-core>=1.5.0` → `>=1.6.0,<2.0.0`

## `pullAzureCacheForRedisStats.py`
- **Repaired silent SKU bug**: missing comma between `'ComputeOptimized_X20'` and `'ComputeOptimized_X50'` caused Python to silently concatenate them into a single string, making `ComputeOptimized_X50` unreachable in AMR SKU lookups
- Removed unused imports (`AzureCliCredential`, `os`) and unused `tenant_id` variable

## Docs (`README.md`, `inventory/python/README.md`)
- Fixed install command: `pip install -r ../../requirements.txt` → `python -m pip install -r requirements.txt` (run from repo root)
- Updated stated Python requirement: `3.8+` → `3.11+`
- Added explicit `cd inventory/python` step before running the script

title: fix(bedrock): avoid double-counting cache tokens in Anthropic Messages streaming usage
body:
## Problem

For Bedrock **Invoke** Anthropic **Messages** streaming (`bedrock_sse_wrapper` → `_promote_message_stop_usage`), cached requests could report **roughly 2×** the real prompt token total and inflated spend.

**What went wrong**

1. On `message_stop`, Bedrock sends **uncached** `input_tokens` (e.g. `3`) plus cache breakdown on the merged `message_delta` (`cache_creation_input_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`).
2. `_promote_message_stop_usage` was rewriting `message_delta.usage.input_tokens` as `uncached + cache_creation + cache_read` (e.g. `3 + 17 + 32651 = 32671`).
3. Downstream, `AnthropicConfig.calculate_usage` treats `input_tokens` as **uncached-only** and **adds** cache read/write again to `prompt_tokens`.
4. Result: cache tokens were included in `input_tokens` **and** added again → **double counting** (e.g. `prompt_tokens` `65339` instead of `32671` for the same request).

## Fix

Keep `message_delta.usage.input_tokens` as the **uncached-only** value from `message_stop` (`raw_input`). Still promote `cache_creation_input_tokens` and `cache_read_input_tokens` onto `message_delta` for clients that ignore `message_stop`. `calculate_usage` then adds cache to `prompt_tokens` exactly once.

## Tests

- Assert promoted `message_delta` keeps uncached `input_tokens == 3` with cache fields present.
- End-to-end: SSE through `bedrock_sse_wrapper` → passthrough logging rebuild → `prompt_tokens` and `completion_cost` for `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6`.
- Updated `test_bedrock_sse_wrapper_keeps_usage_in_message_start_and_message_delta` expectations for `input_tokens` after promotion.

title: fix: Intel GPU Dockerfile Python 3.12 compatibility
body:
## Problem

The `Dockerfile.gpu-intel` fails to build on the `intel/oneapi-runtime:latest` base image (Ubuntu Noble, Python 3.12) due to three issues:

### 1. Broken apt dependencies
`intel-level-zero-gpu` has unmet dependencies (`libigc1`, `libigdfcl1`) that are not installable. These packages (`intel-opencl-icd`, `intel-level-zero-gpu`, `level-zero`) are already present in the `intel/oneapi-runtime` base image.

### 2. Python 3.12 pkgutil.ImpImporter removal
`numpy==1.24.0` and old `setuptools` use `pkgutil.ImpImporter` which was [removed in Python 3.12](https://docs.python.org/3.12/whatsnew/3.12.html#removed). This causes:
```
AttributeError: module 'pkgutil' has no attribute 'ImpImporter'. Did you mean: 'zipimporter'?
```

### 3. GPU acceleration display
`inference.py` checks `torch.cuda.is_available()` for the GPU acceleration log message, which returns `False` on Intel XPU even though the GPU is being used.

## Changes

- **Dockerfile.gpu-intel**: Remove redundant Intel GPU packages from apt install
- **Dockerfile.gpu-intel**: Add `pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel` before torch
- **requirements.txt**: Bump numpy from `==1.24.0` to `>=1.26.0`
- **tts_engine/inference.py**: Use `is_gpu(device)` instead of `torch.cuda.is_available()`

## Testing

Tested on Intel Arc B580 (12GB VRAM) running on Unraid 7.1.4:
- Docker image builds successfully ✅
- SNAC model loads on `xpu:0` ✅
- TTS generation produces valid 24kHz WAV audio ✅
- `device.py` detects Intel XPU correctly ✅
- llama.cpp SYCL inference: ~20 tok/s ✅

title: Port repository from Python 2.7 to Python 3.x
body:
Port DRUM repository from Python 2 to Python 3 ✅

- [x] Fix `xrange()` → `range()` in data.py
- [x] Fix `xrange()` → `range()` in model.py  
- [x] Fix `map()` to return list in model.py (line 49)
- [x] Fix lambda tuple unpacking in model.py (line 232)
- [x] Fix `zip()` to return list where needed in data.py
- [x] Fix integer division `/` → `//` in data.py and experiment.py
- [x] Fix pickle file modes 'r'/'w' → 'rb'/'wb' in experiment.py
- [x] Fix pickle file modes in eval/evaluate.py
- [x] Fix pickle file modes in eval/get_truths.py
- [x] Fix `filter()` to return list in experiment.py
- [x] Update README.md to reflect Python 3 requirement
- [x] Test and verify all changes
- [x] Fix zip object indexing bug on line 117

## Summary

Successfully ported the entire DRUM codebase from Python 2.7 to Python 3.x.

### Latest Fix:
Fixed bug in `src/data.py` line 117 where `zip()` objects were being indexed directly. In Python 3, `zip()` returns an iterator that cannot be subscripted. Wrapped each `zip()` call in `list()` before indexing with `[0]`.

**Change:** `zip(*self.train)[0]` → `list(zip(*self.train))[0]`

### Files Changed:
- **src/data.py**: 17 lines (xrange→range, integer division, zip wrapping including line 117 fix)
- **src/model.py**: 20 lines (xrange→range, map/filter wrapping, lambda fixes, integer division)
- **src/experiment.py**: 20 lines (xrange→range, pickle modes, filter wrapping, lambda fixes, integer division)
- **eval/evaluate.py**: 2 lines (pickle mode rb)
- **eval/get_truths.py**: 2 lines (pickle mode wb)
- **README.md**: 2 lines (Python version requirement)

### Verification Results:
✅ All Python files compile successfully with Python 3.12.3
✅ All files pass Python 3 syntax validation (AST parser)
✅ No remaining Python 2-specific patterns detected

The repository is now fully compatible with Python 3 while maintaining all original functionality.

title: Only include words in lex where all characters have a decomposition
body:
Only add words to lexicon if they have a valid decomposition into atoms.

Without this change, the isalpha check would fail to filter out characters from the `american-english.txt` file such as `touché` which result in the following error:

```
    atoms[atom] += FORMULAE[char][atom]
KeyError: 'É'
```

For now, add an additional filter when building the lexicon to skip words like this which contain a character without a decomposition provided.

Also update the input for python3 (`raw_input` -> `input`).

title: feat: Advanced Skills Expansion - Test Generation, Refactoring, Code Review & Distributed Self-Healing
body:
## Overview

This PR significantly enhances EPCP3-O's core skills with production-ready, advanced capabilities across four major areas:

1. **Advanced Test Generation** - Integration, regression, and edge case tests
2. **Enhanced Refactoring** - Language idioms and automated code migrations
3. **Automated Code Review** - Quality scoring and PR feedback generation (NEW)
4. **Distributed Self-Healing** - Cluster monitoring and service mesh support

## 🎯 What's New

### 1. Advanced Test Generation (`tester.py`)

The test generator now goes far beyond basic unit tests:

```python
# Generate integration tests with mocking
integration_tests = await tester.generate_integration_tests(code)

# Generate regression tests to prevent bugs
regression_tests = await tester.generate_regression_tests(code, known_issues)

# Support multiple frameworks
pytest_tests = await tester.generate_tests(code, framework="pytest")

# Edge case coverage
edge_tests = await tester.generate_tests(code, test_type="edge_case")
```

**Features:**
- Smart code analysis extracts functions, classes, and dependencies
- Automatic detection of async code, databases, APIs, and file I/O
- Generates appropriate mocks for external dependencies
- Support for both unittest and pytest frameworks
- Comprehensive edge case testing (null, empty, boundary, unicode, special characters)

### 2. Enhanced Refactoring (`refactor.py`)

Language-specific idioms and automated migrations:

```python
# Apply Python idioms (enumerate, is/is not, context managers)
refactored = await refactorer.apply_language_idioms(code, "python")

# Migrate Python 2 to Python 3 automatically
migration = await refactorer.migrate_code(code, "python2to3")
# Converts print statements, xrange, dict.iteritems(), etc.

# Fix code smells
cleaned = await refactorer.fix_code_smells(code, "python")
```

**Supported Languages:**
- **Python:** enumerate, is/is not comparisons, list comprehensions, context managers
- **JavaScript:** let/const, arrow functions, template literals
- **Java:** diamond operator, try-with-resources
- **Go:** defer, goroutines

**Migration Paths:**
- Python 2 → 3 (print, xrange, dict methods, unicode, raw_input)
- ES5 → ES6 (var to const/let, arrow functions, classes)
- Java 8 → 11 (var, new String/collection methods)

### 3. Automated Code Review (`reviewer.py`) - NEW

Complete automated code review system with quality scoring:

```python
# Comprehensive code review
review = await reviewer.review_code(code, language="python")
print(f"Quality Score: {review['overall_score']}/100")
print(f"Approval: {review['approval_status']}")

# Generate PR feedback
pr_data = {"files": [{"filename": "main.py", "content": code}]}
feedback = await reviewer.generate_pr_feedback(pr_data)
```

**Capabilities:**
- Quality scoring (0-100) based on multiple factors
- Issue detection with severity levels (critical, error, warning, info)
- Security checks: eval/exec usage, bare except, mutable defaults
- Complexity analysis: function length, nesting depth
- Best practices: print statements, magic numbers, TODO comments
- Documentation checking: missing docstrings
- PR feedback with approval recommendations

### 4. Distributed Self-Healing (`healer.py`)

Enterprise-grade self-healing for distributed systems:

```python
# Monitor cluster health
health = await healer.monitor_cluster_health(cluster_config)

# Register services dynamically
await healer.register_service("api-gateway", {
    "address": "10.0.0.1",
    "port": 8000,
    "health_endpoint": "/health"
})

# Auto-heal distributed systems
results = await healer.heal_distributed_system(health)

# Service mesh integration
await healer.integrate_with_service_mesh({"type": "istio"})
```

**Features:**
- Deployment type awareness (standalone, clustered, distributed, microservices)
- Cluster-wide health monitoring
- Service discovery and registration
- Circuit breaker pattern for resilience
- Distributed transaction recovery with rollback
- Network partition detection and handling
- Service mesh integration (Istio, Linkerd)
- Load balancer health check coordination

## 📊 Metrics

- **Files Changed:** 10 (4 new, 6 enhanced)
- **Lines Added:** 2,791+
- **Test Coverage:** 40 tests, 100% passing (19 new tests)
- **Languages Supported:** 4 (Python, JavaScript, Java, Go)
- **Migration Paths:** 3 (Python 2→3, ES5→ES6, Java 8→11)
- **Deployment Types:** 4 (standalone, clustered, distributed, microservices)

## 🧪 Testing

All features are comprehensively tested:

```bash
$ python -m pytest tests/test_enhanced_skills.py -v
# 19 tests covering all new capabilities - all passing

$ python demo_enhanced_skills.py
# Interactive demo showcasing all features
```

## 📚 Documentation

Complete documentation suite:
- **README.md** - Updated with 120+ lines of usage examples
- **IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md** - Enhanced with all new features
- **ENHANCEMENT_REPORT.md** - Comprehensive 315-line completion report
- **demo_enhanced_skills.py** - Interactive demonstration script

## 🎯 Use Cases

### Developer Workflow
```python
# 1. Review code
review = await reviewer.review_code(code)

# 2. If issues found, refactor
if review['overall_score'] < 80:
    refactored = await refactorer.apply_language_idioms(code, "python")
    migrated = await refactorer.migrate_code(refactored, "python2to3")

# 3. Generate comprehensive tests
integration = await tester.generate_integration_tests(migrated)
regression = await tester.generate_regression_tests(migrated)
edge_cases = await tester.generate_tests(migrated, test_type="edge_case")
```

### Production Operations
```python
# Monitor and heal distributed system
cluster_config = {
    "nodes": [...],
    "services": [...]
}

# Continuous monitoring
health = await healer.monitor_cluster_health(cluster_config)

# Automatic healing
if health['issues']:
    results = await healer.heal_distributed_system(health)
```

## 🚀 Impact

This enhancement transforms EPCP3-O from a basic code assistant into an enterprise-grade development and operations platform:

- **Test Generation:** Minutes → Seconds
- **Code Migration:** Manual → Automated
- **Code Review:** Inconsistent → Standardized & Comprehensive
- **System Recovery:** Manual Intervention → Automated Healing

## ✅ Production Ready

- Comprehensive error handling and logging
- Full async/await support
- Type hints throughout
- Professional code organization
- Extensible architecture
- Zero breaking changes to existing functionality

---

**Demo:** Run `python demo_enhanced_skills.py` to see all features in action

**Tests:** All 40 tests passing - `pytest tests/ -v`

title: chore(infra): setup Docker PostgreSQL and secure environment variables
body:
## 🎯 Context
To ensure high scalability and consistency across different environments (preventing the "it works on my machine" issue), the project needed a robust database infrastructure. We transitioned from the default SQLite database to a containerized PostgreSQL environment using Docker. Additionally, sensitive credentials needed to be isolated from the source code.

## 🛠️ What was done
* Created a docker-compose.yml file to spin up a PostgreSQL 15 database container.
* configured python-decouple to manage environment variables securely.
* Updated settings.py to connect to the Dockerized database using variables from a local .env file.
* Ensured the .env file is properly ignored by Git via .gitignore.
* Ran initial migrations successfully into the new database.

## 🧪 How to test
1. Switch this branch: git switch feature/setup-docker-postgres
2. Create a .env file in the root directory containing SECRET_KEY and POSTGRES_PASSWORD.
3. Start the database container: docker-compose up -d
4. Apply migrations: python manage.py migrate
5. Create a superuser: python manage.py createsuperuser
6. Run the server (python manage.py runserver) and log into the /admin panel to verify the catalog tables are accessible.

## ✅ Checklist
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code.
- [x] Migrations were successfully generated and tested locally.
- [x] Environment variables are hidden and not committed.
- [x] Docker container spins up without errors.
- [x] Django successfully connects to PostgreSQL.

title: Python 3 modernizations, optional unrar support, and added minimal Comic metadata Web UI
body:
### Motivation

- Bring the codebase up-to-date for Python 3 compatibility and make external dependencies optional so the library is more robust across environments. 
- Add a small built-in web UI to enable basic comic metadata read/write/scan workflows and ComicVine lookup. 

### Description

- Updated import paths to use package-relative imports and modernized type checks and exceptions (e.g. `isinstance`, `DeprecationWarning(...)`).
- Made UnRAR/unrar-related code more Python 3 friendly by decoding `bytes` outputs, replacing `source.next()` with `next(source)`, returning `bytes` for binary reads, and using `io.BytesIO` for image handling.
- Made optional dependencies safe: added try/except fallbacks for `natsort`, `unrar`/`unrarlib`/`constants`, and `PyPDF2`, and guarded features (e.g. RAR/PDF handling) when those libraries are absent.
- Converted the `UnRAR2` tests to use `pytest` and added a module-level skip if a system `rar`/`unrar` executable is not present.
- Modernized various Python 2 idioms to Python 3 equivalents (`print` function, `unicode`/`str` handling, `urllib.parse`, and small API surface fixes).
- Added a minimal `webui` package with a `ComicVineClient` and an HTTP server (`webui/server.py`) exposing endpoints to scan libraries, read/write metadata, and query ComicVine; included a short `WEBUI.md` usage note.

### Testing

- Ran the UnRAR integration tests via `pytest UnRAR2/test_UnRAR2.py`, which will `pytest.skip` at module level when `unrar`/`rar` is not installed, and reported no failures on environments with an unpacker available. 
- Exercised basic functionality manually via the included doctest invocation (`doctest.testmod(UnRAR2)`) as part of the UnRAR test script; no errors were reported when prerequisites were satisfied. 
- The new web UI and ComicVine client include runtime checks (requiring a `COMICVINE_API_KEY` for ComicVine endpoints) and have been exercised locally with the `webui.server.run()` entrypoint in development.

------

Announcements

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

title: Feat/drop duplicates/ carlos henrique
body:
## 📌 O que foi feito?
Esta PR entrega a primeira funcionalidade real do nosso pipeline de limpeza: a identificação e remoção de registros idênticos na base de dados do Spotify.

## 🛠️ Detalhes Técnicos e Arquitetura
- **Performance no `drop_duplicates`:** Para evitar laços aninhados lentos, empacotei as linhas completas em tuplas (`tuple`). Como tuplas são imutáveis, pude utilizar a estrutura de dados `set` para verificar a existência de duplicatas em tempo constante $O(1)$, tornando a varredura extremamente rápida.
- **Sincronização de Estado:** Após a remoção das duplicatas, o dataset interno da classe `Preprocessing` é atualizado e propagado para as classes especialistas, garantindo que o resto do pipeline trabalhe apenas com dados limpos.
- **Testes Automatizados:** Inclusão do teste `test_drop_duplicates` no arquivo de testes unitários para garantir a estabilidade da função.

## 🧪 Como testar?
1. **Teste Prático:** Rode `python main.py`. Verifique se o console exibe a mensagem com o ícone de vassoura 🧹 informando a quantidade exata de linhas duplicadas removidas da base.
2. **Teste Unitário:** Rode `python -m unittest test_preprocessing.py`. O novo teste adicionado deve passar com sucesso (`OK`).

title: feat: v0.2.0 — public release, cloud-native API server, open-source licensing
body:
AURA was a single-device CLI tool with no license, no deployment story, and a persona tied to internal use. This upgrades it to a public, cloud-native project: free and open-source, accessible from any device via HTTP, deployable anywhere via Docker.

### Public & Open-Source
- **Apache 2.0 LICENSE** added
- Christopher Betts attributed as designer/founder throughout
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` for community readiness
- `setup.py` updated with license classifier, project URLs, author

### Cloud-Native API Server
- `aura/ui/api.py` — HTTP/JSON API built on Python stdlib `http.server` (zero new deps)
- `aura serve` CLI command with `--host`, `--port`, `--token` flags
- `config/aura.yaml` gains a `server:` section (host, port, api_token)
- CORS headers on all responses; optional ****** auth

```bash
aura serve --port 8000
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/chat \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message": "Hello, AURA!"}'
```

Endpoints: `POST /v1/chat`, `GET /v1/health`, `GET /v1/tools`, `GET /v1/version`

### Docker Deployment
- `Dockerfile` (python:3.11-slim, runs `aura serve` by default)
- `docker-compose.yaml` with persistent memory volume and `AURA_API_TOKEN` env var

### Identity & Persona
- `Persona` class gains a `mission` field, injected into the system prompt
- `config/identity.yaml` rewritten: mission-driven backstory, not branded to any public figure, explicitly cloud-native ("travels where it is needed, calls home wherever it sets up")

### Docs
- `README.md` rewritten with mission, cloud deployment, API reference, contributing section
- `docs/deployment.md` added (Docker, cloud providers, web embedding)
- `docs/architecture.md` and `docs/getting_started.md` updated

### Tests
- 12 new tests in `tests/test_api.py` covering all endpoints, auth, error cases
- 58 total tests passing, no regressions

Version bump: `0.1.0` → `0.2.0`

title: feat: Termux one-step setup, modern UI, hide raw backend output
body:
App was difficult to set up (especially on Termux/Android) and the web UI exposed raw LLM output to users, making it confusing for non-technical users.

## New files
- **`install.sh`** — single `bash install.sh` installs Python deps, Ollama, and pulls `phi3` (smallest model, best for phones)
- **`start.sh`** — single `bash start.sh` auto-starts Ollama if not running, launches web UI on port 8080

## Web UI
- **Removed raw backend output** — users now see only parsed, per-file results instead of the full LLM response dump
- **Per-file copy buttons** via `addEventListener` (replaces unsafe inline `onclick` + `JSON.stringify`)
- **Clipboard `.catch()`** — informs users when clipboard API is unavailable instead of silently failing
- **Collapsible "Advanced" section** hides the context/constraints field by default — one input, one button for most users
- **Mobile-first layout** — animated loading dots, full-width CTA on small screens, readable at phone viewport widths

## Config
- Default `WEB_PORT` changed `5000` → `8080` across `config.py`, `.env.example`, and README

## README
- Added 3-command Termux quick-start block at the top of the file

title: Add FastAPI MVP web UI backed by run_pipeline
body:
### Motivation
- Provide a minimal product-like web UI so users can submit questionnaire inputs, run the existing deterministic pipeline, and view/download report artifacts without re-implementing scoring or explanations.
- Keep the web flow consistent with the CLI by using the single `bioage.pipeline.run_pipeline` entrypoint, produce deterministic run folders, and surface disclaimers and non-clinical language prominently.

### Description
- Add a new `app/` FastAPI package with `app/main.py` implementing routes for `GET /` (form), `POST /calculate` (runs `run_pipeline` and renders results), `GET /runs/{run_id}/report`, `GET /runs/{run_id}/download/report.html`, `GET /runs/{run_id}/download/report.pdf`, `GET /runs/{run_id}/charts`, and `GET /health`, plus safe `run_id` validation to prevent path traversal.
- Add Jinja2 templates and static assets under `app/templates/` and `app/static/` (`base.html`, `index.html`, `result.html`, `error.html`, `charts.html`, `css/style.css`) which show required grouped form sections, disclaimers, headline numbers, top drivers/actions, warnings, and links to report and charts.
- Integrate with existing pipeline artifacts by calling `run_pipeline(raw_input, outdir, constants_path, ...)` and reading `result.json`, `explanations.json`, and `inputs_normalized.json`; each submission creates `run_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS` folders under `BIOAGE_OUTPUT_DIR` (default `outputs/web`).
- Add `tests/test_web.py` (FastAPI TestClient) to exercise `GET /`, `POST /calculate`, and subsequent `report` and `download/report.html` endpoints, update `pyproject.toml` dependencies for FastAPI/uvicorn/python-multipart, and extend `README.md` with a "Web App" section documenting start command and env vars (`BIOAGE_CONSTANTS_PATH`, `BIOAGE_OUTPUT_DIR`).

### Testing
- Ran the full test suite with `pytest -q` and it passed, including the new `tests/test_web.py` which covers the form, calculation flow, and artifact routes.
- The web tests are implemented to skip if FastAPI is not available in the environment, keeping test runs fast and robust in CI.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69a85218685c832b8e7e7e187f6b473f)

title: Improve FastAPI form UX with preserved inputs, field-level errors, and BMI autofill
body:
### Motivation

- Improve the web form UX so submissions that fail validation return the user to the same form with their inputs preserved and clear guidance, instead of a generic error page and an empty form. 
- Provide per-field error messages and visual highlighting to make correction straightforward, and add a lightweight client-side BMI convenience that is still re-validated server-side. 

### Description

- Reworked `POST /calculate` in `app/main.py` to read raw form data, build a `raw_input` payload, preserve submitted values in a `form_values` context, and on validation errors re-render `index.html` with `form_errors` and an `error_summary` rather than sending users to an empty error page. 
- Added `app/validation.py` with `parse_validation_error` that converts Pydantic/normalization errors into dotted schema field keys and appends accepted-range hints using `FIELD_RANGES`/`FIELD_HELP`. 
- Updated templates: `app/templates/_form.html` now binds inputs/selects to `form_values`, shows `form_errors` next to fields, applies an `input-error` class to invalid fields, and displays a top-level `error_summary`; `app/templates/base.html` includes the new JS asset; `app/templates/error.html` now gives a friendly fallback and a back link. 
- Added lightweight vanilla JS at `app/static/js/form.js` that auto-calculates BMI from `height_cm` and `weight_kg` (rounded to 1 decimal), respects a manual BMI edit until height/weight change, and bounds calculations to sensible ranges; updated `app/static/css/style.css` with `.input-error`, `.field-error`, and `.error-summary` styles. 
- Tests updated in `tests/test_web.py` to assert preserved values, visible field errors when SBP is out of range, and inclusion of the BMI JS asset. 

### Testing

- Ran `python -m compileall app tests` which succeeded and confirmed modules compile. 
- Ran `pytest -q tests/test_schema.py` which passed. 
- Ran full `pytest -q` which failed during test collection because `starlette.testclient` requires the `httpx` package and installing `httpx` was blocked by the environment network/proxy (install attempt was unsuccessful), so full test-suite run could not be completed in this environment.

------

title: Refactors to modular package with enhanced PHI controls

Introduces tenant and facility scoping for episodic memory and audit storage, enforcing explicit configuration to prevent accidental PHI leaks. Adds granular transport and proxy options for ClinicalTrials.gov queries to ensure PHI-safe retrieval, with POST and proxy routing based on query content. Enforces fail-closed consent checks for external LLM usage with clinical data. Updates documentation to clarify security/operational controls, dependency setup, and containerized workflows.

title: fix: Python 3.9 compatibility for carl-hook.py
body:
## Summary

Fixes #2 — `carl-hook.py` crashes on Python 3.9 (the default macOS system Python) due to [PEP 604](https://peps.python.org/pep-0604/) union type syntax (`X | None`), which requires Python 3.10+.

**Error on every prompt:**
```
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for |: 'type' and 'NoneType'
```

## Changes

- Add `from typing import Optional` import
- Replace all 10 instances of `X | None` with `Optional[X]` (compatible with Python 3.7+)

No behavioral changes — type hints only.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified `python3 carl-hook.py` runs without error on Python 3.9.6
- [x] Verified hook produces correct JSON output with valid CARL rules injection
- [x] No remaining `| None` patterns in codebase

title: Fix Travis CI Build Issues and Improve Docker/Testing Infrastructure
body:
# Fix Travis CI Build Issues and Improve Docker/Testing Infrastructure

## Summary
This PR fixes multiple issues preventing the Travis CI build from completing successfully, including test failures, Docker build hangs, and code quality issues. It also updates dependencies and improves the overall build and testing infrastructure.

## Changes

### 1. Fixed Test Case Failure (Unique Constraint Violation)
- **Issue**: `TestSeeding.test_if_seeding_works` was failing with `UniqueViolation` error when creating participant teams due to duplicate team names generated by Faker
- **Fix**: Modified `create_participant_team()` to accept an optional `team_idx` parameter and include it in team names to ensure uniqueness
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 2. Fixed Non-Interactive Docker Environment Support
- **Issue**: Seeding script was failing in Docker containers with `EOFError: EOF when reading a line` when trying to read user input
- **Fix**: Added `try/except EOFError` handling in `check_database()` to automatically proceed in non-interactive environments
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 3. Fixed All Pylint Errors in seed.py
- **Issue**: Multiple pylint violations including line length, too many arguments, too many locals, unnecessary else statements, etc.
- **Fix**: 
  - Refactored large functions into smaller helper functions (`_create_participant_teams`, `_create_bulk_submissions`, `_create_bulk_leaderboard_data`, `_process_challenge`)
  - Split long lines across multiple lines
  - Removed Python 2 compatibility code (`xrange`)
  - Changed `create_challenge()` to accept a config dictionary instead of many positional arguments
  - Wrapped all file operations with `with` statements
  - Added proper encoding specification for text file operations
  - Fixed inconsistent return statements
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 4. Reduced Test Data Volume
- **Change**: Reduced `NUMBER_OF_SUBMISSIONS` from 10000 to 100 to speed up test execution
- **Files**: `scripts/seed.py`

### 5. Updated Node.js Version
- **Issue**: npm packages were showing `EBADENGINE` warnings because they require Node 20+ but the Dockerfile was using Node 18
- **Fix**: 
  - Updated Node.js from 18.19.0 to 20.18.0 in both dev and production Dockerfiles
  - Updated `package.json` engines requirement to Node >=20.0.0 and npm >=10.0.0
- **Files**: 
  - `docker/dev/nodejs/Dockerfile`
  - `docker/prod/nodejs/Dockerfile`
  - `package.json`

### 6. Fixed Chrome/Karma Hanging Issue
- **Issue**: Karma tests were hanging after completion, preventing the build from finishing
- **Fix**: 
  - Removed `--remote-debugging-port=9222` flag which was keeping Chrome open
  - Added Chrome flags to prevent background processes from keeping it alive
  - Reduced timeout values to make Chrome exit faster
  - Added cleanup function in entrypoint script to kill Chrome processes on exit
- **Files**: 
  - `karma.conf.js`
  - `docker/dev/nodejs/nodejs-entrypoint.sh`

### 7. Fixed Docker Build Hanging Issue
- **Issue**: `docker-compose run` commands were getting stuck because the entrypoint always started the dev server, even when a command was provided
- **Fix**: Updated entrypoint script to check if arguments are provided and execute them directly instead of always starting the dev server
- **Files**: `docker/dev/nodejs/nodejs-entrypoint.sh`

### 8. Improved Docker Build Resilience
- **Issue**: ARM64 Docker builds were failing due to network timeouts when installing Chromium
- **Fix**: Added retry logic (3 attempts with 10-second delays) for Chromium installation
- **Files**: `docker/dev/nodejs/Dockerfile`

## Testing

### Before
- ❌ Travis CI build failing with unique constraint violation
- ❌ Docker builds hanging and never completing
- ❌ Karma tests hanging after completion
- ❌ Multiple pylint errors

### After
- ✅ All tests passing
- ✅ Docker builds complete successfully
- ✅ Karma tests run and exit cleanly
- ✅ All pylint checks passing
- ✅ Frontend tests can be run with: `docker-compose run nodejs npm test`

## Impact

- **Build Time**: Reduced test execution time by reducing submission count from 10,000 to 100
- **Reliability**: Improved Docker build reliability with retry logic for network issues
- **Code Quality**: All pylint violations resolved, code is more maintainable
- **Compatibility**: Updated to Node.js 20 to meet package requirements

## Related Issues
Fixes Travis CI build failures and Docker build hanging issues.

## Checklist
- [x] All tests pass
- [x] Code follows project style guidelines
- [x] Pylint checks pass
- [x] Docker builds complete successfully
- [x] Frontend tests can be executed
- [x] No breaking changes introduced

title: feat: add DiagnosticConfigManifest and install ingestion dependencies…
body:
… (#1)

* feat: add DiagnosticConfigManifest and install ingestion dependencies

* Added configuration struct `DiagnosticConfigManifest` using Pydantic BaseSettings to handle direct zip fallback and CMS endpoint base urls.
* Added `dlt`, `requests`, `beautifulsoup4`, `psycopg2-binary`, `dbt-postgres` and related `pydantic-settings` to `pyproject.toml`.
* Fully tested the new config component (100% test coverage).
* Fixed all pre-commit hooks, added `pydantic-settings` to mypy's additional dependencies, and correctly declared runtime variables to suppress deptry errors.

* fix: constraint dbt-postgres dependencies for py3.14

* `dbt-extractor=0.6.0` fails to build from source on python 3.14 and `astral-sh/uv` fails when `[project]` is missing `version`.
* Switched to `dbt-extractor==0.5.1` and `dbt-postgres==1.8.2` which have python 3.14 compatible builds.
* Ensured `psycopg2-binary` avoids local compilation with `pg_config`.

* fix: temporarily remove dbt dependencies for python 3.14 CI checks

* Removed `dbt-postgres` and `dbt-extractor` due to upstream incompatibilities with Python 3.14 that prevent installation from succeeding (`dbt-extractor` requires a build from source but its `pyproject.toml` is invalid for modern build systems like `uv`).
* These tools can be added later once Python 3.14 wheels are shipped, or when the dbt portion is implemented via subprocesses / specialized docker environments.
* Preserved `psycopg2-binary`, `dlt`, and configuration manifest.

* fix: remove dlt and psycopg2 for py3.14t build failures

* `dlt` depends on `orjson` which explicitly fails compilation on python 3.14t (freethreading) because it doesn't support the free-threaded GIL architecture.
* `psycopg2-binary` lacks wheels for python 3.14 on windows/macos and tries to compile from source but missing `pg_config`.
* Removed these upstream dev dependencies from `pyproject.toml` so CI passes. We will install the downstream data packages in a different way or in the later units once Python 3.14 is stabilized.

title: fix(render): use preDeployCommand for Alembic migrations; expand deploy docs
body:
Migrations were chained into `startCommand`, meaning schema changes ran concurrently with traffic switchover. Moves them to `preDeployCommand` so Alembic runs before the new instance goes live.

## `render.yaml`
- Extract `alembic upgrade head` from `startCommand` → dedicated `preDeployCommand`
- `startCommand` is now purely `uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`

```yaml
preDeployCommand: alembic upgrade head
startCommand: uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
```

## `README.md` — Deploying to Render
- Replaces minimal 5-line section with a full Blueprint Deploy walkthrough (7 steps covering build → migrate → start)
- Adds env-var table with Stripe key sources, exact webhook URL pattern (`/api/payments/webhook`), and a note that `DATABASE_URL` is wired automatically by Blueprint (do not set manually)
- Adds Manual Deploy path showing the Pre-Deploy Command field
- Notes Stripe is optional — credits and crypto-wallet flows work without it

title: Default self-driving to BeamNG.tech via single Python script
body:
The project defaulted to Assetto Corsa; BeamNG.tech support existed only in a multi-module sub-project (`autonomy_project/`). This consolidates the BeamNG.tech driver into a single runnable script and makes it the default entry point.

## Changes

### `beamng_driver.py` (new)
Single-file BeamNG.tech autonomous driver — no separate config, no sub-modules:
- Top-level constants for all tunables (BeamNG host/port, map, vehicle, PID gains, perception thresholds)
- `SteeringPID` / `SpeedPID` — PID controllers with deadband, rate-limiting, and coast band
- `detect_lanes()` — classical OpenCV pipeline (HSV road mask → Canny → Hough → normalised lane offset)
- `obstacle_ahead()` — depth-image forward-strip check
- `Mode` state machine (`DRIVE / EMERGENCY / STOPPED`) with recovery path back to `DRIVE` when conditions clear
- `main()` — full BeamNG.tech loop: connect → spawn vehicle → attach sensors → perception→control at 20 Hz

```powershell
python beamng_driver.py                            # default (west_coast_usa, etk800, 40 kph)
python beamng_driver.py --speed 60 --no-overlay
python beamng_driver.py --beamng-home "C:\BeamNG.tech"
```

### `main.py` (updated)
Now routes to BeamNG.tech by default; Assetto Corsa preserved behind `--ac`:

```powershell
python main.py        # BeamNG.tech
python main.py --ac   # legacy Assetto Corsa (classical or neural)
```

### `requirements.txt`
Added `beamngpy>=1.28` as the primary dependency alongside existing `opencv-python` / `numpy`.

### `README.md`
BeamNG.tech quick-start promoted to the top; Assetto Corsa instructions moved to a legacy section.

title: Fix Python 3.9+ compatibility by replacing pkg_resources with pathlib
body:
Replace deprecated pkg_resources.resource_filename() with pathlib.Path
for loading patient parameter CSV file. This removes the setuptools
dependency and improves compatibility with modern Python environments.

Change: PATIENT_PARA_FILE now uses Path(__file__).parent.parent
instead of pkg_resources.resource_filename().

title: remove Python2 support
Add datasets, capstone projects, and docx generator for OI textbook
body:
Continuing textbook content creation (Steps 15–17): datasets documentation, five capstone projects, and a Markdown-to-Word compiler.

### Datasets
- `datasets/README.md` — Overview of five dataset categories (MEA electrophysiology, organoid imaging, simulation outputs, genomic/transcriptomic, benchmarks) with Python loading snippets and licensing
- `datasets/datasets_index.md` — 15 indexed datasets (DS-001–DS-015) with format, size, license, citation, related chapters, and access DOIs

### Capstone Projects
- **Capstone 1** — Hodgkin-Huxley neuron simulation: HH equation implementation, f-I curves, 10-neuron coupled network, cross-correlation analysis
- **Capstone 2** — Organoid fabrication plan: iPSC differentiation protocol, maturation timeline (Week 0–20), vascularization strategy, QC checkpoints, cost estimation
- **Capstone 3** — MEA signal processing pipeline: bandpass filtering, spike detection/sorting (PCA + k-means), burst detection, raster plots
- **Capstone 4** — Reservoir computing: ESN implementation, NARMA-10 benchmark, spectral radius sweep, biological reservoir with Dale's law and small-world topology
- **Capstone 5** — Integrated OI system design (final project): 5 application domains, 6-phase timeline, simulation component, ethics review, 200-point rubric

### Document Generator
- `generate_docx.py` — Compiles 24 chapters + 5 appendices into `.docx` using `python-docx`. Handles heading hierarchy, inline formatting, code blocks, tables, LaTeX→Unicode conversion, title page, and TOC placeholder.

```python
# Usage
pip install python-docx
python generate_docx.py
# → organoid_intelligence_textbook.docx
```

title: Add REFIT dataset integration and end-to-end NILM pipeline
body:
The project had no mechanism to ingest REFIT data and no runnable pipeline to disaggregate individual appliance states (kettle, microwave, fridge, TV) from household power consumption.

## New: `Processed_Data_CSV/` — dataset drop location
- `README.md` with download instructions and expected file layout (`CLEAN_House3.csv` → `House_3.csv`)

## New: `Projet_NILM/` — complete pipeline

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `refit_metadata.py` | `Appliance1`–`Appliance9` → device name for all 20 REFIT houses; helpers `get_appliance_column()`, `parse_house_number()` |
| `preprocessing.py` | `load_refit_csv()`, `hampel_filter()`, `interpolate_missing()`, `preprocess_house()` — full preprocessing chain |
| `train_hmm.py` | Fits per-appliance `GaussianHMM` from sub-metering columns; saves JSON models to `models/<house>/` |
| `disaggregate.py` | **Sub-metering mode**: Viterbi per channel. **NILM mode** (`--nilm`): combinatorial nearest-match on aggregate. Emits plots. |
| `run_nilm.py` | Entry point — trains → disaggregates → prints state-occupancy table |

```bash
pip install -r Projet_NILM/requirements.txt
# drop House_3.csv in Processed_Data_CSV/
python Projet_NILM/run_nilm.py --house Processed_Data_CSV/House_3.csv
# → state table: kettle OFF/ON, microwave OFF/ON, fridge OFF/LOW/HIGH, tv OFF/ON
```

## Fixed: existing files (pandas 2.x compatibility)
- `Appliance.py` — `iteritems()` → `items()`; removed stray top-level `data = df.iloc[...]`; added missing imports
- `hampel_filter.py` / `interpolation.py` — refactored from raw notebook scripts into importable modules with `--csv` CLI entry points; fixed `ChainedAssignment` in interpolation

## Added: `.gitignore`
Excludes `models/`, `plots/`, `*.png`, and all REFIT CSV files (large binary, user-provided).

title: feat: add inter-tier contract schemas (#421)
body:
## Summary

Implements #421 — Inter-tier contracts for structured T1↔T2↔T3 communication.

## Problem

`WorkItem.input.prompt` was a free-form string with no schema validation or structured error reporting between tiers.

## Changes

### New: `src/autopoiesis/contracts/` package

**`schemas.py`** — Pydantic v2 models:
- `BudgetConstraint` — resource limits (tokens, tool calls, wall time)
- `UserIntent` — T1→T2 structured input (raw_input, effort_level, classified_topic, constraints)
- `ExecutionPlan` — T2→T3 task plan (task, context, tools_allowed, budget, success_criteria)
- `ExecutionResult` — T3→T2 outcome (status, output, artifacts, error, token_usage)

**`validation.py`** — contract validation helpers:
- `validate_work_item_input(work_item)` — parses JSON contract from prompt; returns `None` for free-form strings
- `validate_execution_result(result, contract)` — checks output satisfies success criteria

### Backward-compatible model extensions

- `WorkItem.contract: dict | None = None` — optional attached contract
- `WorkItemOutput.structured_result: dict | None = None` — optional structured result

Free-form string prompts continue to work unchanged.

### Tests

`tests/test_contracts.py` — 33 tests covering:
- Schema validation (all models)
- Serialization/deserialization round-trips
- Backward compatibility (free-form strings return `None`)
- Contract parsing from JSON prompts
- Success criteria validation

## CI

All checks pass locally:
- `ruff check` ✅
- `ruff format --check` ✅
- `pyright` ✅ (0 errors)
- `bandit` ✅ (0 issues)
- `arch_check` ✅
- `pytest` ✅ (33 passed)

title: feat: inter-tier Pydantic contracts (#421)
body:
## Summary
Typed Pydantic schemas for structured T1↔T2↔T3 communication, replacing free-form strings.

### Contracts (`src/autopoiesis/contracts/`)
- `UserIntent` — T1→T2 (raw_input, classified_topic, effort_level, constraints)
- `ExecutionPlan` — T2→T3 (task, context, tools_allowed, budget, success_criteria, output_schema)
- `ExecutionResult` — T3→T2 (status, output, artifacts, trace_id, tokens_used, error)
- `BudgetConstraint` — max_tokens, max_iterations, timeout_seconds
- `validate_contract()` with configurable retries and structured errors

### Integration
- `WorkItemInput` auto-wraps raw strings into UserIntent (backward compatible)
- `WorkItemOutput.structured_result` now typed as ExecutionResult
- Delegation helpers build ExecutionPlan for T2→T3
- WorkItem.contract auto-attaches from input

### Tests
- Contract schema validation, round-trip serialization
- validate_contract() success/failure/retry behavior
- WorkItem integration (raw string wrapping, JSON promotion)
- Updated delegation + topic tool tests

Closes #421

title: add pipeline CI
body:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a GitHub Actions CI pipeline and the first unit test suite for the `core.parser` module, alongside a refactoring that extracts shared models and parsing helpers into a dedicated `core/` package. **Key changes:** - New `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with `code-quality` (ruff + bandit) and `business-logic-tests` (pytest + coverage) jobs, triggered on both `push` and `pull_request` to `dev`/`main`. - Four new test files covering `parse_user_story`, `parse_backlog_file`, and internal helpers — all with correct assertions. - `core/models.py` and `core/parser.py` extracted as a shared package consumed by `main.py`, `pipeline/agents.py`, and both integrations. - `pipeline/__init__.py` and `core/__init__.py` added as package entrypoints. **Issues found:** - No `conftest.py` or `pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` section configures `pythonpath = ["."]`. CI works because `PYTHONPATH` is set in the workflow, but local `pytest` runs will fail with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'core'` without manually exporting the variable. - `pytest --cov=.` instruments all Python files in the workspace root, including heavyweight pipeline and integration modules that are not exercised by the parser tests — consider scoping coverage to `--cov=core` etc. - `tests/test_parser_helpers.py` imports private underscore-prefixed functions (`_extract`, `_slugify`) directly, coupling tests to internal implementation details.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Mergeable with minor follow-up — test logic is sound but local developer experience and CI coverage scoping need attention. - The new tests are logically correct and the parser refactoring is clean. However, the absence of a `pythonpath` pytest configuration means local development is broken out of the box, the coverage command is noisier than necessary, and several CI issues flagged in earlier review rounds (redundant pip cache, ruff linting notebooks) remain unaddressed. - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` still has a redundant `actions/cache@v4` step in `business-logic-tests` and `ruff check .` without notebook exclusions. `tests/test_parser_basic.py` (and all test files) need a `pythonpath` configuration to work locally.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | .github/workflows/ci.yml | New CI workflow with two jobs: code-quality (ruff + bandit) and business-logic-tests (pytest). Now correctly includes push triggers and excludes notebooks from bandit. However, the business-logic-tests job retains a redundant `actions/cache@v4` step alongside setup-python's built-in cache, `ruff check .` lacks notebook exclusions, and `--cov=.` instruments all source files unnecessarily. | | tests/test_parser_basic.py | New test covering core parser field parsing (ID, type, title, persona, story points, domain). Logic is correct. Missing local pytest pythonpath configuration means tests fail locally without manually setting PYTHONPATH. | | tests/test_parser_helpers.py | New tests for private helpers `_slugify` and `_extract`. Tests are correct but import private underscore-prefixed functions directly, coupling tests to implementation internals. | | main.py | CLI entrypoint refactored into discrete subcommands. The `--model` argument is declared in the pipeline subparser but never forwarded to `run_pipeline`, silently ignoring user input (pre-existing, flagged in previous thread). The `full` command also hardcodes `max_revisions=2` with no user override. | | core/parser.py | Backlog parsing helpers extracted into a dedicated module. Logic is sound: ID preference by work-item-type prefix, Fibonacci story-point validation, section extraction via DOTALL regex, and technical-notes marker filtering all behave as expected by the new tests. | | pipeline/agents.py | Agent node functions for the LangGraph pipeline. Model names are module-level constants (not wired to the CLI's `--model` flag). Classification, synthesis, extraction, generation, validation, and revision flows all look logically correct. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Dev as Developer (push/PR) participant GHA as GitHub Actions participant CQ as code-quality job participant BLT as business-logic-tests job Dev->>GHA: push / pull_request (dev or main) GHA->>CQ: trigger code-quality CQ->>CQ: checkout + setup-python (cached pip) CQ->>CQ: pip install ruff bandit CQ->>CQ: ruff check . (no notebook exclusion ⚠️) CQ->>CQ: ruff format --check . CQ->>CQ: bandit -r . -ll --exclude .git,notebooks,data CQ->>GHA: write step summary GHA->>BLT: trigger (needs: code-quality) BLT->>BLT: checkout + setup-python (cached pip) BLT->>BLT: actions/cache@v4 (redundant ⚠️) BLT->>BLT: pip install -r requirements.txt BLT->>BLT: pip install pytest pytest-cov BLT->>BLT: pytest --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing BLT->>GHA: write test summary ```

Comments Outside Diff (6)

 1. `integrations/github.py`, line 402-412 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/dda42a89f89d9a14ecd647075d888685491ba2ec/integrations/github.py#L402-L412)) **`find_existing_issue` only inspects the first 100 open issues** The endpoint is called with `per_page=100` but without any pagination loop. If the target repository has more than 100 open issues, the de-duplication check will silently miss issues that fall outside the first page, causing `create_issue` to create a duplicate. This is especially problematic because there is no warning logged when the limit is hit. Either paginate through all pages until exhausted, or use GitHub's issue search endpoint (which accepts a `q=` title filter) to perform an exact lookup in a single round-trip: ```python def find_existing_issue(self, title: str) -> int | None: page = 1 while True: response = self.session.get( self._url("issues"), params={"state": "open", "per_page": 100, "page": page}, ) if response.status_code != 200: return None issues = response.json() if not issues: return None for issue in issues: if issue.get("title") == title: return issue["number"] page += 1 ``` 2. `pipeline/agents.py`, line 403-407 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/dda42a89f89d9a14ecd647075d888685491ba2ec/pipeline/agents.py#L403-L407)) **Hard `ValueError` when classification needs human validation** When `classify_input` determines that a human must review the classification (`classification_needs_human_validation = True`), it sets `confirmed_work_item_type` to `None`. The graph in `pipeline/graph.py` has no conditional edge between the classifier and the rest of the pipeline, so `generate_stories` is always invoked. When it is, this guard raises a `ValueError` that bubbles unhandled all the way up through `run_pipeline → app.invoke` and ultimately through `main.py`'s bare `except Exception` handler, which prints an unhelpful message and exits with code 1. Users have no way to know that the input was ambiguous or that the pipeline stopped early waiting for human input. Consider either: 1. Adding a conditional edge in `graph.py` that routes ambiguous classifications to an `END` node with a clear state flag, or 2. Returning a partial/empty result from `generate_stories` with the `classification_needs_human_validation` flag preserved so callers can inspect it. As-is, this edge case is completely invisible to the end user. 3. `pipeline/agents.py`, line 483-485 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/34509226b146b24357073c7fdb4456372eeaaf90/pipeline/agents.py#L483-L485)) **Validator fail-open silently bypasses revision loop** When `json.JSONDecodeError` is raised (i.e., the LLM validator returned malformed output), `story.valid` is forced to `True`. Because `revise_stories` only processes stories where `story.valid is False`, a story that was never actually validated will skip all revision rounds entirely and flow straight through to the final output. A JSON parse error means the validation outcome is *unknown* — not that the story is valid. The safer default is to keep it as invalid (which it already is, since `GeneratedStory.valid` defaults to `False`) so the revisor has a chance to improve it. Consider also adding a diagnostic feedback string so downstream logging captures what happened: 4. `main.py`, line 181-195 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/34509226b146b24357073c7fdb4456372eeaaf90/main.py#L181-L195)) **`full` command hardcodes `max_revisions=2` — not user-controllable** `_command_full` constructs the inner `Namespace` for `_command_pipeline` with a hardcoded `max_revisions=2`. The `full` subparser (lines 94–116) never defines a `--max-revisions` argument, so there is no way for a user invoking `python main.py full ...` to change the number of revision rounds. This is inconsistent with the `pipeline` subcommand, which does expose `--max-revisions`. Consider adding `--max-revisions` to the `full` subparser and threading it through: ```python full_parser.add_argument( "--max-revisions", type=int, default=2, help="Max revision rounds" ) ``` Then in `_command_full`: ```python max_revisions=args.max_revisions, ``` 5. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, line 123-127 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/f9a4b46dd58050c6f2d6d068539d0b694e86f90b/.github/workflows/ci.yml#L123-L127)) **`--cov=.` includes test files and unrelated modules** `pytest --cov=.` instruments every Python file discovered under the working directory, including the test files themselves and heavyweight modules like `pipeline/agents.py`, `integrations/github.py`, and `integrations/jira.py`. These files import `langchain_mistralai`, `requests`, etc. at module level. While those packages should be installed via `requirements.txt`, the coverage report will be polluted with lines from files that are never exercised by the parser tests. Limiting coverage to just the source packages avoids noise and gives a more meaningful report: 6. `tests/test_parser_basic.py`, line 1 ([link](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/blob/f9a4b46dd58050c6f2d6d068539d0b694e86f90b/tests/test_parser_basic.py#L1)) **No `conftest.py` or pytest `pythonpath` configuration for local development** The CI workflow sets `PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}` before running pytest, which is why `from core.parser import ...` resolves correctly in CI. However, there is no `conftest.py`, `pytest.ini`, or `pyproject.toml` section that configures `pythonpath` for local development. Any developer who clones the repo and runs `pytest` without manually exporting `PYTHONPATH=.` will immediately get: ``` ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'core' ``` The simplest fix is to add a minimal `pyproject.toml`: ```toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["."] ``` Or a root-level `conftest.py` with a `sys.path` insert. This would also make the CI step self-documenting rather than relying on an environment variable that is easy to forget. This same issue affects `tests/test_parser_backlog_file.py`, `tests/test_parser_helpers.py`, and `tests/test_parser_user_story.py`.
Last reviewed commit: ["feat: Add bandit sec..."](https://github.com/erwannlesech/ai-agentic-sdlc-solution/commit/f9a4b46dd58050c6f2d6d068539d0b694e86f90b)

title: F registry
Bump markdown from 3.6 to 3.8.1
body:
Bumps [markdown](https://github.com/Python-Markdown/markdown) from 3.6 to 3.8.1.
Release notes

Sourced from markdown's releases.

Release 3.8.1

Fixed

  • Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser (#1534).
  • Fixed dropped content in md_in_html (#1526).
  • Fixed HTML handling corner case that prevented some content from not being rendered (#1528).

Release 3.8

Changed

  • DRY fix in abbr extension by introducing method create_element (#1483).
  • Clean up test directory by removing some redundant tests and port non-redundant cases to the newer test framework.
  • Improved performance of the raw HTML post-processor (#1510).

Fixed

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

Release 3.7

Changed

Refactor abbr Extension

A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

A call to Markdown.reset() now clears all previously defined abbreviations.

Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying abbr tags to text without a title value.

Added an optional glossary configuration option to the abbreviations extension.

... (truncated)

title: Add QGPS session endpoints, Dockerfile, and plug-and-play demo script
body:
The backend lacked session management endpoints required by the `run_shelf_scout_qgps.sh` demo workflow, and there was no Dockerfile for containerised deployment.

## Changes

### `app.py` — session management
- `POST /scan/session/start` — accepts `gps`, `qgps`, `orientation`, `device_id`; returns a UUID session token
- `GET /scan/session/<session_id>/export` — returns full session record (metadata + frames); 404 on unknown ID
- In-memory `_sessions` dict (documented as non-persistent; swap for Redis/DB for production)

### `Dockerfile`
- `python:3.11-slim` base, installs `requirements.txt`, runs via `gunicorn -w 4`
- Build context is repo root, so `app.py` and `requirements.txt` are copied directly

### `run_shelf_scout_qgps.sh`
- End-to-end demo: builds Docker image → starts container → creates QGPS-tagged session → launches Expo mobile client
- `BACKEND_DIR="."` points to repo root; all vars (`GEMMA_MODEL`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `BACKEND_PORT`) are overridable via env

### `tests.py`
- 5 new tests covering: session creation (full payload + empty body), export, 404 on unknown ID, QGPS field round-trip

```bash
# Quick demo usage
GOOGLE_API_KEY=<key> bash run_shelf_scout_qgps.sh

# Or with custom model/port
GEMMA_MODEL=gemma-4-31b-it BACKEND_PORT=8080 GOOGLE_API_KEY=<key> bash run_shelf_scout_qgps.sh
```

## Summary by Sourcery

Add backend support for scan sessions with QGPS context and provide a containerized, plug-and-play demo workflow.

New Features:
- Introduce in-memory session management endpoints to create and export scan sessions with GPS/QGPS and device metadata.
- Add a Dockerfile to run the Flask backend in a containerized Gunicorn-based service.
- Add a plug-and-play shell script to build and run the backend in Docker and launch the mobile Expo client wired to an auto-created session.

Tests:
- Add tests covering session creation, empty-payload handling, session export including metadata and frames, 404 behavior for unknown sessions, and QGPS field persistence.

title: v0.2.0: security fixes, deprecation cleanup, contact links, CI/CD upgrades, changelog
body:
## Summary

Comprehensive audit, review, and enhancement of the PyBrain & PyHeart packages culminating in a **v0.2.0** release-ready branch.

---

## Security Fixes (Critical)

- **Real AES-256-GCM encryption**: `EncryptionManager.encrypt_data()` previously used SHA-256 (a one-way hash, not encryption) — replaced with proper AES-256-GCM via the `cryptography` library. `decrypt_data()` now correctly reverses the ciphertext. AAD handling is consistent between encrypt/decrypt (always `b""` for no-context).
- **`EncryptionManager` key management**: Now accepts a caller-supplied 32-byte `key` parameter. Falls back to the `PYHEART_ENCRYPTION_KEY` environment variable (hex-encoded). Warns when using an ephemeral random key (single-process only). This ensures ciphertexts are decryptable across process restarts and multiple instances.
- **`decrypt_data()` input validation**: Added explicit validation for missing `encrypted_data` key, invalid hex, payloads shorter than 13 bytes, algorithm tag mismatch, and tampered ciphertext — each raising a clear, typed exception.
- **Async task bug — `FHIRClient.close()`**: Was calling `asyncio.create_task()` outside an async context (a `RuntimeError`). Fixed to use `asyncio.get_running_loop()` when inside a running loop and `asyncio.run()` as a synchronous fallback.
- **Async task bug — `PluginRegistry.unregister_plugin()`**: Same pattern applied — `asyncio.get_running_loop()` with `asyncio.run()` fallback, ensuring cleanup always executes and resources are not leaked. `asyncio` is now imported at module level.

## Deprecation Fixes

- `datetime.utcnow()` (deprecated in Python 3.12) replaced with `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` across:
  - `pyheart/core/security.py`, `pyheart/core/client.py`, `pyheart/core/workflow.py`
  - `pybrain/core/harmonizer.py`, `pybrain/core/analytics.py`
- `WorkflowInstance` Pydantic field defaults updated to timezone-aware lambdas.

## Code Quality

- Bare `except:` → `except Exception:` in `harmonizer.py` and `analytics.py`
- F-string `logger.error(f"…")` → parameterized `logger.error("…", arg)` in `harmonizer.py`
- `import logging` moved to top of `pybrain/__init__.py` (was at bottom, after exports)

## Contact Reference Links Fixed

All broken/wrong URLs updated to point at the canonical repository (`Fadil369/pybrain-pyheart`) across every file:
- `github.com/brainsait/pybrain` → `github.com/Fadil369/pybrain-pyheart`
- `github.com/brainsait/pyheart` → `github.com/Fadil369/pybrain-pyheart`
- `github.com/brainsait` (org link) → `github.com/Fadil369`
- `pybrain.readthedocs.io` / `pyheart.readthedocs.io` → GitHub tree URLs
- `brainsait.com/resources` → GitHub wiki
- `community.brainsait.com` → GitHub Discussions
- Docker image `brainsait/pyheart:latest` → `ghcr.io/fadil369/pybrain-pyheart/pyheart:latest`

Files updated: `pybrain-package.py`, `pyheart-package.py`, `pybrain-pkg/README.md`, `pyheart-pkg/README.md`, `unified-healthcare-example.py`, `examples/plugins/README.md`, `PLUGINS.md`, `QUICKSTART_PLUGINS.md`

## CI/CD Upgrades (`release.yml`)

- `actions/setup-python` `@v4` → `@v5`
- `actions/upload-artifact` / `download-artifact` `@v3` → `@v4`
- `softprops/action-gh-release` `@v1` → `@v2`
- Fixed pip install command in release body: `pybrain` / `pyheart` → `brainsait-pybrain` / `brainsait-pyheart`
- Removed hardcoded `password: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}` from publish steps (uses OIDC trusted publisher)
- Test matrix restored to Python 3.8–3.12 to stay consistent with `requires-python = ">=3.8"` and existing classifiers

## Package Metadata (`pyproject.toml` — both packages)

- Correct GitHub URLs (was pointing at non-existent `brainsait/` org)
- Removed unused `setuptools-scm` from `[build-system]`
- Added Python 3.12 classifier; fixed malformed classifier line (missing newline)
- **pybrain**: Updated `langchain>=0.0.200` → `>=0.2.0` (0.0.x is years out of date)

## Tests Added (`pyheart-pkg/tests/test_security.py`)

21 new tests covering:
- **`EncryptionManager`**: roundtrip with/without context, empty string, random nonce uniqueness, caller-supplied key, `PYHEART_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var, wrong key length, wrong env var length, all `decrypt_data()` error paths (missing key, bad hex, short payload, algorithm mismatch, tampered ciphertext), PII field encryption
- **`AuthProvider`**: valid credentials, missing fields, token validation, unknown token
- **`TestFHIRClientClose`**: verifies `aclose()` is invoked from a synchronous context
- **`TestPluginRegistryUnregister`**: verifies `cleanup()` runs in both sync and async contexts

Full test suite: **52 tests, 0 failures**

## Version & Changelog

- Bumped both packages `0.1.0` → `0.2.0` in `pyproject.toml` and `__init__.py`
- Added full `[0.2.0]` entry to `CHANGELOG.md` documenting all changes above

title: Implement Stories 1.2, 1.4, 1.5, 2.2, 5.1, 7.2 — SCL parser, COMTRADE parser, GOOSE capture/monitor tools, tutorial, test data
body:
Implements 6 of the remaining user stories from the grid-ops PDF across Epics 1, 2, 5, and 7. All new modules are pure-Python (stdlib only), tested, and lint-clean. Test count grows from 51 → 111.

## Story 1.2 — SCL File Parser (`iec61850/scl/`)
- Parses IEC 61850-6 XML (ICD/CID/SCD) via stdlib `xml.etree`
- Resolves `DataTypeTemplates` references to populate full `IED → LD → LN → DO → DA` hierarchy
- `scl_to_json()` serialisation; registered as `scl-parse` CLI entry point

```bash
python -m iec61850.scl.cli test-data/scl/busbar_protection.scd --output json
```

## Story 1.4 — GOOSE Tutorial (`docs/tutorial_goose.md`)
- Covers frame structure, APPID, stNum/sqNum semantics, encode → publish → subscribe flow
- All code blocks runnable without root (mock-patched socket)

## Story 1.5 — GOOSE Capture Tool (`tools/goose_capture.py`)
- Raw AF_PACKET capture with optional APPID filter and JSON-line log output

## Story 2.2 — COMTRADE Parser (`comtrade/`)
- Handles revisions 1991/1999/2013, ASCII and BINARY `.dat` formats
- Optional Parquet export (`pyarrow`); registered as `comtrade-parse` CLI entry point

```bash
python -m comtrade.cli test-data/comtrade/phase_a_ground.cfg --output json
```

## Story 5.1 — OT GOOSE Anomaly Monitor (`tools/goose_monitor.py`)
- Passive capture with configurable baseline learning window
- Alerts on: unexpected APPID, MAC spoofing, StNum rollback (replay attack), excessive GoCBRef diversity
- JSON-line alert log output

## Story 7.2 — Test Data Library (`test-data/`)
- 3 SCL files: feeder bay (ICD), transformer bay (ICD), busbar protection (SCD)
- 5 COMTRADE records: no-fault, phase-A-ground, A-B phase-to-phase, three-phase, close-in with DC offset
- PCAP catalogue documented in `test-data/README.md`

## Summary by Sourcery

Add SCL and COMTRADE parsing modules, GOOSE capture and anomaly monitoring tools, a GOOSE tutorial, and shared test data, and wire them into the CLI, packaging, and documentation.

New Features:
- Introduce IEC 61850 SCL parser module with CLI for summarising and exporting SCL models to JSON.
- Add IEEE C37.111 COMTRADE parser module with CLI supporting summary, JSON, and optional Parquet export.
- Provide live GOOSE capture CLI tool with optional APPID filtering and JSON logging.
- Provide OT GOOSE anomaly monitor CLI tool with baseline learning and multiple detection rules.
- Add a step-by-step GOOSE tutorial document for developers.
- Add a structured test data library containing sample SCL, COMTRADE, and PCAP datasets.

Enhancements:
- Expand README with feature table, usage examples for new CLIs, and project structure updates.
- Expose new packages and tools in packaging configuration and bump project version to 0.2.0.

Build:
- Extend package discovery configuration to include comtrade and tools packages.

Documentation:
- Document the GOOSE tutorial and test data catalogue in the main README and dedicated markdown files.

Tests:
- Add unit test suites for the SCL parser, COMTRADE parser, and GOOSE anomaly monitor using the new test data.

title: feat: structured error reporting with typed errors and partial results
body:
## Summary

- Replace untyped `list[Any]` in `ExtractionError.errors` with discriminated union of `TransformError` and `ModelValidationError`
- Both error types carry source location (`BlockLocation` or `RowLocation`) with line numbers, indices, and headings
- `ExtractionError.__str__()` produces numbered, human-readable error summaries with field names and locations
- Add `partial=True` parameter to `parse_tables()` and `parse()` returning `PartialResult` with both valid data and errors
- Export `PartialResult` and `FieldError` from the package

Closes #8

title: Fix pandas 2.0 incompatibility and parameterize load_biome_herarchy_dict
body:
This PR fixes two issues in utils.py.

1. Fix pandas 2.0 incompatibility

Problem:
`Series.iteritems() `was removed in pandas 2.0, causing an AttributeError.

Fix:
Replaced `Series.iteritems()` with `Series.items()` in `split_tt()`.

Additional improvement:
Replaced `type(lin) == str` with `isinstance(lin, str)` in `split_tt() `and `i_T()`.


2. Parameterize `load_biome_herarchy_dict()
`
Problem:
`load_biome_herarchy_dict()` always resolved the biome hierarchy file through
TaxonomyToVectorParams even when called from other components.

Because the function used an LRU cache, the resolved path was shared across
callers regardless of the pipeline.

Fix:
Added optional parameters:

`model_name`
`model_version`

These allow callers to explicitly specify the model repository while keeping
backward-compatible defaults.

resolved issue: #7 #9

title: Fix dataclass mutable default fields for Python 3.12 compatibility
Updated script to use pandas.dataframe.items insteads of pandas.dataframe.iteritems
body: pandas.dataframe.items is also found in the pandas version used in prod (1.02)

title: Enhance logging, API clarity, and dependency management
body:
### Description

This pull request introduces several improvements and changes:

- **Logging and Debugging:**
  - Added logging for token handling and job execution steps to enhance debugging capabilities.
  - Improved logging across various modules and introduced detailed logging in Google Calendar batch processing.

- **API Improvements:**
  - Updated HTTP methods for API routes to enhance clarity and security.
  - Added Swagger for comprehensive API documentation.

- **Code Refactoring:**
  - Refactored code to improve typing annotations and constant organization.
  - Moved Google OAuth login logic to `/login` route for enhanced clarity and maintainability.

- **Dependency Management:**
  - Switched to Poetry for dependency management and replaced `requirements.txt` with `uv.lock`.
  - Updated dependencies and IDE configurations accordingly.

- **Scheduling Enhancements:**
  - Refactored `scheduled_job` to use environment variables for the default group.
  - Updated block timings to a new schedule format.

- **Maintenance:**
  - Removed commented-out code and updated JDK configuration.

These changes collectively improve system maintainability, security, and documentation.

title: fix(lab-00): workflow correctness, shared EVE-NG helper, .unl shipping standard, docs
body:
## Summary

Four-commit PR hardening the switching lab-00 workflow end-to-end and
establishing repo-wide standards that future labs will inherit.

### 1. `6450c1a` fix(lab-00): workflow correctness, setup_lab robustness, VPC configs
- `workbook.md` Section 9: reorder workflow so `apply_solution.py` resets before inject; clarify `setup_lab.py` vs `apply_solution.py` roles
- `workbook.md` Ticket 1: drop misleading "and/or SW3" wording -- `inject_scenario_01.py` only modifies SW1
- `setup_lab.py`: drop broken `--ssh` flag; use `save_config()` (handles "Destination filename?" prompt); fail fast on unset `--host`
- Add missing `initial-configs/PC{1,2}.vpc` (required by EVE-NG skill §4)
- `requirements.txt`: add `requests` (needed by the new REST discovery helper)

### 2. `d2fb8d0` refactor(lab-00): shared EVE-NG helper with REST port discovery
- New `labs/common/tools/eve_ng.py`: `require_host()`, `discover_ports()` (EVE-NG REST), `connect_node()`, typed `EveNgError`
- Rewrite `setup_lab.py`, `apply_solution.py`, `inject_scenario_0{1,2,3}.py` to discover ports at runtime instead of hardcoding 32768-32771
- **Add pre-flight checks to inject scripts** -- verify target is in expected solution state before injecting; `--skip-preflight` escape hatch
- Meaningful exit codes: 2=missing host, 3=API error, 4=preflight fail

### 3. `8082647` feat(lab-00): `topology/` folder + .unl shipping standard
- Move `topology.drawio` into `topology/` subfolder
- New `topology/README.md` with import steps, export workflow, manual-rebuild fallback
- Bump `.agent/skills` submodule to pick up new Section 7 requiring `.unl` shipping (skills PR: FonNkwenti/cisco-lab-skills-eve-ng#1)
- Update `meta.yaml` files block: diagram/eve_ng_lab/readme

### 4. `aa618b4` docs: top-level and lab-level READMEs + gitignore local settings
- Expand root `README.md` with full VMware -> EVE-NG -> scripts -> workbook walkthrough
- New `labs/switching/lab-00-vlans-and-trunking/README.md` with Stage 0-5 flow
- `.gitignore` + untrack `.claude/settings.local.json`

## Known follow-ups (out of this PR)

- [ ] Export the actual `.unl` from a running EVE-NG instance and add to `topology/` (requires hands-on with the EVE-NG host)
- [ ] Apply the same refactor pattern to `ospf/lab-00` and subsequent labs
- [ ] Consider Python packaging (`pip install -e .`) once >5 labs share `labs/common/tools/`

## Test plan

- [ ] `python -m py_compile` on all 6 rewritten scripts (verified locally)
- [ ] With a running EVE-NG lab: `python setup_lab.py --host <ip>` pushes initial configs to all 4 nodes
- [ ] `apply_solution.py` -> `inject_scenario_01.py` -> verify PC1 cannot ping PC2 -> fix per workbook -> ping succeeds
- [ ] Repeat for scenarios 02 and 03
- [ ] Confirm inject scripts refuse to run without prior `apply_solution.py` (preflight)

title: fix(lab-01): apply lab-00 standards -- shared helper, preflight, topology/, READMEs
body:
## Summary

Applies the standards established by #2 (switching/lab-00) to
switching/lab-01-etherchannel. Same workflow-correctness fixes, same
shared EVE-NG helper usage, same `.unl` shipping standard.

## Per-file highlights

### Scripts now use `labs/common/tools/eve_ng.py`
- `setup_lab.py`, `apply_solution.py`, three inject scripts rewritten to:
  - discover console ports via REST API (no hardcoded 32768-32771)
  - fail fast if `--host` left as placeholder (exit 2)
  - return meaningful exit codes (3=API error, 4=preflight failed)
- **Pre-flight checks** on inject scripts verify target is in expected
  solution state before injecting -- refuses to layer fault on fault
- `apply_solution.py` converted from delta-only restore to full-config
  restore (consistent with lab-00; truly "reset to known-good")

| 03 | SW3 Gi0/1 | `channel-group 3 mode on` (static) | switch to LACP passive (Po3 cannot form) |

Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.
