title: chore: drop python2 from insights-client
body:
### All Pull Requests:

Check all that apply:

* [x] Have you followed the guidelines in our Contributing document, including the instructions about commit messages?
* [x] No Sensitive Data in this change?
* [ ] Is this PR to correct an issue?
* [ ] Is this PR an enhancement?
* [ ] Need backport to `3.0_egg`? Yes, refer to [RPM/Egg Delivery](https://github.com/RedHatInsights/insights-core/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#rpmegg-delivery) to open a new PR.
* [ ] Is this a backport from `master`? Yes, this is a backport of #PR-ID
<!--
Replace the "PR-ID", if this PR needs to be backported from the master branch.
-->

### Complete Description of Additions/Changes:

<!--
Provide complete details of the issue or enhancement. You may link to existing open publicly-accessible issues or enhancement requests that provide these details.

Please do not include links to any websites that are not publicly accessible. You may include non-link reference numbers to help you and your team identify non-public references.

This information is necessary before your PR can be reviewed.

You may remove this comment.
-->
* CardID: RHEL-141612

* Remove the six dependency from the insights-client part of insights-core.

###  Notes for reviewer

I was looking for occurrences of the following text: 
sys.version_info; py.*2(regex); py.*3(regex); six; PY; PY3; PY2; unicode; long; 2.x; 3.x


## Summary by Sourcery

Remove Python 2 compatibility code and the six dependency from the insights-client portion of insights-core, standardizing on Python 3 behavior.

Enhancements:
- Replace six.string_types and six.PY* conditionals with native Python 3 types and logic across client configuration, connection, and collection rules.
- Simplify HTTP request attachment handling and branch info serialization using standard Python 3 idioms.
- Remove legacy Python 2-specific logging handler configuration and file-write branches in the client code.

Tests:
- Update client utility and collection rules tests to patch builtins.open directly without six-based Python 2/3 branching.

## Summary by Sourcery

Drop remaining Python 2 compatibility code from the insights-client portion of insights-core and standardize behavior on Python 3.

Enhancements:
- Remove Python 2-only normalization and logging logic in the ansible playbook verifier and client configuration code.
- Replace uses of six (string type checks, iteritems, PY2/PY3 branches) with native Python 3 types and idioms in client configuration, collection rules, connection handling, and logging setup.
- Simplify request/response handling and branch info serialization to rely solely on Python 3-compatible JSON and file I/O operations.
- Clean up ansible playbook serialization to operate only on Python 3, including updated handling of numeric and string values.

Tests:
- Remove Python 2-specific tests and skips, and update tests to patch builtins.open directly without Python 2/3 branching in utilities and collection rules.
- Adjust playbook serializer and crypto tests to reflect the removal of Python 2-specific behavior and comments.

title: feat: add module-based piscine directory structure
body:
The repository lacked the module-organized structure matching the official 42 Python for Data Science piscine. Exercises existed only as a flat `exercises/` folder with no module separation.

## Structure added

Five module directories, each with zero-padded exercise subfolders (`ex00`–`exNN`):

```
python_0_starting/   ex00–ex09
python_1_array/      ex00–ex05
python_2_datatable/  ex00–ex04
python_3_oop/        ex00–ex04
python_4_dod/        ex00–ex02
```

## Starter files per exercise

Each exercise folder contains three files following the established repo conventions:

- **`main.py`** — module docstring, `main()` entry point, `if __name__ == "__main__":` guard
- **`README.md`** — Objective / Instructions / Requirements / Implementation Notes / Example sections
- **`notes.md`** — Key Observations / Difficulties / Ideas for Follow-up sections

## Root README

Updated to document the new module layout, explain what the piscine is, describe exercise organization, and update the `python` run path example to match the new structure.

title: Port Telos compiler from Python to C
body:
Full C implementation of the Telos compiler in `csrc/`, preserving all documented semantics: constraint-graph IR, polynomial closed-form detection, constant folding, x86-64 codegen, and C codegen.

### New files (`csrc/`)

- **`telos.h`** — All types: tokens, AST, IR, constraint graph, execution plans, public API
- **`lexer.c`** — Tokenizer (keywords, two-char operators, comments, float/int literals)
- **`parser.c`** — Recursive-descent parser, full expression precedence chain
- **`ir.c`** — IR node constructors, deep clone/free, algebraic simplifier, constant evaluator
- **`lifting.c`** — AST → constraint IR; accumulation loop → `ReductionConstraint` pattern detection
- **`optimizer.c`** — Polynomial decomposition, closed-form summation (degree ≤ 3), constant folding (≤10k iters), goal-graph liveness / dead-code elimination, cost-model plan selection
- **`codegen_x86.c`** — x86-64 machine code emission (System V AMD64 ABI, stack-slot allocation, jump patching)
- **`codegen_c.c`** — `long long`-based C source emission
- **`executable.c`** — `mmap(PROT_EXEC)` wrapper for native execution
- **`compiler.c`** — High-level API (`telos_compile`, `telos_run`, `telos_compile_c`) + CLI
- **`Makefile`** — `make` builds `telos`, `make test` runs suite
- **`tests/test_main.c`** — 45 tests across lexer, parser, lifting, optimizer, and end-to-end execution

### Verification

All optimizations from the spec are exercised end-to-end:

```bash
$ cd csrc && make test
=== Results: 45 passed, 0 failed, 45 total ===

$ ./telos ../examples/fixed_sum.telos
Function: fixed_sum  (29 bytes of x86-64 machine code)
  # constant-folded to mov rax, 45
```

Covers: Gauss formula (`Σi`), sum-of-squares (`Σi²`), sum-of-cubes (`Σi³`), linear combinations, constant folding, inclusive ranges, multi-function files, identity/arithmetic functions.

### Other changes

- **`.gitignore`** — Added `csrc/*.o`, `csrc/telos`, `csrc/tests/test_telos`
- **`README.md`** — Updated repo layout, added C build/run/test instructions alongside existing Python docs

Python implementation and its 119 tests are untouched and still pass.

title: phytron_phymotion: Python3 compatibility fixes, import refactor and move_absolute bugfix
body:
### Motivation

- Make the `phytron_phymotion` package compatible with Python 3 by replacing Python 2-specific types and modernizing imports and checks.
- Fix an incorrect variable usage in `move_absolute` that produced wrong command strings.

### Description

- Converted top-level imports to relative imports across `driver.py`, `factory.py`, `message.py`, and `protocol.py` by replacing bare module imports with `from .module import ...` usage.
- Replaced Python 2 type checks (`long`, `basestring`) with Python 3-compatible checks (`int`, `str`) in `message.py`, `messages/parameter.py`, and `message.AxisMessage` setters.
- Added `__bool__` and preserved `__nonzero__` compatibility in `AbstractResponse` so truthiness works on Python 3 and remains available for Python 2 consumers.
- Fixed `move_absolute` to use the passed `position` argument and ensure its sign and absolute value are formatted correctly in the sent command (`'A' + _signum(position) + str(abs(position))`).
- Cleaned up unused imports in `driver.py` and `protocol.py` (removed obsolete/timeouts and unused symbols), and simplified message/response construction to use local module classes.

### Testing

- Performed a basic smoke test by importing the updated `phytron_phymotion` package modules to verify there are no import errors; the import checks succeeded.

------

title: Add pyserial transport, Python 3 compatibility, and protocol/driver fixes
body:
### Motivation

- Remove the dependency on `e21_util` and provide a minimal, portable serial transport implementation using `pyserial` for direct device access.
- Make the codebase Python 3 compatible by updating type checks and boolean methods and by improving exception handling.
- Harden communication with the controller by adding a `CommunicationError` type, replacing the inter-process lock with an internal `RLock`, and fixing a bug in the driver movement command.

### Description

- Add `SerialTransport` in `phytron_phymotion/transport.py` that wraps `pyserial` and exposes `write`, `read_until`, and `read_bytes` as expected by `PhytronProtocol`.
- Replace `e21_util` usage in `factory.py` with standard logging (using `logging.getLogger` and `NullHandler`), environment variable `PHYTRON_DEVICE` fallback for the device path, and instantiate `SerialTransport` for the driver creation.
- Introduce `CommunicationError` in `errors.py` and update `protocol.py` to use `threading.RLock`, better timeout handling, and exception chaining when read fails, and to raise `CommunicationError` on invalid responses.
- Apply Python 3 compatibility changes across `message.py`, `messages/parameter.py`, and `protocol.py` by replacing `long/basestring/__nonzero__/InterProcessTransportLock` usages with `int/str/__bool__/RLock` and updating type checks and response handling.
- Fix a variable bug in `PhytronDriver.move_absolute` by using the correct `position` argument and applying `abs(position)` when building the axis command.
- Update `setup.py` to depend on `pyserial` instead of `e21_util`.

### Testing

- No automated tests were executed as part of this change.

------

title: Refactor phytron_phymotion for standalone operation and Python3 compatibility
body:
### Motivation
- Remove legacy external dependencies (`slave`, `e21_util`) and provide a minimal, self-contained serial driver stack. 
- Modernize code for Python 3 compatibility (bytes/str handling, removal of `long`/`basestring`, use of `__bool__`).
- Provide a clear architecture overview and a small transport adapter to hide `pyserial` details. 

### Description
- Add `ARCHITECTURE.md` documenting package structure and dataflow. 
- Introduce `SerialTransport` in `phytron_phymotion/transport.py` as a thin `pyserial` adapter exposing `write`, `read_until`, and `read_bytes`. 
- Add `errors.py` with a `CommunicationError` exception and remove dependence on `e21_util.error`. 
- Replace earlier protocol implementation with `phytron_phymotion/protocol.py` that uses a `threading.RLock`, in-process locking, simplified `query`/`read_response` flow, and returns `None` for invalid message types instead of raising. 
- Update `message.py` to be Python 3 safe by replacing `long`/`basestring` checks with `int`/`str`, implement `__bool__`, and tidy response handling. 
- Modify `driver.py` to remove external framework base classes, adapt to the new protocol/transport interfaces, fix small bugs in sign handling and `move_absolute`, and return `None` for missing responses. 
- Update `factory.py` to use `SerialTransport`, standard `logging`, and use `os.environ['PHYTRON_DEVICE']` fallback for device selection. 
- Update `setup.py` to declare `pyserial` as the installation requirement and remove legacy requirements. 

### Testing
- No automated tests were executed as part of this change.

------

title: Refactor to pure-Python serial backend, Python3 compat, add transport/protocol, docs and examples
body:
### Motivation

- Remove hard external dependencies and adapt the package to a standalone Python3-friendly implementation for controlling Phytron phyMotion devices.
- Provide a minimal, well-documented serial transport/protocol stack and example scripts to make the driver usable without the original framework glue.

### Description

- Reworked the core to remove dependence on `slave` and `e21_util` by introducing `SerialTransport` (`phytron_phymotion/transport.py`) backed by `pyserial` and a simplified `PhytronProtocol` (`phytron_phymotion/protocol.py`).
- Made the driver independent of external frameworks by replacing the subclassing of `Driver` with a plain `PhytronDriver` (`phytron_phymotion/driver.py`) and adjusted message handling and error-return behavior to be more robust for missing responses.
- Updated message handling for Python 3 compatibility (removed `long`/`basestring`, adjusted truth/boolean hooks) and fixed small command/sign handling issues in `move_absolute` and other methods (`phytron_phymotion/message.py`, `messages/parameter.py`).
- Added `errors.py` (`CommunicationError`), factory changes to use a `SerialTransport` and environment variable `PHYTRON_DEVICE` for the device path, new docs `ARCHITECTURE.md`, and two example scripts under `examples/` demonstrating basic usage; updated `setup.py` to require `pyserial` only.

### Testing

- Performed a basic automated import smoke test with `python -c "import phytron_phymotion"`, which completed successfully.
- No existing automated unit test suite was present and no additional unit tests were executed as part of this change.

------

title: Make phytron_phymotion standalone (Python3 port, add transport/protocol, driver refactor, examples & docs)
body:
### Motivation

- Remove heavy external dependencies and modernize the package to be self-contained and Python 3 compatible so it can be used without the original `slave` / `e21_util` libraries.
- Provide a minimal, well-documented interface for serial communication with phytron phyMotion devices and ship simple example scripts for common operations.

### Description

- Ported codebase to Python 3 by replacing Python 2-specific types and idioms (removed `long`/`basestring`, added `__bool__` fallback, simplified `is None` checks).
- Replaced external transport/locking libraries with a new `SerialTransport` adapter in `phytron_phymotion/transport.py` and a simplified thread-safe `PhytronProtocol` in `phytron_phymotion/protocol.py` that uses an `RLock` and explicit read/write helpers.
- Refactored `PhytronDriver` to remove dependency on external framework classes and to operate on the new transport/protocol API, improved response handling and some API fixes (e.g. `_signum`, `move_absolute`, safer `get_absolute_counter`/`get_position` behavior).
- Added small `CommunicationError` exception type in `phytron_phymotion/errors.py` and updated factory in `phytron_phymotion/factory.py` to create `SerialTransport`, configure logger, and allow `PHYTRON_DEVICE` environment override.
- Added usage examples `examples/minimal_single_file_current_and_move.py` and `examples/set_current_and_move_plus_200.py`, and a high-level `ARCHITECTURE.md` describing package structure and dataflow.
- Updated `setup.py` to drop previous heavy requirements and depend only on `pyserial` in `install_requires`.

### Testing

- No automated test suite was executed as part of this rollout.

title: Add CallGuard AI — MeetStream MIA + FastMCP + Scalekit integration
body:
Bootstraps the full CallGuard AI project: a silent call-monitoring agent that scores customer calls for churn risk, refund likelihood, and escalation need, then autonomously creates Jira tickets and sends Gmail alerts via Scalekit-authenticated tool calls.

## Structure

```
callguard-ai/
├── mcp_server/
│   ├── server.py          # FastMCP server — exposes create_jira_ticket + send_gmail at port 8000
│   ├── Dockerfile         # python:3.11-slim, exposes 8000
│   └── requirements.txt   # fastmcp, scalekit-sdk-python, python-dotenv
├── webhook/
│   └── main.py            # Flask webhook — scores MeetStream transcription events, calls Scalekit tools directly
├── auth_check.py          # One-time interactive OAuth consent flow for Jira + Gmail connected accounts
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```

## Key design points

- **Two execution paths**: MIA agent → MCP server → Scalekit (real-time, LLM-driven) and webhook → Scalekit direct (event-driven fallback for batch/transcript events)
- **Scoring heuristic** in `webhook/main.py` keyword-matches transcript text; thresholds at 1 hit (Medium) and 3 hits (High)
- **Tool routing**: `create_jira_ticket` fires on churn OR escalation High; `send_gmail` only on escalation High, with Jira URL injected into body
- **Auth gating**: `ensure_all_authenticated()` runs at webhook startup — blocks boot if Scalekit connected accounts are not active
- **Security**: Flask `debug` mode is off by default; opt-in via `FLASK_DEBUG=true` env var (fixes CodeQL `py/flask-debug`)
- **README** includes full MIA system prompt with explicit tool signatures and scoring rules, plus `create_bot` curl command

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


## CallGuard AI — MeetStream + Scalekit MCP Integration

Create the full CallGuard AI project structure in this repository. This is a complete working integration between MeetStream MIA (AI meeting agent), a Docker-based FastMCP server, and Scalekit for authenticated tool calling (Jira + Gmail).

### Architecture

```
MeetStream MIA Agent
        ↓  (MCP Server URL = ngrok URL pointing to Docker)
Docker MCP Server — FastMCP (port 8000)
        ↓  (tool calls: create_jira_ticket, send_gmail)
Scalekit — validates OAuth tokens, executes tools on behalf of user
        ↓
Jira + Gmail
```

### File Structure to Create

```
callguard-ai/
├── mcp_server/
│   ├── server.py
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   └── requirements.txt
├── webhook/
│   └── main.py
├── auth_check.py
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```

---

### `mcp_server/server.py`

```python
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from fastmcp import FastMCP
from scalekit.client import ScalekitClient

load_dotenv(dotenv_path="../.env")

IDENTIFIER       = os.getenv("IDENTIFIER", "user-001")
JIRA_CONNECTION  = os.getenv("JIRA_CONNECTION_NAME", "jira")
GMAIL_CONNECTION = os.getenv("GMAIL_CONNECTION_NAME", "gmail")

scalekit = ScalekitClient(
    env_url=os.getenv("SCALEKIT_ENV_URL"),
    client_id=os.getenv("SCALEKIT_CLIENT_ID"),
    client_secret=os.getenv("SCALEKIT_CLIENT_SECRET"),
)

mcp = FastMCP("callguard-tools")


@mcp.tool()
def create_jira_ticket(summary: str, description: str, priority: str = "High") -> str:
    """
    Create a Jira ticket for a high-risk or escalation call.
    Use when churn risk is High or escalation need is High.
    Parameters:
    - summary: One-line title e.g. "[High Escalation] Customer threatening to cancel — 2026-03-28"
    - description: Full breakdown including churn risk, refund likelihood, escalation need, and a 2-3 sentence call summary
    - priority: "High", "Medium", or "Low" — match to the highest score across all three dimensions
    """
    result = scalekit.actions.execute_tool(
        tool_name="jira_issue_create",
        identifier=IDENTIFIER,
        tool_input={
            "project_key": os.getenv("JIRA_PROJECT_KEY", "CG"),
            "summary": summary,
            "description": description,
            "issue_type": "Task",
            "priority": priority,
        },
    )
    return f"Jira ticket created: {result.data.get('url', 'done')}"


@mcp.tool()
def send_gmail(to: str, subject: str, body: str) -> str:
    """
    Send a Gmail alert to a stakeholder about a critical call outcome.
    Use when escalation need is High. Include Jira ticket URL in body if one was created.
    Parameters:
    - to: The escalation email address
    - subject: e.g. "🚨 CallGuard Alert — High Escalation on call 2026-03-28"
    - body: Include churn risk, refund likelihood, escalation need, call summary, and Jira ticket URL
    """
    scalekit.actions.execute_tool(
        tool_name="gmail_send_email",
        identifier=IDENTIFIER,
        tool_input={"to": to, "subject": subject, "body": body},
    )
    return f"Email sent to {to}"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="streamable-http", host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)
```

---

### `mcp_server/requirements.txt`

```
fastmcp>=2.0.0
scalekit-sdk-python>=2.6.1
python-dotenv>=1.1.1
```

---

### `mcp_server/Dockerfile`

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY server.py .
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python", "server.py"]
```

---

title: feat(Ingestion): Remove dataframe and make batch job DRA-1118
body:
### Summary 

- Replaced pd.DataFrame with list[dict] throughout the ingestion pipeline (DBService, SQLLocalService, QdrantService, all three ingestion sources) to reduce memory footprint. Legacy DF wrappers kept for backward compatibility in non-ingestion paths.
- Added batched flushing (INGESTION_BATCH_SIZE=500) so large datasets are written to DB/Qdrant in chunks instead of all at once.
- Added RLIMIT_AS memory caps on ingestion and webhook subprocess workers to fail early on OOM instead of getting killed by K8s.
- Enhanced dead-letter queue with OOM error classification, structured metadata, and a CLI utility for listing/replaying/purging dead-lettered messages.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Configurable subprocess memory cap and new ingestion batch size (default 500).
  * Row-oriented, streaming APIs for reading, inserting, and syncing large datasets.
  * New datetime parsing/normalization utilities.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved detection and clearer user-facing messages for out-of-memory failures with remediation hint.

* **Performance**
  * Lower memory usage and more efficient large-batch processing via streaming and simplified in-memory handling.

* **Tests**
  * Updated tests and mocks to use row/list-based workflows.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

title: Fix pydub on Python 3.13+ and eliminate per-entry ffmpeg errors
body:
Two related issues: `audioop` was removed in Python 3.13 (PEP 594), breaking pydub's import entirely; and when ffmpeg is missing from PATH, the script emits `ERROR: pydub processing failed: [WinError 2]` once per Pokémon entry (~532×) instead of failing fast.

## `tools/requirements.txt`
- Add `audioop-lts; python_version>="3.13"` — LTS backport of `audioop`, no-op on older Pythons

## `tools/generate_voices.py`
- Add `HAS_FFMPEG = HAS_PYDUB and bool(shutil.which("ffmpeg") or shutil.which("avconv"))` checked once at module load
- Gate pydub processing on `HAS_PYDUB and HAS_FFMPEG`; raw-audio fallback runs silently (startup warning is sufficient)
- Suppress pydub's own `RuntimeWarning: Couldn't find ffmpeg` at import time via `warnings.catch_warnings()` — replaced by our single, OS-specific startup warning
- Startup warning block gains an `elif not HAS_FFMPEG` branch with actionable install instructions per OS

## `README.md`
- Requirements table: note Python 3.13+ support via `audioop-lts`; fix install path to `tools/requirements.txt`
- Troubleshooting table: replace vague `ffmpeg not found` row with specific rows for `[WinError 2]`, `ffmpeg not found on PATH`, and the Python 3.13 `audioop` ImportError

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problem

On Python 3.13+, the built-in `audioop` module was removed (PEP 594). This causes `pydub` to fail to import because it depends on `audioop` internally. The error manifests as:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audioop'
```

followed by:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pyaudioop'
```

Because the script catches this `ImportError` and sets `HAS_PYDUB = False`, users see a misleading warning: `"pydub is not installed"` even though pydub IS installed — it just can't load without `audioop`.

## Fix

Add `audioop-lts` as a conditional dependency in `tools/requirements.txt`. This is the community-maintained LTS port of the removed `audioop` module for Python 3.13+.

Add the following line to `tools/requirements.txt` under the audio processing section (near the `pydub` dependency):

```
audioop-lts; python_version>="3.13"
```

This ensures the dependency is only installed on Python 3.13+ where `audioop` is missing, and has no effect on older Python versions.

Also update the README.md if it contains any relevant installation or troubleshooting instructions to mention this Python 3.13+ requirement.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: It keeps saying pydub not installed but it is

Assistant: Now I can see the issue. Looking at the code in `generate_voices.py`, here's what's happening:

## The Problem

The script detects `pydub` at **import time** (lines 82–87) by setting a global `HAS_PYDUB` flag:

```python name=tools/generate_voices.py url=https://github.com/SilvertongueRED/KIF-Pokedex-Voice-Over/blob/2dc4bf32850c3ffdf09edb32675b3faa695d1597/tools/generate_voices.py#L82-L87
try:
    from pydub import AudioSegment
    from pydub.effects import normalize
    HAS_PYDUB = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_PYDUB = False
```

Even though `pydub` the Python package is installed, the import can **silently fail** if **ffmpeg is not installed or not on your PATH**. When `pydub` loads, it tries to locate `ffmpeg`/`ffprobe`, and some versions or configurations raise an `ImportError` or a downstream error during the import of submodules when ffmpeg is missing.

This causes `HAS_PYDUB` to be `False`, and when the script runs it prints (line 633–638):

> `WARNING: pydub is not installed — Dexter audio effects will be skipped.`

## The Fix

**Install ffmpeg and make sure it's on your system PATH:**

| OS | Command |
|---|---|
| **Windows** | Download from [ffmpeg.org](https://ffmpeg.org/download.html), extract, and add the `bin/` folder to your system PATH. Or use `choco install ffmpeg` / `winget install ffmpeg`. |
| **macOS** | `brew install ffmpeg` |
| **Linux (Debian/Ubuntu)** | `sudo apt install ffmpeg` |

After installing ffmpeg, verify it works:
```bash
ffmpeg -version
```

Then re-run the script — `pydub` should import successfully and `HAS_PYDUB` will be `True`.

## How to confirm this is the issue

You can test it quickly in Python:

```bash
python -c "from pydub import AudioSegment; from pydub.effects import normalize; print('pydub OK')"
```

If this prints `pydub OK`, the script should work. If it throws an error mentioning ffmpeg/ffprobe, that confirms ffmpeg is the missing piece.

User: ffmpeg is in path. 
This is the error I get trying to test like you say:
PS C:\Users\rolan> python -c "from pydub import AudioSegment; from pydub.effects import normalize; print('pydub OK')"
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\utils.py", line 14, in <module>
    import audioop
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audioop'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
    from pydub import AudioSegment; from pydub.effects import normalize; print('pydub OK')
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
    from .audio_segment import AudioSegment
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\audio_segment.py", line 11, in <module>
    from .utils import mediainfo_json, fsdecode
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\utils.py", line 16, in <module>
    import pyaudioop as audioop
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pyaudioop'
PS C:\Users\rolan>

Assistant: ## Root Cause

This is a **Python version incompatibility**, not a pydub installation problem.

You're running **Python 3.14**, which (along with 3.13) **removed the built-in `audioop` module** that pydub depends on ([PEP 594](https://peps.python.org/pep-0594/)). You can see pydub trying two things and both failing:

1. `import audioop` → ❌ removed from Python 3.13+
2. `import pyaudioop as audioop` → ❌ not installed

Because this import fails, the entire `from pydub import AudioSegment` raises `ImportError`, which the script's `try/except` catche...

</details>

title: fix: upgrade pydantic and openai for Python 3.13 compatibility
body:
Railway deploys on Python 3.13, which is incompatible with pydantic-core==2.16.3 (bundled with pydantic==2.6.4). The build failed because pydantic-core had no pre-built wheel for Python 3.13, forcing a Rust/maturin source compilation that crashed due to a breaking change in ForwardRef._evaluate() in Python 3.13.

Bumped versions to packages that ship pre-built py3.13 wheels:
- pydantic: 2.6.4 → 2.7.4
- openai:   1.14.0 → 1.30.0
- fastapi:  0.110.0 → 0.115.0

No source code changes required — purely a dependency version fix.

title: Fix Railway build failure and security vulnerabilities in requirements.txt
body:
Railway's default Python 3.13 runtime rejected the pinned dependencies — `numpy`, `matplotlib`, `kiwisolver`, and `contourpy` had no Python 3.13 wheels and failed to compile from source.

## Dependency upgrades

| Package | Old | New | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| `numpy` | 1.24.3 | 2.2.4 | 1.24.x tops out at Python 3.11; 2.x required for 3.13 |
| `matplotlib` | 3.7.1 | 3.9.4 | Python 3.13 wheels available from 3.8+ |
| `kiwisolver` | 1.4.4 | 1.4.8 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `contourpy` | 1.0.7 | 1.3.1 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `Pillow` | 9.5.0 | 12.1.1 | Python 3.13 wheels + CVE: out-of-bounds write in PSD loader |
| `fonttools` | 4.39.4 | 4.55.3 | Python 3.13 wheels |
| `Django` | 4.2.1 | 4.2.29 | CVE: SQL injection in column aliases and `_connector` kwarg; DoS in `HttpResponseRedirect` on Windows |
| `asgiref`, `cycler`, `packaging`, `pyparsing`, `sqlparse`, `tzdata` | various | bumped | Routine updates alongside above |

## New files

- **`.python-version`** — pins `3.13` so Railway/Nixpacks uses an explicit version rather than defaulting to whatever is latest at build time.

title: 🔒 [security] Replace hardcoded APNs tokens and improve Python 3 compatibility
body:
This pull request addresses a security vulnerability where a hardcoded APNs token was present in the test suite and documentation.

🎯 **What:** Replaced the hardcoded APNs token `b5bb9d8014a0f9b1d61e21e796d78dccdf1352f23cd32812f4850b87ae4944c` with a dummy token `00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff`.
⚠️ **Risk:** Hardcoding tokens that look like production data is a security risk and bad practice. While these were in tests, replacing them with clearly marked dummy values is a best practice.
🛡️ **Solution:**
- Replaced tokens in `tests.py` and `README.markdown`.
- Updated `tests.py` and `apns.py` to be compatible with Python 3, which was necessary to verify the fix in the current environment. This included:
    - Updating exception handling syntax.
    - Replacing `xrange` with `range`.
    - Correctly handling `str` vs `bytes` in binary data construction and parsing.
- Refactored `testFrame` in `tests.py` to construct the expected binary output dynamically instead of using a hardcoded string, making the test more robust and readable.

All tests passed successfully using Python 3.10.19.

---

title: Migrate to GitHub Actions CI
body:
## Migrate CI from Travis CI to GitHub Actions

Travis CI was inherited from the upstream orator project and tested Python 2.7–3.7 — versions incompatible with this fork's python = "^3.10" requirement. Travis is also effectively unavailable for open-source projects. This PR replaces it with GitHub Actions.

Changes:

- Add .github/workflows/tests.yml with two jobs:
- unit-tests — runs across Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14 (no external services needed)
- integration-tests — runs across Python 3.10 and 3.12 using Docker Compose to spin up Postgres 14 and MySQL 8
- Delete .travis.yml
- Delete tox.ini (also stale, same Python 2.7–3.7 matrix)
- Fix exit-code masking in make test and make test-int — previously docker-down ran via ; which swallowed a failing pytest exit code; now the exit code is captured and re-raised after cleanup

title: [codex] migrate Python tooling to uv
body:
## Summary
This change migrates the repository from the old single `requirements.txt` workflow to a root `uv` project backed by `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`, and adds a repo-level `Makefile` to make the common development commands easier to use.

For contributors, the practical effect is that Python setup, test execution, app startup, and script usage now run through `uv` consistently from the repository root. The repo still behaves as one shared Python application spanning the existing backend modules, so the migration improves dependency management and local reproducibility without forcing a risky package split.

## Problem
The repository was still carrying a mixed Python toolchain story: a single `requirements.txt`, shell-default interpreter drift, and docs/scripts that assumed `pip`-style installation or direct `python scripts/...` execution. That made local setup less reproducible and made it too easy to end up on the wrong interpreter or with imports that only worked incidentally from a specific shell context.

At the same time, the project needed a lighter-weight contributor workflow. The new `Makefile` provides short aliases for the commands people are actually using repeatedly, while keeping the underlying `uv` commands explicit and visible.

## Root Cause
The Python side of the monorepo had never been formalized as a root project. It relied on repo-root execution conventions and implicit import behavior instead of an explicit dependency manager and lockfile.

That was especially visible in direct script execution and pytest collection: once the repo moved under `uv`, commands needed a stable way to resolve the repo-root modules (`api`, `agents`, `shared`) from the root project environment.

## Fix
The change introduces a root `pyproject.toml` with Python 3.12 constraints, first-party runtime dependencies, and a dev dependency group, then generates and commits `uv.lock`. The old `requirements.txt` file is removed.

The docs and script/help text were updated to standardize on `uv sync`, `uv run pytest tests`, `uv run python -m uvicorn ...`, and `uv run python scripts/...` commands. A concise `AGENTS.md` was also rewritten to reflect the repo's real toolchain and constraints.

To keep the current monorepo shape working safely, the Python project remains a single shared app rather than being split into multiple Python packages. Tests and direct repo scripts were updated so imports continue to resolve correctly from the root `uv` environment.

The new `Makefile` adds short, repo-friendly commands for setup, tests, local servers, smoke checks, formatting, linting, type checking, and registry sync. `make check` is positioned as the practical passing verification set, while `make lint` and `make typecheck` are available to expose the existing repo-wide backlog.

## Validation
I validated the migration with the commands below:

- `uv sync`
- `uv run pytest tests -q`
- `uv run python scripts/sync_agents_from_registry.py --help`
- `uv run python -m uvicorn api.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8010`
- `uv run python -m uvicorn agents.research.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5002`
- `make help`
- `make check`

## Notes
`make lint` / `ruff` and `make typecheck` / `mypy` are intentionally exposed in the `Makefile`, but they still report pre-existing repo-wide issues and were not treated as merge-blocking for this migration.

title: Element count from xyz
body:
## Summary
  This PR extends `xyz_to_periodic_table.py` so periodic-table visualizations can be generated from XYZ/EXTXYZ or CSV inputs.
  It also adds practical plotting controls, including fraction/log-fraction modes, excluded-element rendering, and text-color options.

## Changes
- Added support for XYZ/EXTXYZ and CSV input (`Element,element_count`) 
- Made CSV export default behavior (`--save-csv` still allows override)
- Added `--fraction` (`count / max_count`)
- Added `--log-fraction` (`log10(element_fraction)`)
- Updated colorbar title in `--log-fraction` mode to `log(Element fraction)`
- Added `--unique-structure` (deduplicate by `info['structure_name']`)
- Added `--exclude-elements` (excluded from counting; rendered white with black border)
- Added `--cmap`, `--color-min`, and `--color-max`
- Added `--print-data` (in-cell values, hide NaN labels, integer display)
- Added `--all-black-text` (force all labels to black)
- Added validation to disallow `--log-scale` with `--log-fraction`
- Updated README and CHANGELOG
- Fixed pandas compatibility in core (`iteritems` -> `items`)

## Notes
- PNG export depends on Bokeh image export runtime requirements (selenium + browser/driver).

title: [WIP] Update requirements.txt for Streamlit Cloud compatibility
body:
- [x] Update `requirements.txt` with correct, compatible package versions for Streamlit Cloud (Python 3.11+):
  - Fix `openpyxl==3.10.0` → `openpyxl>=3.1.2` (non-existent version causing pip failure)
  - Update `python-Levenshtein==0.21.1` → `python-Levenshtein>=0.25.0` (compatible with Python 3.11+)
  - Update `pandas==1.5.3` → `pandas>=2.0.0` (Python 3.11+ compatible)
  - Update `numpy==1.24.3` → `numpy>=1.26.0` (Python 3.11+ compatible)
  - Switch all constraints from `==` to `>=` to prevent future failures from unavailable exact versions

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Problem
> 
> The Streamlit Cloud deployment is failing with `installer returned a non-zero exit code` / `Error during processing dependencies!` because of invalid/non-existent package versions in `requirements.txt`.
> 
> ### Root Causes
> 
> 1. **`openpyxl==3.10.0`** — This version does **not exist** on PyPI. The actual version series is `3.1.x`. This causes pip to fail immediately.
> 2. **`python-Levenshtein==0.21.1`** — Outdated and can fail to build on Python 3.11+ environments used by Streamlit Cloud.
> 3. **`pandas==1.5.3`** and **`numpy==1.24.3`** — These are old and incompatible with Python 3.11+, which Streamlit Cloud now uses by default.
> 
> ### Fix Required
> 
> Update `requirements.txt` with correct, compatible package versions that work on Streamlit Cloud (Python 3.11+):
> 
> ```
> streamlit>=1.28.1
> pandas>=2.0.0
> openpyxl>=3.1.2
> plotly>=5.17.0
> fuzzywuzzy>=0.18.0
> python-Levenshtein>=0.25.0
> numpy>=1.26.0
> altair>=5.0.1
> ```
> 
> Replace the contents of `requirements.txt` exactly with the above. Using `>=` minimum version constraints avoids pip failures caused by unavailable exact versions on Streamlit Cloud's resolver.
> 
> 
> The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):
> 
> User: In this repo, I have the code for price benchmarking. Right now, I have only azithromycin to compare from both the excels. I am giving the input azithromycin of frontend, however I ma not getting any visualizations that have been implemented.
> 
> User: @Copilot Accepted Confirmation: Are you sure?
> 
> Assistant: I've kicked off a deep research session to investigate the full codebase and pinpoint exactly why the visualizations aren't rendering when you enter azithromycin. It will examine the data loading pipeline, drug name matching logic, any conditional rendering gates, and potential silent failures.
> 
> ````list type="task"
> data:
>     - title: 'Investigate the full codebase to understand: 1. How the Streamlit dashboard is s...'
>       repository: TanviEY/pharma-price-benchmarking
>       url: https://github.com/TanviEY/pharma-price-benchmarking/tasks/bae49fe9-993b-4212-bb79-4655479dd844
>       state: queued
> ````
> 
> The research session will look into:
> - **String matching** — case sensitivity, whitespace, exact vs. partial matching for "azithromycin"
> - **Data loading** — how the two Excel files are read and which columns are used for filtering
> - **Visualization gating** — any `if` conditions that prevent charts from rendering
> - **Silent failures** — empty DataFrames, exception swallowing, or missing `st.plotly_chart`/`st.pyplot` calls
> 
> You can track the progress at the link above. Once it completes, I'll have a detailed breakdown of the root cause and a fix.
> 
> User:  installer returned a non-zero exit code
>  Error during processing dependencies! Please fix the error and push an update, or try restarting the app.
> 
> Getting the above error when deploying to streamlit
> 
> Assistant: I can immediately see the issues in `requirements.txt`. There are **two broken package versions**:
> 
> 1. **`openpyxl==3.10.0`** — This version does **not exist**. The latest is `3.1.x`. Streamlit Cloud's pip installer will fail hard on this.
> 2. **`python-Levenshtein==0.21.1`** — This version is also problematic on some Python environments. The current stable version is `0.25.x`.
> 
> Additionally, the pinned versions of `pandas==1.5.3` and `numpy==1.24.3` are quite old and may conflict with each other or with the Python version Streamlit Cloud uses (Python 3.11+).
> 
> Would you like me to create a PR that fixes the `requirements.txt` with correct, compatible versions? Here's what I'd update it to:
> 
> ```pip requirements name=requirements.txt
> streamlit>=1.28.1
> pandas>=2.0.0
> openpyxl>=3.1.2
> plotly>=5.17.0
> fuzzywuzzy>=0.18.0
> python-Levenshtein>=0.25.0
> numpy>=1.26.0
> altair>=5.0.1
> ```
> 
> Using `>=` (minimum versions) is more robust on Streamlit Cloud as it avoids pip being unable to find exact versions. If you prefer strict pinning, I can do that too with the correct version numbers.
> 
> **Shall I open a PR to fix the `requirements.txt`?**
> 
> User: Yes
> 
> Assistant: I've submitted the PR creation task to the coding agent. It will:
> 
> 1. Fix `openpyxl==3.10.0` → `openpyxl>=3.1.2` (the non-existent version that was crashing pip)
> 2. Update `python-Levenshtein` to `>=0.25.0` (compatible with Python 3.11+)
> 3. Update `pandas` and `numpy` to versions compatible with Streamlit Cloud's Python 3.11 environment
> 4. Switch to `>=` constraints to prevent future failures from unavailable exact versions
> 
> Once the PR is ready, you'll see it in your repo at [github.com/TanviEY/pharma-price-benchmarking/pulls](https://github.com/TanviEY/pharma-price-benchmarking/pulls). Just merge it and Streamlit Cloud should redeploy successfully.
> 
> 


</details>

title: Apply manual Python3 fixes (2to3-equivalent)
Fix CI: Python 3.10 compatibility and black formatting drift
body:
Two CI failures on the `copilot/fix-module-not-found-error` branch: `datetime.UTC` (Python 3.11+) caused `ImportError` on the Python 3.10 runners, and 9 files had accumulated Black formatting drift.

## Changes

- **`datetime.UTC` → `timezone.utc`** across all affected files — `datetime.UTC` was introduced in Python 3.11; replaced with `timezone.utc` (available since Python 3.2) to restore 3.10 compatibility:
  ```python
  # Before (Python 3.11+ only)
  from datetime import UTC, datetime
  return datetime.now(UTC)

  # After
  from datetime import datetime, timezone
  return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  ```
  Files affected: `auth/model.py`, `finance/models.py`, `finance/routes.py`, `finance/service.py`, `tests/test_finance.py`

- **Black formatting** — ran `black .` to bring all 9 drifted files into conformance with the enforced style check

title: Add live web dashboard for system monitoring
body:
## Summary
- Standalone Node.js/TypeScript web dashboard (`dashboard/`) with Express + WebSocket backend and React + Tailwind frontend
- **Kanban view** with task-centric output streaming — click any In Progress, Completed, or Failed card to see its Claude output in a split panel
- **Overview tab** with queue depths, active locks, consumer groups, and stuck task alerts
- Stuck task detection: orphaned pending markers, high attempt counts, stale consumer group entries
- Multi-project support via wildcard Redis key patterns (`*:tasks:*`, `*:lock:*`, etc.)
- Separate Docker container on `orcest` network (read-only Redis access, 512MB limit)
- Makefile targets: `build-dashboard`, `dev-dashboard`, `deploy-dashboard`

## Architecture
```
Browser  ←WebSocket→  Express + ws (Node container)  ←ioredis→  Redis
                      serves static Vite build
```

## Test plan
- [ ] `cd dashboard && npm ci && npm run build` — server + frontend build clean
- [ ] `cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit` — no type errors
- [ ] `docker build -t orcest-dashboard ./dashboard` — image builds
- [ ] Run alongside orchestrator via `docker-compose.dashboard.yml` — accessible on :8080
- [ ] WebSocket connection indicator shows "Connected"
- [ ] Overview tab shows live queue depths, locks, consumer groups
- [ ] Kanban tab shows cards in Queued/In Progress/Completed/Failed columns
- [ ] Clicking an In Progress card opens live task output panel
- [ ] Clicking a Completed/Failed card shows historical task output

title: App cleanup: Remove duplicate code and improve function naming clarity
body:
## Overview

This PR addresses code quality issues identified during preparation for production deployment. The changes are minimal and surgical, focusing on removing redundancy and improving code clarity without altering functionality.

## Changes Made

### 1. Fixed Duplicate CSV File Type Check

**File:** `src/utils/cleaning.py`

The `load_provider_data()` function had a duplicate condition checking for CSV files:

```python
# Before
if suffix == ".csv":
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
elif suffix == ".xlsx":
    df = pd.read_excel(path)
elif suffix == ".csv":  # ❌ Duplicate check
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
```

```python
# After
if suffix == ".csv":
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
elif suffix == ".xlsx":
    df = pd.read_excel(path)
# ✅ Duplicate removed
```

This duplicate condition was unreachable code that added confusion. The fix improves maintainability and follows DRY principles.

### 2. Renamed Misleading Function Name

**File:** `src/data/preparation.py`

The function `_load_excel()` was misleadingly named as it handles both Excel **and** CSV files. This caused confusion for developers reading the code, as documented in `docs/INGESTION_MODULE_REVIEW.md`.

```python
# Before
def _load_excel(raw_input: Any, filename: Optional[str] = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load Excel data from various input types."""
    # ... handles both Excel AND CSV files
```

```python
# After
def _load_data(raw_input: Any, filename: Optional[str] = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Load data from various input types (Excel, CSV, or DataFrame).
    
    Args:
        raw_input: Can be Path/str to file, BytesIO/bytes buffer, or pd.DataFrame
        filename: Optional filename for logging and format detection
    
    Returns:
        pd.DataFrame with normalized column names (whitespace stripped)
    """
    # ... handles both Excel AND CSV files
```

Updated all call sites (2 locations) and improved docstrings throughout to accurately reflect CSV support.

### 3. Code Style Improvements

**File:** `src/utils/cleaning.py`

- Changed `== False` comparison to pythonic `~mask` negation operator
- Removed trailing whitespace
- Improved variable naming: `work_empty_mask` → `work_not_empty_mask` for clarity

### 4. Added Comprehensive Documentation

**File:** `docs/APP_CLEANUP_SUMMARY.md` (new)

Created a detailed summary document covering:
- All changes made during cleanup
- Code quality assessment
- Deployment readiness evaluation
- Recommendations for future enhancements

## Testing

All tests pass with no regressions:
- ✅ **114 tests passed**, 1 skipped
- ✅ No new linting errors introduced
- ✅ Full backward compatibility maintained
- ✅ All existing functionality preserved

## Impact

These changes improve code quality and developer experience without changing application behavior:

- **Better Clarity**: Function names accurately describe what they do
- **Reduced Redundancy**: Removed unreachable duplicate code
- **Improved Documentation**: Accurate docstrings reflecting actual functionality
- **Pythonic Code**: Follows Python best practices for boolean operations

## Deployment Readiness

The application has been assessed and is **ready for production deployment** with:
- Clean, well-organized codebase
- Comprehensive test suite (114 passing tests)
- Excellent documentation (16 docs)
- No critical issues identified

See `docs/APP_CLEANUP_SUMMARY.md` for complete analysis.

## Related Issues

Closes #[issue-number] - App Clean-Up, Polishing, and Preparation

title: Add LLM-based command interpreter with Gemini function calling
body:
Implements Google Gemini integration as alternative to rule-based pattern matching. LLM adapter interprets natural language commands via function calling and converts responses to existing Intent objects.

## Architecture

**Domain Layer** (`app/domain/services/agent_service.py`)
- Function schemas mapping ActionProvider capabilities (type_text, press_key, open_browser, open_url, search_on_page)
- System instruction defining "Xerife" persona (concise, productivity-focused)
- Function-to-CommandType mapping

**Infrastructure Layer** (`app/adapters/infrastructure/gemini_adapter.py`)
- Drop-in replacement for CommandInterpreter interface
- AsyncIO support via `asyncio.to_thread()` prevents voice loop blocking
- Handles clarification requests by invoking `VoiceProvider.speak()` when confidence is low
- Converts Gemini function calls to Intent objects

**Container** (`app/container.py`)
- `use_llm` flag toggles between rule-based and LLM modes
- Configuration via `GEMINI_API_KEY` and `GEMINI_MODEL` environment variables

## Usage

```python
from app.container import create_edge_container

# Enable LLM mode
container = create_edge_container(use_llm=True)
assistant = container.assistant_service
assistant.start()
```

The LLM interprets commands through function calling:
```
User: "xerife escreva meu nome"
→ Gemini calls type_text(text="meu nome")
→ Adapter converts to Intent(TYPE_TEXT, {"text": "meu nome"})
→ Existing IntentProcessor/AssistantService execute unchanged
```

## Implementation Notes

- Preserves hexagonal architecture: domain layer remains dependency-free
- Backward compatible: both modes coexist, selectable at runtime
- 25 new tests (11 AgentService, 14 LLMCommandAdapter), all existing tests pass
- No security vulnerabilities (CodeQL clean, dependency scan clean)

## Configuration

Add to `.env`:
```env
GEMINI_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-1.5-flash
```

See `LLM_INTEGRATION.md` for detailed guide.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Atue como Especialista em IA. Vamos substituir o CommandInterpreter atual (baseado em regras) por um LLMCommandAdapter usando a API do Google Gemini.
> No app/domain/services/, crie uma lógica de 'Agente' que utiliza Function Calling.
> O Agente deve conhecer as capacidades do ActionProvider (type_text, press_key, open_browser).
> Crie um novo Adapter em app/adapters/infrastructure/gemini_adapter.py. Ele deve:
> Usar google-generativeai.
> Converter a resposta da IA em um objeto Intent que o nosso IntentProcessor já entende.
> Utilize AsyncIO para que a chamada à API da IA não trave o loop de voz.
> Adicione uma instrução de sistema (System Instruction) para o modelo agir como 'Xerife', um assistente focado em produtividade e automação, sendo conciso e eficiente.
> Certifique-se de que, se a IA não tiver certeza do comando, ela peça clarificação via VoiceProvider.speak()."


</details>

title: Implement AI Gateway for intelligent LLM provider routing with cost optimization and auto-fallback
body:
Implements multi-provider LLM routing for Jarvis AssistantService with cost optimization (Groq default), context-based escalation (Gemini for >10k tokens/multimodal), and automatic rate-limit fallback.

## Core Implementation

**`AIGateway` (`app/adapters/infrastructure/ai_gateway.py`)**
- Token-based routing: Groq (default) → Gemini (>10k tokens)
- Multimodal detection → Gemini
- Rate limit detection → transparent provider fallback
- Module-level tokenizer caching (tiktoken)

**`GatewayLLMCommandAdapter` (`app/adapters/infrastructure/gateway_llm_adapter.py`)**
- Integrates AI Gateway with existing LLM command interpretation
- Maintains backward compatibility with `LLMCommandAdapter` interface
- Uses Groq for conversational responses, Gemini adapter for function calling

**Container integration (`app/container.py`)**
- Auto-initialization when API keys present
- Graceful degradation to rule-based interpreter
- Debug logging sanitized (removed API key exposure)

## Configuration

```python
# .env
GROQ_API_KEY=your_key
GROQ_MODEL=llama-3.1-70b-versatile
GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key
GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-flash-latest
```

## Usage

```python
# Automatic via Container
container = create_edge_container(use_ai_gateway=True)
assistant = container.assistant_service

# Direct usage
gateway = AIGateway(groq_api_key="...", gemini_api_key="...")
result = gateway.generate_completion(messages)
# Short payloads → Groq (fast, cheap)
# Large payloads (>10k tokens) → Gemini (high capacity)
# Rate limit → auto-fallback
```

## Routing Decision Tree

```
1. Multimodal? → Gemini
2. Tokens > 10k? → Gemini  
3. Groq available? → Groq
4. Fallback → Gemini
5. Rate limit hit → Switch provider
```

## Dependencies

- `groq>=0.4.0` - Groq API client
- `tiktoken>=0.5.0` - Token counting (fallback to char/4 approximation)

## Testing

- 14 new tests covering routing logic, fallback, token counting
- All existing tests passing (74 total)
- CodeQL: 0 vulnerabilities

title: Wigner function implementation from Fock state tensor
body: 1. Fix bugs in ``BosonicState.wigner`` function - Error when computing the Wigner function of a Gaussian state with ``ncom=1`` -Solution: Squeeze ``exp_imag`` along the last two dimensions to ensure correct broadcasting behavior. - Unnormalized Wigner function values - Solution: Apply an explicit renormalization factor A comparison with the reference implementation in QuTiP is as follows, 2. Add function ``fock_wigner`` for Wigner function implementation from Fock state tensor - Example ``` device = 'cpu' r=1 d=0 cutoff = 30 cir1 = dq.QumodeCircuit(nmode=1, init_state='vac', backend='fock', basis=False, cutoff=cutoff) cir1.s(0, r=r, theta=0) cir1.d(0, r=d) cir1.ps(0, np.pi/3, encode=True) cir1.to(torch.double) cir1.to(device) re = cir1(data=torch.tensor([[0.],[0.],[0.]], dtype=torch.double, device=device)) s = dq.FockState(state=re, basis=False) w = s.wigner(wire=0, xrange=10, prange=10, npoints=100, k=0) ```

title: Switch to self-hosted TrueNAS Scale architecture
body:
This PR shifts the project from a Vercel-hosted deployment model to a fully self-hosted Docker Compose setup designed for TrueNAS Scale.

### Changes Included:
1. **Architecture shift:** The Vue 3 SPA is now statically compiled during a multi-stage Docker build and served by the FastAPI backend on a single port (`8000`). This eliminates the need for separate frontend hosting like Vercel or Render.
2. **Backend Proxying & Database:** External calls to Overpass and OSRM have been migrated from the frontend to the Python backend to avoid client rate-limiting and allow storing results locally in a PostGIS database.
3. **Docker Compose Setup:** A complete `docker-compose.yml` is provided alongside a `.env.example` file. It orchestrates the API server, a PostGIS database, Redis, and Valhalla routing.
4. **Database Configuration:** Uses `postgresql+asyncpg` for asynchronous database connections. Lifecycle events handle creating database metadata on startup.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [15512511679198520980](https://jules.google.com/task/15512511679198520980) started by @USBKayble*

title: Fix Python 2.7 virtualenv PermissionError and Passenger crash-loop lock contention
body:
Two cPanel deployment failures: (1) virtualenv creation fails with `PermissionError` on CloudLinux Python 2.7 `.pyc` files; (2) any startup exception puts Passenger into a crash-restart loop, blocking cPanel's app management lock and causing `Can't acquire lock for app: FakeNTP-cpanel` on operations like **Run Script**.

## Python version enforcement

- `passenger_wsgi.py` / `cli.py` — fail-fast guard before any imports:
  ```python
  if sys.version_info < (3, 8):
      raise RuntimeError("FakeNTP requires Python 3.8 or later. ...")
  ```
- `pyproject.toml` — added `requires-python = ">=3.8"` under a `[project]` section

## Passenger crash-loop prevention

Wraps `create_app()` in `passenger_wsgi.py` with a `try/except` that keeps Passenger alive on any startup failure by serving a `500` fallback instead of crashing:

```python
try:
    from fakentp.app import create_app
    application = create_app()
except Exception as _exc:
    _log.critical("FakeNTP failed to initialize: %s", _exc, exc_info=True)
    _msg = ("FakeNTP failed to start: {}\n"
            "Check the server error log for details.".format(_exc)).encode()

    def application(environ, start_response):
        start_response("500 Internal Server Error", [...])
        return [_msg]
```

This stops the restart loop so cPanel can acquire the management lock for **Run Script** / package installs even when the app is misconfigured.

## Documentation

Added a **Troubleshooting** section to `README.md` covering both errors with cause and fix steps.

title: Fix GC% calculation to exclude ambiguous bases + add preprocessing pipeline
body:
## Problem

GC% calculation incorrectly included N's (ambiguous bases) in denominator, violating NCBI/Ensembl/UCSC genomic standards. Sequence `ATCGNNNN` returned 25% instead of correct 50%.

## Changes

### Core Fix: GC% Calculation
- **Formula corrected**: `(G+C) / len(seq)` → `(G+C) / (A+T+G+C)`
- **Performance**: Refactored to O(n) single-pass via shared `_count_bases()` helper
- **Applies to**: `calc_gc_content()`, `calc_at_content()` in `detectors_utils.py`

```python
# Before: Incorrect - includes N's
def calc_gc_content(seq):
    gc = sum(1 for c in seq if c in {'G', 'C'})
    return (gc / len(seq)) * 100  # Wrong denominator

# After: Correct - excludes N's
def calc_gc_content(seq):
    a, t, g, c = _count_bases(seq)  # Single pass
    valid = a + t + g + c
    return ((g + c) / valid) * 100 if valid else 0.0
```

### Preprocessing Pipeline
- **Module**: `Utilities/sequence_preprocessor.py`
- **7-step workflow**: Normalization → FASTA parsing → character analysis → GC calculation → validation
- **Returns**: `PreprocessingResult` dataclass with sequence stats, GC%, validation status, error positions
- **IUPAC-aware**: Distinguishes valid (ATGCN), ambiguous (RYKMSWBDHV), and invalid characters

### Integration
- **NonBScanner**: Added opt-in `use_preprocessing` parameter (default: `False` for backward compat)
- **Safety module**: New `generate_sequence_quality_report()` for batch validation

### Testing & Docs
- **Tests**: +33 new tests covering GC calculation, FASTA parsing, validation (105 total, all passing)
- **Docs**: New `docs/SEQUENCE_PREPROCESSING.md` with workflow diagram, scientific basis, API reference

## Impact

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| GC% accuracy | ❌ Incorrect for sequences with N's | ✅ NCBI/Ensembl standard |
| Time complexity | O(4n) | O(n) |
| Breaking changes | N/A | None (opt-in) |

**Example**: Sequence with 50% N content now reports 25 percentage points higher GC% (correctly excludes N's from denominator).

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## 🎯 Objective

Implement a **scientifically rigorous, gold-standard sequence preprocessing pipeline** for genomic DNA analysis that ensures data quality and accuracy before motif detection.

## 📋 Current Issues

### 1. **Incorrect GC% Calculation**
**Current Implementation:**
```python
# Utilities/detectors_utils.py (Line 40-54)
def calc_gc_content(seq: str) -> float:
    if not seq: return 0.0
    gc_count = sum(1 for c in seq if c in {'G', 'C', 'g', 'c'})
    return (gc_count / len(seq)) * 100  # ← WRONG: Uses total length (includes N, gaps)
```

**Problem:** 
- Denominator includes **N's** (ambiguous bases) and potentially **invalid characters**
- For sequence `ATCGNNNATCG` (11 chars), reports GC% = 18.18% instead of correct 25%
- Violates genomic analysis standard: **GC% = (G+C) / (A+T+G+C) × 100**

### 2. **Missing Comprehensive Preprocessing**
Current workflow:
```python
# Utilities/nonbscanner.py (Line 155-157)
sequence = sequence.upper().strip()
is_valid, msg = validate_sequence(sequence)  # Only checks ATCGN
```

**Missing Steps:**
- ❌ No explicit FASTA header removal (handled externally)
- ❌ No multi-line sequence concatenation verification
- ❌ No detailed character counting (A, T, G, C, N, invalid)
- ❌ No positional reporting for invalid characters
- ❌ No pre-analysis sequence statistics

### 3. **Incomplete Validation Reporting**
```python
# Utilities/safety.py (Line 125-156)
def validate_sequence_input(sequences: List[str], min_length: int = 10) -> tuple:
    # Only reports: empty, too short, invalid characters
    # Does NOT report: character counts, N positions, GC%, etc.
```

## ✅ Required Implementation

### **New Module: `Utilities/sequence_preprocessor.py`**

Create a comprehensive preprocessing module with these functions:

#### 1. **`preprocess_sequence(raw_input: str) -> PreprocessingResult`**

**Workflow:**
```python
class PreprocessingResult:
    """Data class for preprocessing results."""
    header: str                          # FASTA header (without '>')
    sequence: str                        # Clean, single-line, uppercase sequence
    length: int                          # Total sequence length
    valid_bases: int                     # Count of A+T+G+C only
    character_counts: Dict[str, int]     # {'A': 1234, 'T': 1456, 'G': 987, 'C': 876, 'N': 45}
    invalid_characters: Dict[str, List[int]]  # {'X': [123, 456], '1': [789]}
    gc_percentage: float                 # (G+C) / (A+T+G+C) × 100
    at_percentage: float                 # (A+T) / (A+T+G+C) × 100
    gc_balance: str                      # "Balanced" | "GC-rich" | "AT-rich"
    validation_status: str               # "valid" | "warning" | "error"
    warnings: List[str]                  # Warning messages
    errors: List[str]                    # Error messages
```

**Processing Steps:**
1. **Uppercase normalization**: `raw_input.upper()`
2. **FASTA header extraction**: Parse lines starting with `>`, extract first header
3. **Multi-line concatenation**: Join all non-header lines into single string
4. **Character analysis**:
   - Count: A, T, G, C separately
   - Count: N (ambiguous base)
   - Detect: Invalid characters (non-IUPAC codes)
   - Report: Positions of first 10 invalid characters per type
5. **Length calculation**: 
   - `total_length = len(sequence)`
   - `valid_bases = count(A) + count(T) + count(G) + count(C)`
6. **GC percentage (CORRECT)**:
   ```python
   if valid_bases == 0:
       raise ValueError("No valid ATGC bases found")
   gc_percentage = ((g_count + c_count) / valid_bases) * 100
   at_percentage = ((a_count + t_count) / valid_bases) * 100
   ```
7. **GC Balance classification**:
   - GC% > 60%: "GC-rich"
   - GC% < 40%: "AT-rich"
   - Otherwise: "Balanced"
8. **Validation**:
   - **Warnings** if N's present (>0%)
   - **Errors** if invalid characters detected
   - **Status**: "valid" (no issues) | "warning" (has N's) | "error" (invalid chars)

#### 2. **`fix_gc_calculation()` - Update Existing Function**

**File**: `Utilities/detectors_utils.py`

**Current (Line 40-54):**
```python
def calc_gc_content(seq: str) -> float:
    if not seq: return 0.0
    gc_count = sum(1 for c in seq if c in {'G', 'C', 'g', 'c'})
    return (gc_count / len(seq)) * 100  # ← FIX THIS
```

**Replace with:**
```python
def calc_gc_content(seq: str) -> float:
    """
    Calculate GC content percentage using genomic standard formula.
    
    Formula: GC% = (G + C) / (A + T + G + C) × 100
    
    Args:
        seq: DNA sequence string (case-insensitive)
        
    Returns:
        GC content as percentage (0-100), or 0.0 if no valid ATGC bases
        
    Scientific Basis:
        - Excludes ambiguous bases (N, R, Y, etc.) from denominator
        - Only counts definitive nucleotides (A, T, G, C)
        - Standard used in genomics (NCBI, Ensembl, UCSC Genome Browser)
        
    Example:
        >>> calc_gc_content("ATCG")
        50.0
        >>> calc_gc_content("ATCGNNNN")  # N's excluded
        50.0  #...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
>

title: Add README_SETUP.md: Windows public deployment + iOS Shortcuts integration guide
body:
Adds `README_SETUP.md` — a comprehensive setup guide derived directly from the actual source files (`.env.example`, `config.py`, `scripts/gmail_auth.py`, endpoint schemas).

## Part 1 — Windows Setup for Public Usage
- **Prerequisites** — Python 3.12+, catch-all domain concept, Google Cloud project
- **Env config** — full table of every `.env` variable with must-change flags; secure token generation via `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)`
- **Gmail OAuth** — complete Cloud Console walkthrough: project → Gmail API → Desktop OAuth creds → `credentials.json` → `python scripts/gmail_auth.py` → `token.json`
- **Catch-all domain** — Cloudflare Email Routing, Namecheap, and generic providers
- **Public exposure** — Cloudflare Tunnel (recommended), ngrok, nginx+Certbot on VPS, PaaS (Railway/Render/Fly.io)
- **Windows service** — NSSM install/configure/start so the server survives reboots
- **PostgreSQL upgrade** — install, create DB/user, swap `DATABASE_URL`, `alembic upgrade head`
- **Security checklist** — 9 items (ADMIN_TOKEN rotation, HTTPS enforcement, firewall, token.json hygiene, etc.)

## Part 2 — iOS Shortcuts Integration
- 8 exact Shortcut actions: POST session → extract `session_id`/`client_token`/`alias` → show alias → 30-iteration polling loop with `extracted`/`expired`/`cancelled` branching → 5 s wait → timeout alert
- Variable reference table, cancel-button pattern (calls `POST .../cancel`), and troubleshooting matrix

## Part 3 — End-to-End Verification
- 7-step PowerShell curl walkthrough from server start through code extraction confirming all components work together

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

Use ALL of the following actual source files from the repo to ensure accuracy:

---

## Source files for reference

- `admin_token`: `"change-me"` (warns if left at default)
- `rate_limit_requests`: `30`
- `rate_limit_window_seconds`: `60`
- `retention_enabled`: `false`
- `retention_redact_body`: `true`
- `retention_days`: `7`
- `recycle_enabled`: `false`
- `host`: `"0.0.0.0"`
- `port`: `8000`
- `log_level`: `"INFO"`

### Gmail OAuth scopes (from `app/integrations/gmail/auth.py`)
```python
SCOPES = [
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly",
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify",
]
```

The auth flow uses `InstalledAppFlow.from_client_secrets_file` and `run_local_server(port=0)`.

### `scripts/gmail_auth.py` — the OAuth setup script
Run via `python scripts/gmail_auth.py`. Requires `credentials.json` in project root.

### `Dockerfile`
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /app
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
RUN adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' appuser && chown -R appuser /app
USER appuser
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

### `docker-compose.yml`
```yaml
version: "3.9"
services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: "postgresql+asyncpg://shortcut:shortcut@db:5432/shortcut_api"
    volumes:
      - ./credentials.json:/app/credentials.json:ro
      - ./token.json:/app/token.json
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: shortcut
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: shortcut
      POSTGRES_DB: shortcut_api
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U shortcut -d shortcut_api"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
    restart: unless-stopped
volumes:
  pgdata:
```

### API endpoints used by the Shortcut (from `app/api/v1/endpoints/sessions.py`)
- `POST /api/v1/sessions` — creates session (no auth), returns `session_id`, `client_token`, `alias`, `expires_at`, `status`
- `GET /api/v1/sessions/{se...

</details>

title: fix: remove Codex sandbox workarounds (Phase 1 cleanup)
body:
## Summary

- **Issue 5**: `tests/conftest.py` — removed `_noop_cleanup` monkey-patching of `pytest._pytest.pathlib/tmpdir` internals and the custom `tmp_path` fixture that redirected temp dirs to repo-local `test_artifacts/`. Both were Codex sandbox workarounds that don't belong in the repo. Tests now use pytest's built-in `tmp_path`.
- **Issue 8**: `pytest.ini` — removed `-p no:cacheprovider` from `addopts`. This was a sandbox-only flag (prevents `.pytest_cache/` writes, which the Codex sandbox blocked). Not needed in normal environments.

## Issues audited but already resolved

| # | Issue | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `.gitignore` | Already exists and comprehensive |
| 2 | `requirements.txt` | Already exists |
| 3 | `README.md` | Already exists |
| 4 | `run_pipeline.py` hardcoded path | Already uses `Path(__file__).parent` (local) |
| 6 | `pytest-cache-files-*` dirs | Already in `.gitignore`, not committed |
| 7 | `config.json.example` in repo root | Already exists |
| 9 | `scripts/verify_setup.py` | Already exists |

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest -q` → **19 passed** (no failures after removing sandbox shims)
- [x] `python run_pipeline.py --dry-run` → exit 0

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Migrate project to uv and add handoff docs
body:
## Summary
- add `pyproject.toml` with runtime dependencies and a dev dependency group
- add `uv.lock` for reproducible installs
- add `.python-version` and `.gitignore` for local environment hygiene
- add `HANDOFF.md` and update `README.md` with current project status

## How to use
- `uv sync --dev`
- `uv run python nummer_gen_plb_2021.py`
- `uv run pytest -q`

## Notes
- this PR contains the uv/docs commit that was created after PR #2 had already been merged

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Primarily tooling and documentation changes; no application logic is modified, though the new lockfile may affect local environments if dependency versions differ from prior setups.
> 
> **Overview**
> Migrates the repo to `uv` for environment/dependency management by adding `pyproject.toml` (Python >=3.12, runtime + dev deps) and committing a generated `uv.lock` for reproducible installs.
> 
> Adds developer-facing docs via a new `HANDOFF.md` (setup/run instructions + pickle safety note) and a significantly expanded `README.md` describing the project and clarifying that the active implementation has moved elsewhere. Also introduces a basic `.gitignore` and pins the interpreter with `.python-version`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit c08f5edd7d4161079b6df505ad4c19fcce41eca7. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: chore(deps): Bump requests from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0 in the uv group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the uv group with 1 update in the / directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Updates `requests` from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

title: feat: 반복거래 등록 UX 개선 — 정기거래 뱃지 + next_due_date 보정
body:
## Summary
- 기존 거래에서 반복거래 등록 시 즉시 "실행 대기" 알림이 뜨는 UX 문제 수정
- Expense/Income에 `recurring_transaction_id` FK 추가하여 정기거래 연결
- `source_id` 파라미터로 원본 거래 연결 + `next_due_date` 다음 주기 보정
- TransactionItem 뱃지를 `raw_input` 기반에서 FK 기반으로 통합
- LLM JSONDecodeError except 순서 버그 수정 (Sentry 카카오 webhook 에러)

## Changes
### 백엔드
- Expense/Income 모델에 `recurring_transaction_id` nullable FK (ondelete=SET NULL) + Alembic 마이그레이션
- `POST /api/recurring`에 `source_id` 처리: 원본 거래 FK 연결 + household 소유권 검증
- `execute_recurring()`에서 생성 거래에 `recurring_transaction_id` 자동 설정
- LLM `parse_expense`의 except 순서 수정 (JSONDecodeError → ValueError)

### 프론트엔드
- ExpenseDetail/IncomeDetail: `recurring_transaction_id` 있으면 "반복거래 등록" 버튼 숨김
- RegisterRecurringModal: `sourceId` prop → API에 `source_id` 전달
- TransactionItem: `rawInput` 기반 뱃지 → `recurringTransactionId` FK 기반으로 교체
- 타입/fixtures 업데이트

## Test plan
- [x] 백엔드 전체 테스트 통과 (1111 passed)
- [x] 프론트엔드 전체 테스트 통과 (726 passed)
- [x] 프론트엔드 린트 + 빌드 통과
- [ ] source_id로 반복거래 등록 시 원본 거래에 FK 연결 확인
- [ ] 등록 후 첫 화면에 pending 알림 안 뜨는지 확인
- [ ] 연결된 거래에 "정기" 뱃지 표시 확인
- [ ] 카카오톡 봇 자연어 입력 시 JSON 파싱 실패 재시도 정상 동작 확인

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add Docker, docker-compose, systemd service, and Nginx config for deployment
body:
### Motivation
- Provide containerized deployment for the LineageLens web UI to standardize runtime and packaging.
- Enable simple orchestration on hosts via a systemd unit that runs `docker compose` to manage the service lifecycle.
- Supply an example `nginx` reverse-proxy configuration for hosting the application behind a domain and exposing it on port 80.

### Description
- Add `Dockerfile` based on `python:3.11-slim` that sets `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE`, `PYTHONUNBUFFERED`, and `PYTHONPATH=/app/src`, copies source and metadata, installs the package with `pip`, exposes port `8000`, and runs `python -m lineagelens.ui.app`.
- Add `docker-compose.yml` declaring a `lineagelens` service that builds the image, restarts `unless-stopped`, and maps `127.0.0.1:8000:8000`.
- Add a systemd unit at `deploy/lineagelens.service` which runs `/usr/bin/docker compose up -d --build` on start and `/usr/bin/docker compose down` on stop as a oneshot service rooted at `/opt/lineagelens`.
- Add `deploy/nginx/lineagelens.conf` as an example Nginx server block that proxies requests to `http://127.0.0.1:8000` with common proxy headers and timeouts and a `client_max_body_size` limit.

### Testing
- No automated tests were run for these deployment configuration changes.

------

title: 2to3 refactoring minitwit.py
Fix deployment: update dependencies for Python 3.12+ compatibility
body:
Old pinned versions (numpy==1.24.0, scikit-learn==1.2) depend on distutils which was removed in Python 3.12. Use minimum version constraints instead of exact pins, and add .python-version to pin Python 3.12 on Streamlit Cloud.

title: Implement Rust BMAP library and bmapctl CLI
body:
## Summary

- Full Rust implementation of the BMAP protocol library (`bmap` crate)
- Same layered architecture as Python: protocol → transport → connection → CLI
- `bmapctl` binary — single 599KB native executable
- `Transport` trait enables mock testing without Bluetooth hardware
- QC Ultra 2 fully supported, QC35 stubbed
- Device configs are data structs with `Option<Addr>` fields (compile-time feature checking)

## Verified against real hardware

```
$ cargo run -- status
  Battery      90%
  Mode         immersion
  CNC          ░░░░░░░░░░ 0/10
  EQ           +0/+0/+0 (bass/mid/treble)
  Name         Fargo
  FW           8.2.20+g34cf029
  Sidetone     medium
  Multipoint   on
  AutoPause    on
  Prompts      off (US English)
```

## Test plan

- [x] `make rust-test` — 41 tests pass (protocol, parsers, devices, connection with mock)
- [x] `make python-test` — 91 tests still pass
- [x] `cargo run -- status/battery/buttons/eq` — verified against QC Ultra 2
- [x] `cargo build --release` — 599KB static binary

title: feat: Next.js 15 + FastAPI web redesign (Figma design 1:1)
body:
## Summary

- Migrates UI from PySide6 to **Next.js 15 + Tauri 2 + Python FastAPI**, matching the Figma Make design pixel-perfect
- 3-panel layout: dark sidebar (`#1a1f36`), Kanban board with drag-and-drop (`@dnd-kit`), AI chat panel with SSE streaming
- Framer Motion animations, Lucide icons, Tailwind CSS — all matching the Figma color spec exactly
- Python `core/` backend (tasks_manager, file_tools, models) preserved unchanged; `tasks.json` format 100% compatible

## Key Technical Details

- **SSR fix**: `page.tsx` → `ClientShell.tsx` (`"use client"` + `dynamic(..., {ssr:false})`) → `AppClient.tsx` to prevent browser APIs running server-side
- **Next.js 15.3.0 bug**: `DevOverlay` accessed `localStorage` during SSR — downgraded to `15.2.4`
- **FastAPI CORS**: `allow_origins=["*"]` with `allow_credentials=False` for browser compatibility
- **SSE confirm flow**: `asyncio.Event` pattern — stream pauses via `await event.wait()`, user approves in frontend, event is set

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm run dev` starts on any port without 500 errors
- [ ] Open project via sidebar → Kanban populates from `tasks.json`
- [ ] Settings dialog auto-opens on first run (no API key)
- [ ] Task cards show correct priority badges and can be dragged between columns
- [ ] Chat panel sends messages and streams SSE responses from FastAPI

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: 🎨 Palette: Add validation to interactive prompts
body:

title: 🎨 Palette: Interactive 'Run Now' after Dry Run
body:
## 🎨 Palette: Interactive 'Run Now' after Dry Run

**💡 What:**
Added a small but delightful interaction: after a successful dry run, the tool now asks "🚀 Ready to launch? Press [Enter] to run now...".

**🎯 Why:**
Previously, users had to copy-paste the suggested command manually to proceed with the live sync. This friction is now removed for interactive sessions.

**✨ UX Details:**
- **Seamless:** Pressing Enter immediately restarts the tool in live mode.
- **Smart:** It preserves all your arguments (profiles, folder URLs, etc.) automatically.
- **Secure:** If you entered your API token interactively, it is securely passed to the new process so you don't have to type it again.
- **Safe:** Only appears in interactive terminals (TTY), so it won't block CI/CD pipelines.

**♿ Accessibility:**
- Keyboard-friendly (Enter to confirm, Ctrl+C to cancel).
- Visual feedback with colors (if supported) or plain text.
