title: Fix: Downgrade Python to 3.12 for pydantic compatibility
body:
## Problem

The build fails during dependency installation because Railpack defaults to Python 3.13, which is incompatible with pydantic-core 2.18.4 — a transitive dependency of aiogram 3.7.0. PyO3, used to build pydantic-core's native extension, only supports up to Python 3.12, producing the error: "the configured Python interpreter version (3.13) is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version (3.12)".

## Solution

Added a railpack.toml at the repository root with `python = "3.12"` under the `[build]` section. This explicitly instructs Railpack to use Python 3.12 instead of the default 3.13, which is fully compatible with pydantic-core and all other aiogram dependencies.

### Changes
- **Created** `railpack.toml`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: Fix Stripchat model preview images and hover fanart
body:
### Motivation
- Model hover previews on Stripchat pages were missing images because the API payloads use varying fields and sometimes protocol-relative or relative image URLs.
- The listing code also didn't always expose the resolved image as `fanart`, so hover/preview panels could show an empty image.

### Description
- Added `_normalize_model_image_url` to canonicalize protocol-relative and relative image URLs and return a safe absolute URL. 
- Added `_model_screenshot` to pick the best available preview field from the model payload or fall back to the snapshot-based thumbnail URL generated from `snapshotTimestamp` and `id`.
- Replaced inline thumbnail construction in `List`, `List2`, and `List3` with calls to `_model_screenshot` and pass the resolved value as both the item thumbnail and the `fanart` argument to `site.add_download_link` for consistent hover previews.
- Updated `tests/sites/test_stripchat.py` to assert that the list items include both `iconimage` and `fanart` and added a test for the snapshot fallback behavior.

### Testing
- Ran `pytest -q tests/sites/test_stripchat.py` and all tests passed (`9 passed`).
- The changes were exercised by unit tests that validate API parsing, thumbnail selection, fanart assignment, and the snapshot fallback logic.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_699f928b622483279b3e64f47e2e34b5)

title: Refactor dialog architecture: Remove indirect callbacks and timeouts, add direct typed methods
body:
Dialogs were timing out after 30 seconds, interrupting user workflows with "GUI prompt timed out" errors. Additionally, the dialog architecture used an indirect callback pattern with string-based type switching that made the code hard to understand and maintain.

## Changes

**Removed dialog timeout**
- Changed `event.wait(timeout=30.0)` to `event.wait(timeout=None)` in `handle_gui_prompt_threadsafe()`
- Dialogs now wait indefinitely for user interaction

**Added error handling**
- Wrapped `_handle_gui_prompt_on_main_thread()` in try-finally block
- Ensures `event.set()` always called to prevent deadlocks

**Refactored dialog architecture**
- Removed indirect callback pattern with `request_input(gui_type='...', gui_options={...})`
- Added direct typed methods to Utils class:
  - `show_info_dialog(title, message)` - Info dialogs with OK button
  - `show_question_dialog(title, message, default, warning)` - Yes/No questions
  - `show_choice_dialog(title, message, choices, default_value, warning, note)` - Dropdown selections
  - `show_input_dialog(title, message, placeholder)` - Text input
- Changed from `gui_callback` to `gui_handler` pattern
- Auto-fallback to CLI when GUI not available

**Code cleanup**
- Removed unused `QFileDialog` import from `upload_page.py`
- Migrated all dialogs in `kext_maestro.py` (7 dialogs)
- Migrated all dialogs in `hardware_customizer.py` (2 dialogs)

## Architecture Improvement

**Before (messy indirect pattern):**
```python
gui_options = {
    'title': 'Select WiFi Kext',
    'message': 'Choose kext...',
    'choices': [...],
    'default': '1',
    'warning': 'Warning text'
}
result = utils.request_input("Select...", gui_type='choice', gui_options=gui_options)
```

**After (clean direct methods):**
```python
result = utils.show_choice_dialog(
    'Select WiFi Kext',
    'Choose kext...',
    choices=[...],
    default_value='1',
    warning='Warning text'
)
```

## Dialog Inventory

**Custom dialogs** (11 in `custom_dialogs.py`): Input, Choice, Question, Info, SMBIOS, Kexts, ACPI Patches, macOS Version, WiFi Network Count, Codec Layout, Before Using EFI

**File dialogs** (3 using native `QFileDialog`): Hardware report selection, ACPI folder selection, Console log saving

## Benefits

✅ Much cleaner, more maintainable code  
✅ Type-safe parameters with IDE autocomplete  
✅ Easy to understand what dialog will show  
✅ Consistent behavior in GUI and CLI  
✅ No more string-based type dispatching  
✅ No more annoying timeouts

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> go trough the whole codebase and find other Dialog's that are not in the custom_dialogs.py because we want to have a uniform codebase and good looking Dialog's. with amazing styling and good UI/UX experiance for the user.

body:
## Summary

- Dev server (`flash run`) QB routes now reject `{"input": {}}` with 422, matching RunPod platform behavior which silently times out on empty input
- Deployed handler templates (`handler_generator.py`) return error dict for empty/null input (defense-in-depth)

- Fixes pre-existing bug where `saveEndpoint` included a fresh template when updating an endpoint with an existing `templateId`, causing spurious releases

- [ ] Verify `flash build` generates handlers with empty-input validation guard
- [ ] Verify `make quality-check` passes (2512 tests, 85.59% coverage)

Resolves AE-2744

title: fix: Prevent SystemExit race condition during graceful shutdown (Issue #1314)
body:
## Summary

Fixes race condition where pressing Ctrl+C causes `SystemExit` exception in atexit callback, producing an ugly stack trace.

## Problem

When users press Ctrl+C:
1. Signal handler calls `sys.exit(0)`
2. atexit triggers `_cleanup_on_exit` callback
3. Stop hook tries to read from stdin via `sys.stdin.read()`
4. The pending `sys.exit(0)` interrupts stdin read → **SystemExit exception**

**Before Fix:**
```
^C
Received interrupt signal. Shutting down...
Exception ignored in atexit callback: <bound method ClaudeLauncher._cleanup_on_exit...>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
  File ".../hook_processor.py", line 145, in read_input
    raw_input = sys.stdin.read()
SystemExit: 0
```

## Solution

Add shutdown flag to skip stdin reads during interrupt:

1. **Signal Handler**: Set `AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS=1` before `sys.exit(0)`
2. **Hook Processor**: Check flag in `read_input()`, return `{}` if shutdown in progress
3. **Result**: No stdin read attempted, no SystemExit exception

**After Fix:**
```
^C
Received interrupt signal. Shutting down...
(Clean exit, no exception)
```

## Changes

**Files Modified:** 3
- `src/amplihack/launcher/core.py` - Set shutdown flag in signal handler
- `.claude/tools/amplihack/hooks/hook_processor.py` - Check flag, skip stdin
- `tests/unit/test_hook_processor_run.py` - Add 2 unit tests

**Lines Changed:** +71 insertions

## Testing

- ✅ Pre-commit hooks passed

title: fix: Set shutdown flag in atexit to prevent stop hook hang and traceback
body:
## Summary

Fixes #1320 - Stop hook hangs on exit and shows Ctrl-C traceback despite previous fix attempt.

## Problem

When Claude exits normally, the stop hook hangs waiting for stdin input. When user presses Ctrl-C, a SystemExit traceback appears even though there was a previous fix to prevent this.

**Error observed**:
```
Claude session completed
^C
Received interrupt signal. Shutting down...
Exception ignored in atexit callback:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "...core.py", line 847, in _cleanup_on_exit
    execute_stop_hook()
  ...
  File "...hook_processor.py", line 145, in read_input
    raw_input = sys.stdin.read()  # <-- HANGS HERE
  File "...core.py", line 608, in signal_handler
    sys.exit(0)
SystemExit: 0
```

## Root Cause

The previous fix was **incomplete**. The `AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS` flag check exists in `hook_processor.py` (lines 146-148), but the flag is only set in ONE of TWO places:

1. ✅ `signal_handler()` (core.py:607) - **DOES** set the flag
2. ❌ `_cleanup_on_exit()` (core.py:845) - **DOES NOT** set the flag ← **THE BUG**

When Claude exits normally (not via signal), `_cleanup_on_exit()` runs from atexit but never sets the flag, so the stop hook tries to read from stdin and hangs.

## Solution

Set `os.environ["AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS"] = "1"` in `_cleanup_on_exit()` BEFORE calling `execute_stop_hook()`.

**One-line fix** (core.py:844):
```python
def _cleanup_on_exit(self):
    try:
        # ADD THIS LINE:
        os.environ["AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS"] = "1"
        
        from amplihack.hooks.manager import execute_stop_hook
        execute_stop_hook()
    except Exception:
        pass
```

## Changes

- **Modified**: `src/amplihack/launcher/core.py` (1 line added)
  - Added flag setting in `_cleanup_on_exit()` before stop hook execution

## Testing

**Verification Test**:
```
✓ Fix verified: AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS flag is set in _cleanup_on_exit
✓ Fix uses os.environ to set the flag
✓ All verification checks passed!
```

**Expected Behavior After Fix**:
1. Claude exits normally → atexit runs
2. `_cleanup_on_exit()` sets AMPLIHACK_SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS=1
3. Stop hook checks flag → skips stdin read
4. Clean exit with no hang, no traceback ✓

## Impact

- Affects every user on every session exit
- Fixes annoying hang and ugly traceback
- Improves user experience significantly

## Philosophy Compliance

- ✅ **Ruthless Simplicity**: One-line fix
- ✅ **Zero-BS**: Complete fix, no stub or partial solution  
- ✅ **Clear Intent**: Comment explains why flag is needed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add FastAPI weather backend with multi-source aggregation
body:
No backend existed in the repo. This adds a production-ready Python/FastAPI service that aggregates weather data from OpenWeatherMap and Open-Meteo, normalizes both sources to a common schema, and merges values via weighted averages for improved accuracy.

## Structure

- **`backend/main.py`** – FastAPI app; `/health`, `/geocode`, `/weather` endpoints with CORS enabled
- **`backend/weather_service.py`** – All aggregation logic: concurrent fetching, weighted merging, WMO code mapping, moto suitability scoring
- **`backend/requirements.txt`** – `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `httpx`, `python-dotenv`, `pydantic`
- **`backend/.env.example`** – Env var template (`OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY`, `DEFAULT_CITY`, `PORT`)
- **`backend/README.md`** – Endpoint docs, response schema, deployment guide
- **`backend/tests.py`** – 17 offline unit tests covering helpers, merging logic, and scoring

## Key design points

- `/weather` accepts `city=`, `lat=`+`lon=`, or defaults to `Bucharest`
- Both sources fetched concurrently via `asyncio.gather`; OWM failures degrade gracefully (Open-Meteo always available, no key)
- **Moto score** (0–100) factors precipitation, wind gusts, feels-like temp, and weather codes → label: `IDEAL / OK / ACCEPTABIL / RISCANT / EVITĂ`
- Response includes `current`, `daily` (7-day), and `hourly` (168h) blocks

```
GET /weather?city=Cluj-Napoca
→ { city, latitude, longitude, timezone, current: { temperature, moto_score, moto_label, sources, … }, daily: […], hourly: […] }
```

Deploy with: `uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT`

> 2. Aggregate weather data by polling multiple APIs (beginning with OpenWeatherMap, but designed to incorporate more APIs in the future).
> 3. Compute and provide accurate weather insights, such as averages or trends, by normalizing and combining data from the connected APIs.
> 4. Expose endpoints allowing the frontend to query current weather, weekly forecasts, and statistics for any city of interest.

title: Feature/architecture implementation
body:
이 풀 리퀘스트(PR)는 시스템의 각 주요 레이어(API, Auth, Data, Frontend, Infra, Output, Prompt, Workflow)에 대한 포괄적인 아키텍처 가이드를 `.claude/skills/arch-*.md` 파일 형태로 추가합니다. 이 가이드들은 각 영역별 구현 표준, 파일 구조 및 핵심 디자인 패턴을 제공하여 아키텍처의 일관성을 유지하고 팀원들의 온보딩 과정을 명확하게 합니다. 또한, 기존 문서와 설정 파일에서 Next.js 대신 Vite + React + Tailwind CSS 스택을 사용하도록 일관되게 업데이트하고 프론트엔드 파일 구조를 명확히 했습니다.

**주요 변경 사항:**

---

**아키텍처 스킬 문서 추가:**

* `.claude/skills/arch-api/SKILL.md` 추가: REST API (FastAPI) 구현 가이드. 엔드포인트 목록, 에러 핸들링, 파일 구조 및 필수 컨벤션 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-auth/SKILL.md` 추가: OAuth, JWT 및 API Key 인증 시스템 가이드. 인증 흐름, 보안, 프론트엔드 통합 및 파일 레이아웃 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-data/SKILL.md` 추가: 데이터 모델 및 데이터베이스 스키마 가이드. Pydantic 모델, PostgreSQL 활용 및 파일 조직화 방법 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-frontend/SKILL.md` 추가: 프론트엔드 (Vite + React + Tailwind CSS) 구현 가이드. 페이지/컴포넌트 구조, 인증, i18n 및 컨벤션 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-infra/SKILL.md` 추가: 인프라 및 Docker Compose 설정 가이드. 서비스 개요, 환경 변수 및 배포 규칙 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-output/SKILL.md` 추가: 인터뷰 스크립트 출력 규격, 질문 구조 및 출력 규칙 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-prompt/SKILL.md` 추가: LLM 프롬프트 엔지니어링 가이드. 프롬프트 유형, 스택 및 구현 패턴 포함.
* `.claude/skills/arch-workflow/SKILL.md` 추가: Temporal 워크플로우 파이프라인 가이드. 단계별 분석, 체크포인트 및 액션 패턴 포함.

---

**프론트엔드 스택 및 구조 업데이트:**

* `CLAUDE.md` 및 `docs/architecture/01-overview.md` 내의 모든 "Next.js" 참조를 "Vite + React + Tailwind CSS"로 변경하고, 파일 구조를 `frontend/src/app/`에서 `frontend/src/pages/`로 업데이트했습니다.
* 새로운 스택 및 키워드에 맞춰 `CLAUDE.md`의 `react_frontend` 스킬 활성화 조건을 업데이트했습니다.
* `.claude/skills/implement/SKILL.md`의 React 컴포넌트 구현 섹션에서 Next.js 대신 "Vite + React + Tailwind CSS"를 사용하도록 수정했습니다.

---

**입출력 모델 확장:**

* `docs/architecture/01-overview.md`에 선택적 입력 타입으로 "커버레터"를 추가하고 "경험 레벨" 옵션을 확장했습니다.

---

title: Add CoreDNS DNS allowlist resolver to close DNS exfiltration channel
body:
## Summary

- Adds a CoreDNS service that intercepts all DNS traffic in the shared network namespace and only resolves domains in `allow-list.yaml`, returning `REFUSED` for everything else — closing the DNS exfiltration channel that previously bypassed the mitmproxy allowlist
- Derives the CoreDNS zone allowlist from the same `allow-list.yaml` used by mitmproxy at build time, keeping HTTP and DNS policy in sync with no separate list to maintain
- Extends `entrypoint.sh` with DNAT rules redirecting all port-53 traffic to CoreDNS, explicit blocks for DoT (853), mDNS (5353), DoT-alt (8853), and a blanket `ip6tables OUTPUT REJECT` for IPv6

## Key files

- `infra/coredns/Dockerfile` — multi-stage build: Python Alpine generates the Corefile, CoreDNS image bakes it in
- `infra/coredns/generate-corefile.py` — reads `allow-list.yaml`, emits per-zone `forward` blocks with a catch-all `REFUSED` default
- `infra/coredns/test_generate_corefile.py` — 46 tests, 100% coverage, verified in the `python:3.12-alpine` build container
- `infra/mitmproxy/entrypoint.sh` — DNAT + port blocks + IPv6 reject
- `docker-compose.yml` — `coredns` service; `COREDNS_UID` env var used consistently across both `coredns` and `mitmproxy` services

## Test plan

- [x] `cd infra/coredns && uv run pytest test_generate_corefile.py -v` — all 46 pass locally
- [x] Container test: `docker build --target corefile-generator -f infra/coredns/Dockerfile -t coredns-test-env . && docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)/infra/coredns/test_generate_corefile.py":/work/test_generate_corefile.py coredns-test-env sh -c "pip install pytest pytest-cov --quiet --root-user-action=ignore && python -m pytest test_generate_corefile.py -v"` — 46/46, 100% coverage
- [x] `docker compose config` — validate compose file
- [x] Full devcontainer bring-up: `dig github.com` resolves, `dig evil.com` returns REFUSED, `nc -z -w2 1.1.1.1 853` fails

title: feat: JWT認証・Knowledge API・Gemini連携機能の実装
body:
## 作成した機能

### 認証機能
- JWT認証（Simple JWT）の追加
  - `POST /api/token/` - トークン取得
  - `POST /api/token/refresh/` - トークンリフレッシュ
- CORS設定（localhost:3000からのアクセス許可）

### Knowledge API
- ナレッジのCRUD操作
  - `GET /api/knowledge/` - 一覧取得
  - `GET /api/knowledge/{id}/` - 詳細取得
  - `POST /api/knowledge/` - 新規作成
- タグ機能（ManyToMany）
- 日本語対応の管理画面

### Gemini API連携
- `POST /api/knowledge/` 時に自動でコンテンツ生成
  - タイトル自動生成
  - Markdown形式の本文生成
  - タグ自動付与（3〜5個）
- `GET /api/verify-gemini/` - APIキー検証エンドポイント
- エラー時のフォールバック処理（raw_inputのみ保存）

## 現状の課題

- [x] Gemini API無料枠のレート制限あり（クォータ超過時はフォールバック動作）
- [x] トークンリフレッシュのブラックリスト機能は有効だがmigration未適用の可能性あり

## 動作確認

- [x] `docker compose up --build` でコンテナ起動
- [x] `http://localhost:8000/admin/` に admin/adminpass でログイン
- [x] `POST /api/token/` でJWTトークン取得
- [x] `GET /api/knowledge/` でナレッジ一覧取得（認証必須）
- [x] `POST /api/knowledge/` でナレッジ作成（Gemini連携）
- [x] Next.js (localhost:3000) からのCORSアクセス

title: correctly handle AWS provider setting config vm_[name] to None
body:
### What does this PR do?
Causes salt.config.get_cloud_config_value() to ignore `None` config values at the highest level of precedence: vm_

Setting a vm_[name] config to None or anything else that evaluates to False will still be returned as `value = deepcopy(vm_[name])` 

### What issues does this PR fix or reference?
At some point in the salt-cloud mapfile deployment of AWS EC2 VMs, the aws provider sometimes sets the config value of `vm_[name]` to `None`. Using `None` doesn't seem to make any sense. The documented behavior of `salt.config.get_cloud_config_value()` tries to prefer this over the map, profile, and provider defaults, but fails to `vm_[name].copy()` the value to return the name because it is `None`, which is `NoneType`, and does not implement `.copy()`, and generates an exception to this effect.

    [ERROR   ] There was a profile error: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'copy' Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/cli.py", line 281, in run self.config.get('names')
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/__init__.py", line 1454, in run_profile ret[name] = self.create(vm_)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/__init__.py", line 1284, in create output = self.clouds[func](vm_)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/cloud/clouds/ec2.py", line 2760, in create for key, value in six.iteritems(__utils__['cloud.bootstrap'](vm_, __opts__)):
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/utils/cloud.py", line 362, in bootstrap minion_conf = minion_config(opts, vm_)
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/utils/cloud.py", line 260, in minion_config 'grains', vm_, opts, default={}, search_global=True
    File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/salt/config/__init__.py", line 3309, in get_cloud_config_value value.update(vm_[name].copy())

Add CommentCollapse 

### Tests written?

No

### Commits signed with GPG?

Yes

title: Update pillar/grain master cache logic
body:
### What does this PR do?

When executing _get_minion_grains and all nodes are loaded via cache, len(missed_minions) == 0.
This causes  ```lret = self._get_live_minion_grains(missed_minions)```  to execute with an empty list which results in the print statement ```No minions matched the target. No command was sent, no jid was assigned.``` but everything else executes fine...
This adds additional validation before executing the fallback statements.

This also ensures that directly queried minion grains/pillars "lret" overwrites any cached data "cret" before return.

### What issues does this PR fix or reference?
No issue number.

### Tests written?

No

### Commits signed with GPG?

Yes

title: Refactor add support for python 3.13
body:
- Python 3 compatibility refactor: Migrated all Python 2 syntax to Python 3 — replaced print statements with print(),
  raw_input() with input(), has_key() with in operator, and updated email module imports from the old
  email.MIMEMultipart/email.MIMEBase style to the modern email.mime.* namespace.
  - Dropped colorlog/colorama dependencies: Replaced ColoredFormatter with the stdlib logging.Formatter, removing two
  third-party dependencies and simplifying the dependency tree.
  - Switched os.path to pathlib.Path: Modernized path handling throughout setup.py, send.py, and maildiff_cmd.py.
  - Expanded Python version support: Updated classifiers and Makefile to reflect support for Python 3.7–3.13.
  - Version bump: Bumped version from 2.0.3 → 3.0.0 to reflect the breaking compatibility changes.
  - Package import fix: Changed import maildiff_cmd to from . import maildiff_cmd in emaildiff/__init__.py to make the package
  importable as a proper Python package.
  - Subprocess improvement: Added text=True to subprocess.Popen call to return strings instead of bytes.
  - README updates: Fixed install command (pip install .), updated dependency links to Python 3 docs, removed colorlog/colorama
   entries, and refreshed badges.

title: Fix alembic installation error
body:
Fixes an installation error where `pip` was attempting to build an old version of `alembic` that used the removed `use_2to3` argument in `setuptools`. By explicitly adding `alembic>=1.13.0` to `pyproject.toml`, we ensure a compatible version is installed.

body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.
Changelog

Sourced from requests's changelog.

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.

```bash
TypeError: One of the data arrays does not have 32-bit float type
```

---
## Cause

I check the types of `pos, vel, elem_den, temp, hh` and elem_den returns float64 instead of 32, causing mismatch.
Simliar for ratenetworkspectra.py there are mismatch in density and lienergy.

I suspect somewhere in the code the float32 rule is more strict than 2023 version (or maybe numpy is more restrict), but I am not familiar with the code enough to pinpoint that. So perhaps there are multiple occurrences need to patch

## Fix

Current fix for me is change the dtype of `self.rscale` from float64 to float32, so it won't affect the downstream `elem_den`.
And for `ratenetworkspectra.py` the fix is making these two float32
```python
       ldensity = np.log(density).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
        lienergy = np.log(ienergy).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
```
copy false intentionally avoid creating new copy.

In case it's helpful, this is the [notebook](https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1f0foFXJRgbeHg0_nwMJm28T0PVVf3IFS?usp=sharing) to run on my branch.

title: drop YouTube transcript refs, fix Amazon country param, bump 2.4
body:
## Summary
- Remove leftover ScavioYouTubeTranscript references from the README (the tool was removed in df89bb4 but docs still shipped them).
- Switch the LangGraph agent example to the current \`langchain.agents.create_agent\` API.
- Update \`pyproject.toml\` description so the PyPI project page surfaces Amazon/Walmart/YouTube support; bump version 2.3 -> 2.4.
- Drop the misleading \`country\` field from \`ScavioAmazonSearch\` / \`ScavioAmazonProduct\` args schemas. It was not the Amazon marketplace selector and caused upstream 400s when agents passed values like \`"US"\`. \`domain\` is the only way to pick a store.
- Expand the \`domain\` description to enumerate all 22 supported marketplaces (com, co.uk, ca, de, fr, es, it, co.jp, in, com.au, com.br, com.mx, nl, pl, se, sg, ae, sa, eg, cn, com.be, com.tr).

- [ ] CI green across Python 3.10-3.13
- [ ] After merge: publish 2.4 to PyPI and verify the new description on the project page

title: fix: improve fcc tests branch filtering pattern
body:
The FCC file has these branches/subbranches:
```
 'PARAMETERS',
 'PARAMETERS/_intMap',
 'PARAMETERS/_intMap/_intMap.first',
 'PARAMETERS/_intMap/_intMap.second',
 'PARAMETERS/_floatMap',
 'PARAMETERS/_floatMap/_floatMap.first',
 'PARAMETERS/_floatMap/_floatMap.second',
 'PARAMETERS/_stringMap',
 'PARAMETERS/_stringMap/_stringMap.first',
 'PARAMETERS/_stringMap/_stringMap.second'
 ```
When we do `tree.iteritems()` to extract the base form, subbranch names only contain the subbranch part and not the full path. The function sees PARAMETERS first. Then it sees _intMap, then it sees _intMap.first and so on. Therefore, the filtering function `lambda x: "PARAMETERS" not in x` is not enough. I'm updating it to a regex to exclude `_*Map*` patterns too.

title: Jj ctfset iteritems
BUG: stats.levy_stable: fix PDF in Tails for `beta=+-1`
body: #### Reference issue Closes #20636 by fixing integration. #### What does this implement/fix? The PDF of the Levy Stable distribution did not produce the correct results in the far tails for $\beta = \pm 1$. This PR fixes that as show in the graph where the y-axis is the log of the pdf Furthermore, for the S1 parameterization with $\alpha\approx 1$ and $\beta=\pm 1$, the PDF was wildly off as shown in the graph where the y-axis is the log of the pdf With this PR, the above graph looks like #### Additional information Correcting these issues was accomplished with two main fixes. First, I modified the points used to integrate in the function `_pdf_single_value_piecewise_post_rounding_Z0`. The integrand in this function goes from very peaked for small $x$ to somewhat broad for large $x$. The previous method to get the integration points worked for an integrand that was peaked but not broad. To fix this, I caught when the previous technique broke down and then chose evenly spaced integration points which works well for a broad integrand. For very small $x$, the integrand becomes so peaked that integration is hopeless. To fix this, I used a Taylor Series for small $x$ which fixes the "step" behavior we see in the second graph above. One issue is that this implementation is about 2-3x slower than the previous implementation. This could probably be fixed by vectorizing the function `g` in [`levyst.c`](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/main/scipy/stats/_levy_stable/c_src/levyst.c), but I figured I'd wait for a review before trying that.

title: Merge setup.py into pyproject.toml
body:
Consolidates packaging configuration into `pyproject.toml`, removes `setup.cfg`, and simplifies `setup.py` to only the Cython/C extension build.

## Changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added full `[project]` table with all metadata previously in `setup.py` — name, version (`2.1.0`), description, author, license, classifiers, dependencies, homepage URL (`https://github.com/sequana/idr`), `requires-python = ">=3.9"`. Added `[tool.setuptools]` with `script-files = ["bin/idr"]` to preserve the CLI script. Added `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` from `setup.cfg`. The `readme = "README.md"` field ensures the README serves as the long description on PyPI, preserving the full project description previously in `setup.py`.
- **`setup.py`**: Reduced to extension-only — retains Cython/C extension build logic (`idr.inv_cdf`) which cannot be expressed natively in `pyproject.toml` with the setuptools backend.
- **`setup.cfg`**: Removed; pytest config migrated to `pyproject.toml`.
- **`idr/__init__.py`**: `__version__` is now dynamically inferred at runtime using `importlib.metadata`, reflecting the version declared in `pyproject.toml`.
- **`README.md`**: Added a "Packaging" section documenting the migration from `setup.py`/`setup.cfg` to `pyproject.toml`, explaining what each file now contains and how the project description is preserved.

title: Refactor StructureAnalyzer to use iterative stack-based traversals
body:
This pull request addresses reliability and performance bottlenecks in `StructureAnalyzer` by migrating recursive file system traversals to stack-based iterative algorithms.

**Improvements:**
1. **Prevent Recursion Errors**: Deeply nested directories no longer risk hitting Python's recursion limit.
2. **Crash Prevention**: Added `try...except PermissionError` blocks when iterating over directories, preventing the entire analysis from crashing due to a single unreadable folder.
3. **Memory/Performance**: Iterative traversals generally incur less overhead than deeply nested function calls in Python.

The changes preserve the exact output structure and behavior (including the dictionary insertion order in `_build_tree`) while significantly hardening the analyzer against real-world repository anomalies.

---

title: NSCK V10: full-stack cognitive extensions, Rust concurrent memory, and final audit
body:
Implements all Tier 1–3 roadmap items from the V9 report: Rust concurrent memory exposed, dense embedding↔VSA bridge, differentiable FHRR VSA, probabilistic NLU, attention-weighted GWT, neural rule scoring, safety verification, and a REST API. Follows with a comprehensive quality audit fixing latent bugs, dead code, and bringing all docs current.

## V10 New Modules

| Module | What it does |
|---|---|
| `vsa/vsa_embedding_bridge.py` | Random-projection bridge: 768-dim dense → 10,240-bit HV and back; sentence-transformers optional |
| `vsa/fhrr.py` | Complex-phasor VSA — bind=multiply, unbind=conjugate-mul; gradient-compatible via `to_gradient_input()` |
| `vsa/rust_concurrent_shim.py` | Exposes `SemanticMemoryConcurrent`, `EpisodicMemoryConcurrent`, `CognitiveWorkerPool` from rebuilt Rust `.so`; Python fallbacks |
| `language/ngram_nlu.py` | Naive Bayes n-gram NLU (33K sent/s); intent + entity extraction |
| `reasoning/attention_gwt_bridge.py` | Multi-head attention weights coalition salience before GWT competition |
| `learning/rule_neural_scorer.py` | Single-hidden-layer perceptron re-ranks rules; online SGD; wired into `decide()` via `enable_neural_rule_scoring()` |
| `cognitive/safety_verifier.py` | Formula-based safety properties in restricted `eval` namespace; `SafetyGateVerifier.gate_decision()` |
| `api/nsck_api.py` | FastAPI REST wrapper (POST `/decide`, `/learn`, `/sleep`; GET `/status`); stdlib fallback |

## Rust

Both `hypervec_rs` and `snn_rs` rebuilt. `hypervec_rs` now exports `SemanticMemoryConcurrent`, `EpisodicMemoryConcurrent`, `Episode`, `CognitiveWorkerPool`, `HyperVectorRegistry`, `PersistentStorage`, `parallel_bundle`, `batch_parallel_similarity_search` — previously compiled but not wired to Python.

```python
from python.core.vsa.rust_concurrent_shim import SemanticMemoryConcurrent, USE_RUST_CONCURRENT
# USE_RUST_CONCURRENT=True when .so is present; falls back to pure-Python transparently
sm = SemanticMemoryConcurrent()
sm.add_concept("cat", hv)
```

## Wiring into `CognitiveEngine.decide()`

Neural rule scorer integrates as an opt-in re-ranker; score is taken from the same post-ranking position to avoid rule/confidence mismatch:

```python
engine.enable_neural_rule_scoring()
# decide() now: get applicable rules → neural re-rank → take top rule AND its score
```

## Quality Audit

- **Bug**: `Rule` is a non-frozen `@dataclass` (`__hash__ = None`); using rule objects as dict keys crashed the neural scorer. Fixed with identity comparison (`r is rule`).
- **Bug**: `api/nsck_api.py` `handle_decide` propagated exceptions uncaught. Wrapped in try/except; errors returned as structured JSON.
- **Dead code**: no-op `for prop in DEFAULT_PROPERTIES: pass` loop in `SafetyGateVerifier.__init__` removed.
- **Test warnings**: 3 `PytestReturnNotNoneWarning`s eliminated (`return True`/`return bool` → `assert`).

## Documentation

- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — §15 V10 module table and updated decision loop
- `docs/MODULE_REFERENCE.md` — full V10 section (all 8 modules, classes, methods, signatures)
- `docs/WORKFLOWS.md` — §9 neural scoring, safety gate, FHRR+embedding, REST API sequence diagrams
- `docs/NSCK_V10_EXTENSIONS.md` — new end-to-end V10 spec
- `README.md` — V10 capabilities and quick-start

**1,248 tests pass with Rust active · 0 CodeQL alerts · warnings 4 → 1**

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Goal

Transform NSCK from a "collection of cognitive modules" into a **true modality-agnostic cognitive substrate** — inherently capable of learning, reasoning, remembrance, recall, generalization, cross-domain transfer, and lifelong learning for **any input type** (numbers, text, audio, images, sensor streams, dicts, etc.).

The constraint: **no neural nets in the understanding/reasoning/memory/decision core**. Neural components (SNN) are only allowed as optional perception adapters at the edge. The core remains purely VSA + symbolic.

## Branch

Base branch: `NSCK_V3`

## The 7 Implementation Steps

### STEP 1: Universal PerceptPacket Contract

**Problem:** Currently `decide()` in `cognitive_engine.py` (line ~438) accepts raw `Dict[str, Any]` and does perception inline — calling `GroundingVerifier.get_active_predicates(state)` and `EpisodicMemory.create_situation_hv(state, ...)` inside the decision loop. This means perception and cognition are tangled. Each new modality must know how `decide()` works internally.

**What to implement:**

1. Create `nsck/python/core/types/percept_packet.py` with a frozen dataclass `PerceptPacket`:
   - `modality: str` ("text", "numeric", "dict", "snn", "multimodal", "stream")
   - `timestamp: float`
   - `situation_hv: HyperVector` (bundled summary)
   - `entity_hvs: Dict[str, HyperVector]` (named concept → HV)
   - `relation_hvs: List[Tuple[str, str, str, HyperVector]]` (subj, pred, obj, triple_hv)
   - `active_predicates: FrozenSet[str]` (grounded symbolic predicates)
   - `confidence: float` (0.0–1.0)
   - `raw_state: Optional[Dict[str, Any]]` (original state dict for backward compat)
   - `adapter_name: str`
   - `adapter_trace: Dict[str, Any]`

2. Create `nsck/python/core/types/modality_adapter.py` with abstract base class `ModalityAdapter` having one method: `encode(raw_input: Any, task_tag: str) -> PerceptPacket`

3. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/dict_state_adapter.py` — wraps the existing `GroundingVerifier` + `EpisodicMemory.create_situation_hv()` logic currently inline in `decide()` lines 458-473.

4. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/text_adapter.py` — wraps `UniversalInput.ground_text()`.

5. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/numeric_adapter.py` — wraps `UniversalInput.ground_scalar()` and `ground_sequence()`.

6. Create `nsck/python/core/adapters/snn_adapter.py` — wraps the logic from `perceive_and_decide()` lines 366-427.

7. Modify `CognitiveEngine.decide()` to accept `Union[Dict, PerceptPacket]` — if dict, auto-wrap via the registered adapter (backward compatible). If PerceptPacket, use directly. The core body of `decide()` from line ~473 onward (curiosity check, coalition building, GWT, safety, explanation) stays unchanged but reads from `percept.active_predicates` and `percept.situation_hv` instead of computing them inline.

8. Modify `register_task()` to also register an adapter (auto-creates `DictStateAdapter` from verifier if no adapter provided).

9. Add `self.adapters: Dict[str, ModalityAdapter] = {}` to `CognitiveEngine.__init__()`.

**Backward compatibility:** ALL existing tests must pass unchanged because `decide()` auto-wraps dicts.

### STEP 2: Wire Generalization Into the Automatic Learning Loop

**Problem:** `SemanticMemory.build_prototypes()`, `infer_transitive()`, `extract_schema()`, and `AnalogyEngine.auto_discover_abstractions()` all exist as callable methods but are NEVER invoked automatically by `sleep()` or `learn()`. Generalization is "available" but not "inherent."

**What to implement:**

Add to `CognitiveEngine.sleep()` (after existing step 3 "_consolidate_semantic" and before step 4 "planner refresh"):

- **Step 4a: Prototype building** — call `self.semantic_memory.build_prototypes(min_members=2)` and store results back.
- **Step 4b: Transitive inference** — call `self.semantic_memory.infer_transitive("is_a", max_hops=3)` and `infer_transitive("causes", max_hops=2)`.
- **Step 4c: Cross-task auto-discovery** — for every pair of tasks being consolidated, call `self.analogy.auto_discover_abstractions()` with concept HVs from both tasks.

Add helper `_collect_concept_hvs_for_task(task_tag)` that gathers HVs from rules + causal graph nodes.

Track new stats: `prototypes_built`, `transitive_inferences`, `auto_abstractions`.

### STEP 3: Automatic Cross-Domain Transfer on New Task Registration

**Problem:** `register_task()` (line ~298) only sets up verifier, causal graph, and causal reasoner. No transfer attempt happens. The `transfer()` method exists separately and must be called manually.

**What to implement:**

At the end of `register_task()`, if `self.config.enable_auto_transfer` is True and there are known tasks with learned rules:
- For each known task, call `self.analogy.auto_discover_abstractions()` between it and the new task.
- If mappings found, call `self.analogy.transfer_rule()` for each learned rule and inject transferred rules into `rule_learner`.
- Add `add_transferred_rule(task_tag, condition, consequence, sour...

</details>

title: NSCK V14: Rich Perception, Rust Shim, Knowledge Packs, Hybrid Distillation, Scale Benchmarks
body:
Implements the full NSCK V14 architecture upgrade across 6 work packages, adding a rich perception layer with neural bridge adapters, Rust-accelerated spreading activation, serializable knowledge packs, hybrid distillation, and scale validation — while preserving full backward compatibility in `pure` mode.

## WP-1: Packaging & Cleanup
- Deleted dead `compositional_semantics_backup.py`
- Added `nsck/Makefile` (`test`, `build-rust`, `bench`, `clean`)

## WP-2: Rust Spreading Activation Shim
- `semantic_memory_shim.py` — same try/except pattern as `hypervec_shim.py`; caches already-synced concepts so repeated calls only push *new* nodes to Rust's DashMap
- `SemanticMemory.spread_activation()` tries shim first, falls through to existing Python path if unavailable

## WP-3: Rich Perception Layer
- `RichTextAdapter`, `RichImageAdapter`, `RichAudioAdapter` — each attempts a neural bridge (`sentence-transformers` / `timm` / `whisper`), falls back to classical adapter; optional deps are never required
- `NSCKConfig` gains 8 V14 fields; new `rich()` and `for_scale(n)` factory methods
- `NSCKSubstrate` routes to rich adapters when `perception_mode ∈ {"bridge", "hybrid"}`

```python
cfg = NSCKConfig.rich()               # perception_mode="bridge", all research flags
cfg = NSCKConfig.for_scale(50_000)    # auto-tunes memory_capacity + HNSW settings
sub = NSCKSubstrate(config=cfg)
result = sub.ingest("The sky is blue", "demo")  # uses RichTextAdapter if ST available
```

## WP-4: Hybrid Distillation
- `PerceptionDistiller` — rolling 100-observation quality window per modality; graduates modality to pure-internal encoding once `avg(similarity) ≥ threshold`; warns on out-of-range similarity values

## WP-5: Knowledge Packs
- `KnowledgePack` — gzip-pickle bundles with `add_concept / add_relation / add_causal_link / save / load / inject_into`
- `NSCKSubstrate.__init__` auto-injects packs from `config.knowledge_packs`
- ⚠️ `load()` uses pickle — documented as trusted-source only; JSON migration noted as future work

## WP-6: Scale Validation
- `eval/scale_benchmarks.py` — latency table at 100–5K nodes
- Integration tests assert 100-concept spread activation <100 ms, 1K-concept <500 ms

## Documentation
- New: `GOAL_TRACKER.md`, `V14_CHANGELOG.md`, `V14_REPORT.md`
- Updated: `MODULE_REFERENCE.md`, `FORMULAS.md`, `README.md` (bridge projection formula, distillation graduation criterion)

**Test delta:** 1311 passed / 152 skipped / 3 xfailed (+29 new tests, 0 regressions vs V13 baseline of 1282)

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

# NSCK V14 Master Plan — Complete Implementation

## The Vision (Must Be Preserved)

The creator's true goal for NSCK:

> An architectural substrate which is inherently capable of learning, reasoning, remembrance, recall, generalization of learnings, cross-domain knowledge transfer after generalizations, lifelong learning and improvement. NSCK must have these inherently for no matter the input type — series of numbers, texts, audio, image(s), sensory input or streams, numbers, etc — and can know and understand them, multiple kinds of input simultaneously or sequentially, and can find patterns, meanings, learnings, generalizations of the learnings and knowledge and find correlations. Its transparent glass-box structure means every decision, response, and action the system takes can be traceable end to end. By having all these inherently, NSCK can be used in many different things in many different ways like neural networks and other learning architectures, and it's very efficient and robust compared to current architectures. With these, other developers can build upon it with other modules etc. and we can reach AGI.

## Work Packages — Strict Order

### WP-1: Packaging & Cleanup

**Goal:** Remove IDE/environment artifacts from git, add proper Python packaging, fix hardcoded paths, delete dead files.

**Files to create/modify:**

1. **Create `nsck/pyproject.toml`** — Standard Python packaging:
```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.backends._legacy:_Backend"

[project]
name = "nsck"
version = "0.14.0"
description = "Neuro-Symbolic Cognitive Kernel — an architectural substrate for AGI"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = ["numpy>=1.24", "networkx>=3.0"]

[project.optional-dependencies]
rich-perception = ["torch>=2.0", "torchvision", "timm", "sentence-transformers"]
rich-audio = ["torch>=2.0", "openai-whisper"]
eval = ["scikit-learn", "matplotlib"]
all = ["nsck[rich-perception,rich-audio,eval]"]
```

2. **Create `nsck/Makefile`** with targets: `test`, `build-rust`, `bench`, `clean`

3. **Update `.gitignore`** — Add `.idea/`, `.venv/`, `.vscode/`, `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `*.so`, `nsck_brain.db`, `checkpoints/`

4. **Delete `nsck/python/core/language/compositional_semantics_backup.py`** — Dead backup file

5. **Fix hardcoded paths** in:
   - `nsck/python/core/perception/snn_perception.py` line ~22 — Replace `Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent.parent` with proper relative import
   - `nsck/eval/train_and_evaluate.py` — Replace any `/workspaces/Node_network/` with relative paths
   - `nsck/scripts/generate_v11_report.py` — Same fix

---

### WP-2: Rust Spreading Activation Wiring

**Goal:** Wire existing Rust `SemanticMemoryConcurrent.parallel_spread_activation()` as a drop-in accelerator for Python's `SemanticMemory.spread_activation()`. Add the two features currently missing in Rust: relation-type weights and stigmergy boost.

**Files to create/modify:**

1. **Modify `nsck/rust_vsa/src/semantic.rs`**:
   - Change the graph storage to include relation types with edges: currently `graph: DashMap<String, Vec<String>>` stores `(concept → [neighbours])`. Needs to become `(concept → [(neighbour, relation_type)])` so spreading activation can use relation weights.
   - Add a `relation_weight()` function that returns: `is_a → 0.9`, `has_property → 0.7`, `causes/leads_to/results_in → 0.6`, `part_of → 0.5`, `similar_to/semantically_related → 0.4`, default → `0.3`
   - In `parallel_spread_activation`, change from `let spread_val = (act * decay) / num_neighbors as f64;` to `let spread_val = act * decay * relation_weight(&rel);`
   - Add a `DashMap<(String, String), f64>` for stigmergy pheromone values
   - Add `set_stigmergy(a, b, val)` and `get_stigmergy(a, b)` PyO3 methods
   - In the spread calculation, multiply by `(1.0 + stigmergy_value)` when present

2. **Create `nsck/python/core/memory/semantic_memory_shim.py`**:
   - Auto-selects Rust or Python backend (same try/except pattern as `hypervec_shim.py`)
   - Exports a `spread_activation_fast()` function that: tries Rust backend first (syncing the NetworkX graph to Rust's DashMap), falls back to None (caller uses Python)
   - Caches the Rust SemanticMemoryConcurrent instance so graph doesn't need re-syncing every call

3. **Modify `nsck/python/core/memory/semantic_memory.py`**:
   - At the top of `spread_activation()`, try the Rust shim first: call `spread_activation_fast()`, if it returns a result, use it; otherwise fall through to existing NetworkX implementation
   - The existing `_MAX_FRONTIER = 200` cap and all existing Python logic must be preserved as fallback

4. **Create `nsck/tests/unit/test_rust_spread_activation.py`**:
   - `test_rust_python_equivalence()` — Build same graph in both, verify results match within float tolerance
   - `test_rust_relation_weights()` — `is_a` edges carry more activation than `similar_to`
   - `test_rust_stigmergy_boost()` — Pheromone-marked paths get higher activation
 ...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

title: feat: Linked Data rebuild (v0.2.0)
body:
## Summary

CDS v0.2.0 converts every identity to a dereferenceable HTTP URI, making all events valid JSON-LD and achieving Tim Berners-Lee's 5-star Linked Data rating.

### Breaking changes
- `content_type` is now a URI string (was `CDSContentType` object)
- `source.@id` replaces `source.id`
- `integrity.signed_by` is now a full URI (`"https://signed-data.org"`)
- Events include `@context`, `@type`, `@id` fields
- Canonical bytes include `@context`/`@type`/`@id` (v0.1.0 events cannot be verified by v0.2.0 verifiers)

### What's new
- **Vocabulary layer:** 14 JSON-LD files (`vocab/`, `contexts/`) — ontology, source registry, domain definitions
- **Python SDK:** `CDSVocab`, `CDSSources` URI constants, new JSON-LD envelope, lottery/weather ingestors — **39 tests passing**
- **TypeScript SDK:** Mirror of Python vocab, new `CDSEvent` class with `@context`/`@type`/`@id` — **21 tests passing, 0 type errors**
- **Documentation:** Spec v0.2.0, migration guide, Linked Data explainer, updated all existing docs

### 5-star Linked Data
| Stars | Criteria | CDS |
|---|---|---|
| :star: | Available online, open license | MIT |
| :star::star: | Structured data | JSON |
| :star::star::star: | Non-proprietary format | JSON |
| :star::star::star::star: | W3C standards | JSON-LD |
| :star::star::star::star::star: | Links to other data | URI identities |

## Test plan
- [x] Python: `cd sdk/python && pytest` — 39 passed
- [x] TypeScript: `cd sdk/typescript && npm run typecheck && npm test` — 21 passed, 0 errors
- [x] All 14 JSON-LD files validate with `jq`
- [x] End-to-end: construct event → `to_jsonld()` → verify `@context`/`@type`/`@id`
- [x] Signing roundtrip: sign → serialize → deserialize → verify

title: Initial HR Insights package with CLI, HTTP API, metrics, storage, docs, tests, and CI/docker tooling
body:
### Motivation

- Provide a baseline HR KPI calculation toolchain (CLI + HTTP API) to compute KPIs, hiring funnel, and data quality from employee CSV data.
- Add persistence and report history to support exports and retention management for operational usage.
- Include developer and CI ergonomics (packaging, Docker, Makefile, sample data, and automated tests) for easier iteration and validation.

### Description

- Add a new `hr_insights` package with modules: `models`, `metrics`, `hiring`, `quality`, `service`, `storage`, `server`, `cli`, and `config`, plus `__version__` in `__init__.py`.
- Implement KPI calculation and CSV parsing in `metrics.py`, hiring funnel in `hiring.py`, and data quality checks in `quality.py`, all wired by `service.py` for reuse by CLI and server.
- Provide a simple threaded HTTP API server in `server.py` with endpoints for `/kpis`, `/quality`, `/hiring-funnel`, `/reports`, `/reports/export`, `/reports/cleanup`, `/health`, `/ready`, and `/metrics`, along with API key auth, rate limiting, and report persistence using `storage.py` (SQLite).
- Add packaging metadata in `pyproject.toml` including console scripts `hr-kpi` and `hr-server`, a `Dockerfile`, `Makefile` shortcuts, a sample CSV dataset `data/sample_employees.csv`, a PRD `docs/PRD.md`, and an expanded `README.md` describing usage.
- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, a `.gitignore`, and a comprehensive test suite under `tests/` covering CLI, config, metrics, hiring, quality, service, storage, and server integration scenarios.

### Testing

- Ran the unit and integration test suite with `pytest -q` (also exposed via `make test`) and the full suite completed successfully across the repository tests. 
- Server integration tests spawn the HTTP server and exercise `/kpis`, `/hiring-funnel`, report listing/export, API key enforcement, readiness probe, pagination validation, and cleanup and they passed. 
- Storage tests validated SQLite round-trips, CSV export, pagination, and date filtering and they passed. 
- Config and CLI tests (including conflicting CLI modes and JSON output) were executed and passed.

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0 in /docker_client_server_hello_world/client
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @​aminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @​E8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960
  • @​mitre88 made their first contribution in psf/requests#7244
  • @​magsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @​Rohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.
Changelog

Sourced from requests's changelog.

Security

The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

title: Fix use-after-free and leak in encoder ident handling
body:
Fixes #349 (part of #348).

## Changes

1. **Fix double `Py_XDECREF` on `ident`** (Finding 14): When `PyDict_DelItem(s->markers, ident)` fails in `encoder_listencode_obj`, `ident` was XDECREF'd inside the `if` block, then XDECREF'd again unconditionally. Now uses a single `Py_DECREF` after the error check, matching the correct pattern in `encoder_listencode_dict` (line 3086).

2. **Fix `ident` leak on `Py_EnterRecursiveCall` failure** (Finding 15): `ident` acquired via `PyLong_FromVoidPtr` was never released when `Py_EnterRecursiveCall` fails. Added `Py_XDECREF(ident)` before `return rv`.

## Reproducers

Finding 14 segfault (from issue):
```python
import simplejson._speedups as sp
import decimal

markers = {}
class Evil: pass
call_count = [0]
def bad_default(obj):
    call_count[0] += 1
    if call_count[0] <= 1:
        markers.clear()
        return "safe"
    return str(obj)

c_enc = sp.make_encoder(
    markers, bad_default, sp.encode_basestring_ascii, None, ", ", ": ",
    False, False, True, None, False, False, False, None,
    None, "utf-8", False, False, decimal.Decimal, False,
)
c_enc(Evil(), 0)  # Segfault before fix
```

Found using [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).

title: Move lazy-init static string caches to module init (free-threading safety)
body:
Part of #362.

## Summary

Move 11 function-scoped `static PyObject*` variables from lazy initialization to
eager initialization in `init_constants()` / `moduleinit()`. Under free-threaded
Python (PEP 703), the lazy-init pattern is a data race — two threads can both see
NULL, both allocate, and one result leaks.

## Changes

**Moved to `init_constants()`** (interned strings + empty tuple):
- `s_null`, `s_true`, `s_false` (from `_encoded_const`)
- `open_dict`, `close_dict`, `empty_dict` (from `encoder_listencode_dict`)
- `open_array`, `close_array`, `empty_array` (from `encoder_listencode_list`)
- `sortargs` — empty tuple (from `encoder_dict_iteritems`)

**Moved to `moduleinit()`** (requires import):
- `itemgetter0` — `operator.itemgetter(0)` (from `encoder_new`)

All 11 are now file-scope statics with a `JSON_` prefix, initialized once during
module import (which is serialized by Python's import lock). The functions that
previously did lazy init now use the pre-initialized globals directly.

`joinfn` (Python 2 only, inside `#if PY_MAJOR_VERSION < 3`) is left as-is since
free-threading only applies to Python 3.13+.

## Test plan

- [x] `python -m pytest simplejson/tests/ -x -q` — 147 tests pass
- [x] C extension loads correctly, all moved constants work
- [x] Net -15 lines (69 insertions, 84 deletions)

Found using [ft-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/ft-review-toolkit).

<sub>This PR was drafted by Claude Code and reviewed by a human.</sub>

title: Release version 4.0.0: free-threading, CPython json parity, Python 2.7 fixes
body:
## Summary

simplejson 4.0.0 with free-threading support, CPython json module feature parity, and Python 2.7 compatibility fixes.

## Key Changes

### Support matrix
- **Python 2.7** and **Python 3.8+** (dropped 2.5, 2.6, 3.0–3.7)
- Pyodide (wasm32) wheels with C speedups
- PEP 517 build support via pyproject.toml
- Python 3.15-dev CI for early detection of regressions

### Free-threading support (PEP 703)
- C extension is safe to use with the GIL disabled (Python 3.14t+)
- Converted all static types to heap types with per-module state
- Added per-object critical sections to scanner and encoder
- Free-threading-safe dict operations for Python 3.13+

### CPython json module feature parity
- **`array_hook`** parameter for `loads()`, `load()`, `JSONDecoder` — called with each decoded JSON array, analogous to `object_hook` for dicts (CPython 3.15)
- **Trailing comma detection** — `JSONDecodeError` with "Illegal trailing comma before end of object/array" instead of generic errors, in both Python decoder and C scanner (CPython 3.13)
- **`frozendict` encoding** — when `frozendict` is available (CPython 3.15 PEP 814), encoded as JSON object like `dict`, in both Python and C encoders
- **`exc.add_note()` context** — serialization errors on Python 3.11+ get annotated with the path to the error, e.g. "when serializing list item 1" / "when serializing dict item 'key'" (CPython 3.14)
- **Non-ASCII digit rejection** — fullwidth digits (e.g. U+FF10) are no longer accepted in JSON numbers by the Python decoder, matching the C scanner (CPython gh-125687)

### Performance
- **C fast path for `encode_basestring`** (`ensure_ascii=False`) — previously fell back to pure Python regex; now has a C implementation with PyUnicodeWriter on 3.14+ (closes #207)
- PyDict_Next fast path for unsorted exact-dict encoding
- Indexed fast path for exact list/tuple encoding
- PyUnicodeWriter as JSON_Accu backend on Python 3.14+

### Memory safety fixes
- Fixed use-after-free and leak in encoder ident handling
- Fixed NULL dereferences on OOM in module init and static string init
- Fixed reference leaks in dict encoder (skipkeys item, variable shadowing)
- Fixed error-as-truthy bugs in maybe_quote_bigint and is_raw_json
- Fixed iterable_as_array swallowing MemoryError and KeyboardInterrupt

### C extension cleanup
- Removed dead single-phase init code for Python 3.3/3.4
- Removed pre-2.7 and pre-3.8 compatibility shims

### Build infrastructure
- GitHub Actions updated to v6
- cibuildwheel v3.4.1 (Python 3.8+) and v1.12.0 (Python 2.7)
- Python 2.7 wheel build separated into its own parallel CI job
- pyproject.toml for PEP 517 builds
- CI tests free-threaded, debug builds with -Werror, refcount leak detection
- Python 3.15-dev prerelease testing with C speedups

## Test plan
- [x] Full test suite passes locally (436 tests, Python 3.11)
- [x] Python 2.7 C extension builds and tests pass
- [x] Pyodide wheel builds and tests pass
- [x] Free-threading tests pass (3.14t)
- [x] Debug build with -Werror passes (3.14 standard + free-threaded)
- [x] Python 3.15-dev prerelease tests pass (frozendict encoding)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

title: Add warning for 5 features.
body:
This PR adds 5 new Python 2 -> Python 3 compatibility warnings on the pygrate2 side.

- iterator.next() warning
Added a warning in wrap_next() for iterator-style .next() calls. In Python 2, iterators commonly use it.next(), while the supported form in python3 is next(it)

- intern() warning
Added a warning in builtin_intern() for builtin intern() calls. In Python 2, intern() is a builtin, while in Python 3 the supported form is sys.intern().

- range() materialization warning
Added a warning in builtin_range() for range() results used in list-like contexts that may require materialization. In Python 2, range() returns a list, while in Python 3 it returns a lazy range object, so code such as range(5) + [5] may need to become list(range(5)) + [5].

- xrange() materialization warning
Refined the warning in range_new() for xrange() calls to distinguish plain iteration from list-like use. In Python 2, xrange() exists as a lazy iterator-like object, while in Python 3 it is removed and range() is the replacement; in some non-iteration contexts, the result may additionally need list materialization.

- dict list-like consumer warning
Added warnings in dict_keys(), dict_values(), and dict_items() when d.keys(), d.values(), or d.items() are used in list-like contexts. In Python 2, these APIs return lists, while in Python 3 they return view objects, so operations such as indexing or list concatenation may require wrapping them with list(...).

Also add corresponding test in the test_py3kwarn.py

title: infra: add Makefile, Dockerfile, and docker-compose for Pillar stack
body:
Adds the build and container infrastructure to serve the HoloLang/Pillar stack — C core compilation, Python runtime packaging, and multi-service orchestration.

## Makefile
- `.hl`-aware targets: `run`, `check FILE=`, `repl`, `info`, `skills`, `canvas`
- .NET-style lifecycle: `build` / `build-all` / `clean` / `publish`
- C Pillar core targets: `core` (builds `libbolo.a` + `libbolo.so` from `core/`), `core-clean`
- Docker targets: `docker-build`, `docker-up`, `docker-down`, `docker-run FILE=`, `docker-repl`, `docker-logs`
- CI convenience: `make ci` = `build + test`; `make all` = `build + core`

## Dockerfile
Multi-stage build:
- **`builder`** (`gcc:13`) — compiles `core/**/*.c` → `libbolo.so`; no-op if C sources not yet present
- **`runtime`** (`python:3.12-slim`) — installs HoloLang with all extras, copies compiled `.so`, runs as non-root `holonaut` user under `tini` init
- Exposes `50051` (gRPC), `8080` (WebSocket), `8000` (REST/Webhook)

## docker-compose.yml
Four services:

| Service | Image | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `pillar` | `hololang/pillar:latest` | Main runtime; runs `full_system.hl`; depends on `storage` healthy |
| `repl` | same | Profile-gated (`repl`); interactive shell_vm |
| `storage` | `ipfs/kubo:v0.28.0` | Offline+online distributed tensor storage; IPFS API on `:5001` |
| `grpc-proxy` | `envoyproxy/envoy:v1.29` | Profile-gated (`grpc-proxy`); HTTP/1.1 → gRPC transcoding |

Named volumes `pillar_data` and `ipfs_data` persist across restarts on a shared `pillar_net` bridge network.

title: Add build system and Python 3 migration for Atlantr
body:
Implements build infrastructure to package Atlantr as a standalone executable and migrates codebase from Python 2.7 to Python 3.x.

## Build System

- **PyInstaller configuration** (`atlantr.spec`) - bundles application with data files into 17MB executable
- **Build automation** - shell scripts for Linux/Mac/Windows, Makefile with targets: `build`, `clean`, `install`, `test`, `run`
- **Dependencies** - `requirements.txt` with gevent/tqdm, `.gitignore` for artifacts

## Python 3 Migration

Minimal compatibility changes to make code functional:
- Print statements → `print()` functions
- `xrange` → `range`
- `.iteritems()` → `.items()`
- Removed deprecated `compiler` module import
- Updated shebang to `#!/usr/bin/python3`

Original Python 2.7 code backed up as `3.0/atr3.py.backup`.

## Documentation

- **BUILD.md** - prerequisites, build instructions, troubleshooting
- **README.md** - updated system requirements and usage examples for Python 3

Build output is `dist/atlantr` executable requiring no runtime dependencies.

title: Add Docker Compose and AGENTS.md for local development
body:
Adds a Docker Compose setup for local development (web + PostgreSQL) and an `AGENTS.md` to orient AI agents toward using it.

## Changes

- **`docker-compose.yml`** — `web` (Django, port 8000) + `db` (Postgres 16) services; web waits on `db` health check, runs migrations on start, all env vars pre-configured
- **`Dockerfile`** — Python 3.12-slim image; installs deps, runs `manage.py runserver`
- **`config/settings.py`** — Database config now env-driven; defaults to SQLite (no change for existing non-Docker local dev), switches to PostgreSQL when `DB_ENGINE` is set
- **`requirements.txt`** — Added `psycopg2-binary>=2.9`
- **`.env.example`** — Documents all supported env vars
- **`AGENTS.md`** — Quick-start guide for agents: `docker compose up --build`, exec commands, test invocation

## Quick start

```bash
docker compose up --build
# App at http://localhost:8000
```

title: Modernize
body:
This commit modernizes the entire Malwarehouse codebase from Python 2.7 to Python 3.9+:

### Core Changes:
- Updated all print statements to print() functions
- Replaced file() built-in with open() using proper binary mode
- Changed exception handling from 'except Exception, e' to 'except Exception as e'
- Updated .iteritems() to .items() for dictionary iteration
- Replaced ConfigParser.SafeConfigParser with configparser.ConfigParser
- Updated metaclass syntax from __metaclass__ to metaclass= parameter

### Library Modernization:
- Replaced urllib2 with requests library for HTTP operations
- Removed poster library dependency (replaced with requests)
- Updated to ppdeep from pydeep for fuzzy hashing
- Pinned modern versions of SQLAlchemy and python-magic

### New Files:
- Added requirements.txt for pip-based dependency management

### Documentation:
- Updated README.md to reflect Python 3.9+ requirement
- Added installation instructions using pip
- Updated library documentation with modern package names

title: Refactor travel intent extraction and fix duplicate returns
body:
## Summary
This PR improves code quality and robustness across the agent codebase by implementing proper travel intent extraction logic, removing duplicate return statements, fixing SQL injection vulnerabilities, and clarifying documentation.

## Key Changes

### Core Logic Improvements
- **Enhanced `extract_travel_intent()` function** in RootRouter: Replaced stub implementation with actual heuristic-based extraction using regex patterns to identify dates (YYYY-MM-DD, Month Day formats) and now returns `raw_input` for downstream LLM classification
- **SQL injection prevention** in `planning.py`: Added proper escaping of destination parameter in Wikipedia pageviews query using `.replace("'", "\\'")` 
- **Removed duplicate return statements** across multiple agents:
  - `RootRouter/agent.py`: Removed unreachable return after `fetch_profile()` function
  - `BookingOrchestrator/agent.py`: Removed duplicate success response in `finalize_bookings()`
  - `CarRentalSpecialist/agent.py`: Removed duplicate return in `check_loyalty_status()`
  - `HotelSpecialist/agent.py`: Removed duplicate return in `fetch_hotel_inventory()`
  - `WeatherSpecialist/agent.py`: Removed duplicate return in `fetch_weather()`

### Documentation & Code Quality
- **Clarified MCP documentation** (`docs/MCP.md`): Simplified explanations of SSE transport, trace context propagation, and server-side extraction with concrete implementation references
- **Improved A2A documentation** (`docs/A2A.md`): Rewrote for clarity on HTTP delegation, automated tracing, and OIDC token injection
- **Updated prompt instructions** (`prompt.py`): Refined INSPIRATION_INSTRUCTION to better describe agent responsibilities
- **Fixed typo** in `docs/ADK_INSTRUMENTATION.md`: "Safty" → "Safety"
- **Simplified SSE client usage** in `planning.py`: Removed unnecessary `asyncio.wait_for()` wrapper around `sse_client()` context manager
- **Cleaned up imports** in `main.py`: Removed unused `os` import

### Dependencies
- Removed unused OpenTelemetry GCP exporters (`opentelemetry-exporter-gcp-logging`, `opentelemetry-exporter-gcp-monitoring`) from `pyproject.toml` in favor of OTLP gRPC exporter

## Implementation Details
The `extract_travel_intent()` function now performs basic pattern matching to extract dates from user input while preserving the raw input for the LLM's IntentClassifier to perform full semantic analysis. This maintains separation of concerns between heuristic extraction and intelligent classification.

title: Rewrite VM: Frame-based architecture, O(1) stack, 3.11-only, 41 differential tests
body:
Major architectural refactoring of the Python bytecode interpreter:

Stack discipline: Flip TOS from index 0 to index -1. All stack ops
are now O(1) — append/pop instead of insert(0)/pop(0). Fixes pre-existing
unary op stack leak (insert without pop).

Frame-based execution: Introduce Frame class with explicit frame stack.
CALL pushes a new Frame, RETURN_VALUE pops and resumes caller. Eliminates
host recursion for guest calls — guest call depth is no longer bounded by
Python's recursion limit.

VMFunction: Replace _mf_make() closure factory with VMFunction class that
stores code + defaults + closure metadata without creating a host callable.

Index-based fast locals: Frame.locals_fast is a list indexed by co_varnames
slot. LOAD_FAST/STORE_FAST use direct indexing, no name lookups.

IR via dis.get_instructions: Replace manual bytecode_optimize() with
dis.get_instructions(adaptive=False, show_caches=False). Handles
EXTENDED_ARG, CACHE slots, and jump target resolution automatically.

Correct 3.11 CALL protocol: _NULL sentinel (distinct from None) for
PUSH_NULL. CALL checks PEEK(argc+2) to determine method vs non-method
calls, matching CPython's ceval.c behavior.

Drop Python 2.7: Remove hasarg(), variable-width bytecode path, and all
2.7-only opcodes (PRINT_ITEM, SETUP_LOOP, CALL_FUNCTION variants, etc).

Exception handling: General try/except around opcode dispatch routes
exceptions through the 3.11 exception table.

Remove meta-circular hacks: No more py2vm name patching in STORE_NAME
or CALL. VMFunction/native callable distinction makes this unnecessary.

New opcodes: BUILD_SET, END_FOR, LIST_EXTEND, SET_UPDATE, SET_ADD,
LIST_TO_TUPLE, RAISE_VARARGS, BEFORE_WITH, DELETE_ATTR, DELETE_GLOBAL,
IMPORT_NAME, IMPORT_FROM, IMPORT_STAR. Tier 6 (generators/async) rejected
with NotImplementedError.

Testing: Add test_vm.py differential harness (41 tests) comparing VM
stdout against CPython across Tiers 1-5 — arithmetic, functions, closures,
classes, exceptions, comprehensions, imports, f-strings, etc.

title: fix(agents): add missing env vars to normalize_inputs step
body:
The normalize_inputs job was reading INPUT_* environment variables but they were never being set from the workflow inputs. This caused the source, raw_input, and source_url parameters to always be empty, breaking the text file input functionality.

Fixed by adding an env: section that maps the workflow inputs to the expected INPUT_* environment variables that the shell script reads.

Fixes the issue where neither Agent 63 workflow could use text files to add Issues.

title: [Aikido] Fix security issue in python-multipart via minor version upgrade from 0.0.6 to 0.0.18
body:
Upgrade python-multipart to fix ReDoS vulnerability in Content-Type parsing and DoS from malformed multipart boundaries.
⚠️ Breaking changes in this upgrade
All breaking changes by upgrading python-multipart from version 0.0.6 to 0.0.18 ([CHANGELOG](https://github.com/Kludex/python-multipart/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md)) | Version | Description | | --- | --- | | 
0.0.9
| Drop support for Python 3.7 | | 
0.0.13
| Rename import to `python_multipart` | | 
0.0.18
| Hard break if found data after last boundary on `MultipartParser` |
✅ 2 CVEs resolved by this upgrade
This PR will resolve the following CVEs: | Issue | Severity           | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 
[CVE-2024-24762](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/25487533/detail?groupId=79519#CVE-2024-24762)
| 
HIGH
| [python-multipart] A ReDoS vulnerability in HTTP Content-Type header parsing allows attackers to send malicious form data that causes excessive CPU consumption and indefinite stalling, preventing the server from handling additional requests. | | 
[AIKIDO-2024-10493](https://app.aikido.dev/issues/25487533/detail?groupId=79519#AIKIDO-2024-10493)
| 
LOW
| [python-multipart] A maliciously crafted multipart request with junk data after the boundary causes resource exhaustion, leading to Denial of Service. The server becomes unresponsive and unable to process further requests. |

title: Handle floating RRULE UNTIL for local calendar events
body:
### Motivation
- When converting event `DTSTART`/`DTEND` datetimes to floating local time in `_parse_event`, a timezone-aware `RRULE` `UNTIL` can remain timezone-aware and cause naive/aware comparison errors in `dateutil` during recurrence expansion, so `UNTIL` must be normalized to match.

### Description
- Added logic in `_parse_event` to detect when `EVENT_RRULE` is a `Recur` and convert a timezone-aware `rrule.until` to a floating local datetime using `dt_util.as_local(...).replace(tzinfo=None)`.
- Added an inline comment explaining why `UNTIL` must be made floating to match a floating `DTSTART`.

### Testing
- Ran `python -m compileall homeassistant/components/local_calendar/calendar.py`, which could not complete in this environment because the pinned `pyenv` Python `3.14.2` is not installed, so compilation could not be verified.
- No other automated tests were executed in this environment.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69d6aa8b27f483318c4b6ea10cf68937)

title: Handle malformed quoted chat commands
body:
### Motivation
- Prevent `soul chat` from crashing when an in-session slash command contains malformed or unterminated quotes (e.g. `/voice "`) by surfacing a user-friendly syntax error and continuing the session loop.

### Description
- Wrap `shlex.split(raw_input)` in a `try/except ValueError` inside `_handle_session_command` to print an `Invalid command syntax` message and return safely, and add a regression test `test_chat_handles_malformed_quoted_slash_command_without_crashing` in `tests/test_voice_cli.py` that reproduces the unterminated-quote case.

### Testing
- Ran `pytest -q tests/test_voice_cli.py tests/test_chat_save_boost.py tests/test_cli_contract.py` and the targeted tests passed (`9 passed`).
- Ran the full suite with `pytest -q` and all tests passed (`151 passed`).

------

title: Drop Python 2.7 support
body:
Python 2.7 has been EOL since January 2020. This removes compatibility shims, conditional imports, and version guards that existed solely to support Python 2.

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Drops Python 2.7 support and sets the minimum to Python 3.6. Removes compatibility shims, simplifies I/O and Tkinter code, and updates docs, packaging, and tests.

- **Refactors**
  - Removed __future__ imports, raw_input/input shims, and version guards across utilities.
  - Simplified Tkinter usage in guiconfig.py; use tkinter.*, iconphoto(True, …), and Text.replace().
  - Standardized open(..., encoding="utf-8") and removed universal newline hacks.
  - Deleted _IS_PY2 and Python 2 branches in kconfiglib; always use dict.values(), re.ASCII, and decode subprocess output.
  - Cleaned menuconfig and examples (no print_function, unified erasechar decoding).
  - Updated README/KBUILD to “Python 3.6+”, pruned Python 2 instructions.
  - setup.py: python_requires=">=3.6", removed Python 2 classifiers; setup.cfg no universal wheel.
  - tests: reltest runs only python3; testsuite unconditionally runs warn-undef checks.

- **Migration**
  - Use Python 3.6+ for all tools (menuconfig.py, guiconfig.py, scripts).
  - Update scripts/CI to invoke python3 instead of python2.
  - On Windows, install windows-curses for menuconfig; ensure Tkinter (python3-tk) is installed where needed.

<sup>Written for commit b6a23cef11a1c8d5e3da539a477f558e94f9ba3f. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

title: feat: parallelize per-repo parse_repo in analyze command
body:
## Summary

`analyze` コマンドでリポジトリ単位の `SourceParser.parse_repo()` を並列実行できるようにする。複数リポジトリ構成（例: backend + frontend + worker）で解析時間をリポ数に対してほぼリニアに短縮。

### 主要な変更

- `ThreadPoolExecutor` ベースの並列解析を追加（LLM 呼び出しは I/O バウンドなので threading で十分な効果）
- 新しい CLI オプション `--parallel/-j N`（デフォルト `0` = 自動: `min(4, pending_repo数)`）
- `all` コマンドにも `--parallel` を伝播
- 失敗したリポジトリは `completed_repos` に入れず、`--resume` で再試行可能
- チェックポイント schema は無変更（既存の `_checkpoint.json` はそのまま読める）

### アーキテクチャ

4 つの小さな helper に分解してテスタビリティを確保:

1. `_resolve_parallel(parallel, repo_count) -> int` — CLI 値を max_workers に解決、負数は `typer.BadParameter`
2. `_parse_single_repo(repo_path, parser) -> RepoParseResult` — 例外を catch して uniform な結果を返す worker
3. `_run_parallel_parse(pending_repos, parser, parallel, on_complete) -> (successes, failures)` — `ThreadPoolExecutor` + `as_completed` + コールバック
4. `RepoParseResult` dataclass — 成功・失敗を同型で扱うコンテナ（`analyzer/source_parser.py`）

共有状態の更新は `on_complete` コールバック経由でメインスレッドに集約してロックフリー。`all_contexts` は `ThreadPoolExecutor` の外で `config.repo_paths` 順に prebuild することで順序を保証。

### 参考ドキュメント

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-analyze-parallel-design.md`
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-11-analyze-parallel.md`

## Test plan

- [x] `tests/test_analyze_parallel.py` 新規追加（18 テスト: `RepoParseResult` 3 / `_resolve_parallel` 7 / `_parse_single_repo` 3 / `_run_parallel_parse` 5）
- [x] 既存テスト 13 件含め、計 31 テスト pass
- [x] `python main.py analyze --help` で `--parallel/-j` オプション表示確認
- [x] `python main.py all --help` で `--parallel/-j` オプション表示確認
- [x] 3 リポ `--parallel 2 --skip-llm` で並列実行（完了順が投入順と異なることを確認）
- [x] チェックポイント書き込み・`--resume` でスキップ動作確認
- [x] `--parallel -1` で `typer.BadParameter` + exit 2 を確認
- [ ] 実リポジトリ + 実 LLM での smoke test（レビュー後）

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes
