title: Port Drupalgeddon.py to Python 3
body:
### Motivation
- Make the legacy `Drupalgeddon.py` script run under Python 3 by replacing Python 2-only APIs and addressing bytes/str differences used during hashing and HTTP requests.
- Fix password hash generation and base64 conversion so the Drupal password hashing helper works correctly with Python 3 byte digests.

### Description
- Updated shebang to `#!/usr/bin/env python3` and replaced `urllib2` with `urllib.request` and `urllib.error` for HTTP operations.
- Converted the `DrupalHash` helper to explicitly encode strings to bytes before hashing and adjusted the `custom64` routine to operate on byte values instead of using `ord` on str.
- Encoded the POST payload with `data=...encode('utf-8')`, decode HTTP responses safely with `.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')`, and modernized print statements to Python 3 syntax.
- Minor refactors and naming cleanups including renaming `hash` to `password_hash`, `UA` to `ua`, and using `.format()` for the `post_data` construction.

### Testing
- Ran `python3 -m py_compile Drupalgeddon.py` which completed successfully.
- Executed `python3 Drupalgeddon.py` which ran under Python 3 and displayed usage/banner output as expected.

------

body:
Bumps [python-dotenv](https://github.com/theskumar/python-dotenv) from 1.2.1 to 1.2.2.
Release notes

Sourced from python-dotenv's releases.

v1.2.2

Added

  • Support for Python 3.14, including the free-threaded (3.14t) build. (#)

Changed

  • The dotenv run command now forwards flags directly to the specified command by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#607
  • Improved documentation clarity regarding override behavior and the reference page.
  • Updated PyPy support to version 3.11.
  • Documentation for FIFO file support.
  • Support for Python 3.9.

Fixed

  • Improved set_key and unset_key behavior when interacting with symlinks by @​bbc2 in #790c5
  • Corrected the license specifier and added missing Python 3.14 classifiers in package metadata by @​JYOuyang in theskumar/python-dotenv#590

Breaking Changes

  • dotenv.set_key and dotenv.unset_key used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case. For that behavior to be restored in all cases, follow_symlinks=True should be used.

  • In the CLI, set and unset used to follow symlinks in some situations. This is no longer the case.

  • dotenv.set_key, dotenv.unset_key and the CLI commands set and unset used to reset the file mode of the modified .env file to 0o600 in some situations. This is no longer the case: The original mode of the file is now preserved. Is the file needed to be created or wasn't a regular file, mode 0o600 is used.

Misc

  • skip 000 permission tests for root user by @​burnout-projects in theskumar/python-dotenv#561
  • Bump actions/checkout from 5 to 6 in the github-actions group by @​dependabot[bot] in theskumar/python-dotenv#593
  • Add Windows testing to CI by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#604
  • Improve workflow efficiency with best practices by @​theskumar in theskumar/python-dotenv#609
  • Remove the use of sh in tests by @​bbc2 in theskumar/python-dotenv#612

title: Add IoT sensor failure prediction ML system
body:
Manufacturing equipment telemetry (temperature, vibration, pressure, RPM, voltage, error codes) requires a dedicated ML pipeline to predict machine failures before they occur. This adds a self-contained `src/iot/` module alongside the existing NYC Taxi pipeline.

## New module: `src/iot/`

- **`sensor_data.py`** — validates raw telemetry: out-of-bounds clamp → NaN, median imputation for sensor drift/gaps, UTC timestamp coercion, `add_failure_label()` that looks ahead `horizon_minutes` per machine, synthetic data generator for tests/demos
- **`features.py`** — per-machine rolling feature engineering over 10-min / 30-min / 1-h windows:
  - rolling mean, std, min, max, variance
  - OLS gradient (trend) on 10-min window
  - spike indicator: `value > μ + 3σ` within window
  - cyclic hour encoding (sin/cos), error code lag + rolling count
- **`train.py`** — trains Random Forest or Gradient Boosting with MLflow logging, saves `.pkl` + feature schema JSON; ships a CLI entry point

```bash
# Train on real data
python -m src.iot.train --data sensors.csv --algo rf

# Or use built-in synthetic data
python -m src.iot.train --algo gb
```

## API: `/predict/sensor-failure`

New POST endpoint on `api/main.py` accepting a batch of `SensorReading` objects. Returns `failure_probability` + `will_fail` flag per reading, with a heuristic fallback (error-code-based score) when no trained model is present. Prometheus metrics wired in.

```json
POST /predict/sensor-failure
{"readings": [{"timestamp": "2026-03-12T11:05:21Z", "machine_id": "machine_27",
               "temperature": 84.3, "vibration": 0.02, "pressure": 101.3,
               "rpm": 3500, "voltage": 220, "error_code": 0}]}
```

title: v0.3 Memory Matures: Tier 2 complete (F7-F12)
body:
## Summary

Ships the complete Tier 2 milestone in one drop — 6 architectural features, 178 new tests, 0 new pip deps, full backward compat.

| # | Feature | Key modules |
|---|---------|-------------|
| F7 | 4-tier memory consolidation w/ decay | `memory/consolidator.py`, `memory/decay.py` |
| F8 | Contradiction detection + supersession | `memory/contradiction.py`, `store.record_decision_checked` |
| F9 | Time-travel snapshots | `snapshots/manager.py`, `scripts/snapshot-guard.py` |
| F10 | Learned workspace profiles | `memory/profiler.py` |
| F11 | Cross-workspace pattern detection | `memory/pattern_detector.py`, `scripts/pattern-cron.py` |
| F12 | Inter-workspace signals (pub/sub) | `memory/signals.py` |

### Shared primitives (new)
`migration.py` (safe ALTER helper), `decay.py` (Ebbinghaus), `query_helpers.py` (cosine + top-k), `EmbeddingService.embed_batch_from_db`.

### Schema changes (all backward compatible)
- `sessions.memory_tier` (working/episodic)
- `decisions.contradiction_score`
- new `semantic_memories`, `procedural_memories` tables
- new `signals` table in `meta.db`
- All via `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` + OperationalError catch; existing dbs migrate on first open.

### Integration
- **session-preamble**: snapshot on auto-switch; injects semantic facts, workspace profile, pending signals
- **session-end**: `maybe_compact()`, profile regen when stale, fires `pattern-cron.py` weekly
- **memory-bridge**: new `signal` / `read_signals` event types; decision handler now uses `record_decision_checked`
- **hooks.json**: new PreToolUse matcher for `Write` -> `snapshot-guard.py`

### New user skills
`compact-memory`, `decision-history`, `rollback`, `profile-refresh`, `cross-patterns`, `emit-signal`

### Test results
60 -> **238 passing, 0 failing**. Zero regressions.

### Build method
Multi-agent parallel build: Wave 1 (foundation) sequential -> Wave 2 (6 features in parallel) -> Wave 3 (sequential integration) -> Wave 4 (verify + ship).

## Test plan
- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -q` -> 238 passed
- [x] `python -c "import json; json.load(open('hooks/hooks.json'))"` valid
- [x] All new modules import cleanly
- [ ] Human smoke: run a real session, verify PreCompact + snapshot + signal flows end-to-end
- [ ] Verify migration on an existing v0.2 memory.db (auto ALTER should be a no-op on re-open)

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)
- Tier 3 features (F13-F20) — session threads, billing, weekly summaries, environments, vault, deploy linking, inbox, NL router
- Port v0.3 into the local runtime `C:\Tentaqles\.tentaqles\` (still pinned to v0.2)
- LLM-based contradiction confirmation (full scope of F8)
- Weekly cron scheduling for `pattern-cron.py` and `compaction-cron.py`

title: feat: CRUD for documents, components, milestones, and templates
body:
## Summary

Extends the CLI with four new entity types, each with its own storage quirks.

- `huly documents list/get/create/update/describe` — documents live in teamspaces (not projects); content is a blob ref accessed via Collaborator RPC with field `"content"`
- `huly components list/get/create/update` — project-scoped, optional `--lead` with fuzzy person match
- `huly milestones list/get/create/update` — project-scoped, `--target-date YYYY-MM-DD` parsing, status enum (planned/in-progress/completed)
- `huly templates list/get/describe` — descriptions are inline ProseMirror JSON (not blob refs), so reads/writes go through `TxUpdateDoc` directly; safety check prevents corruption if a blob ref is detected
- Collaborator RPC generalized to `get_content(class_id, object_id, field, blob_ref)` — works for any entity, not just issues

## Context

- Template `describe --set` includes a safety check: if the description field looks like a blob ref (`*-description-*` pattern), it aborts rather than corrupting data by overwriting the ref with raw JSON
- `get_description`/`set_description` remain as convenience wrappers for backward compatibility
- Components and milestones are currently empty in the EFS workspace but the API classes are verified queryable

title: hi
Fix ImportError by replacing distlib.compat.raw_input with built-in input()
body:
## Problem

When installing `django-tailwind` without optional dependencies, users encounter an `ImportError`:

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'distlib'

This occurs because:
- `distlib` is not listed as a required dependency in `pyproject.toml` (only `django` and
`pytailwindcss` are)
- `distlib` is only available as a transitive dependency through the optional `cookiecutter` package
(`cookiecutter` → `virtualenv` → `distlib`)
- The management command imports `from distlib.compat import raw_input` at the module level

I encountered this issue when building a Docker image with `pip install django-tailwind`, which
doesn't include optional dependencies.

## Solution

Since `django-tailwind` requires Python 3.11+ (as specified in `pyproject.toml`), we can safely use
Python 3's built-in `input()` function instead of the Python 2/3 compatibility wrapper.

**Changes made:**
- Removed `from distlib.compat import raw_input` import
- Replaced all 3 occurrences of `raw_input()` with `input()`

In Python 3, `input()` behaves identically to Python 2's `raw_input()` (returns a string without
evaluation).

## Testing

- ✅ All existing tests pass (`uv run pytest`)
- ✅ Code formatted with ruff
- ✅ Verified the import no longer depends on an unlisted dependency

This fix ensures users can install and use `django-tailwind` with only the required dependencies
listed in `pyproject.toml`.

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @​aminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @​E8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960
  • @​mitre88 made their first contribution in psf/requests#7244
  • @​magsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @​Rohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25

v2.32.5

2.32.5 (2025-08-18)

Bugfixes

  • The SSLContext caching feature originally introduced in 2.32.0 has created a new class of issues in Requests that have had negative impact across a number of use cases. The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Dropped support for Python 3.8 following its end of support.

title: Phase 2: Port upstream modules from autostock
body:
## Summary
- Port and genericize 8 modules from the autostock trading platform
- All trading-specific content removed (no stock symbols, market hours, trading actions, default trading capabilities)
- New modules: activation, capabilities, adaptive weights, debate orchestrator, gateway (Redis Streams), sessions, mDNS discovery

## New modules

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `agents/activation` | Event-driven and schedule-based agent activation control |
| `agents/capabilities` | Agent skill registration and discovery |
| `consensus/weights` | Performance-based adaptive weight management |
| `debate/orchestrator` | Structured agent disagreement resolution |
| `gateway/messages` | AgentMessage dataclass and metrics |
| `gateway/gateway` | Redis Streams message bus with fail-open semantics |
| `gateway/sessions` | Session management helpers (list, history, send, challenge) |
| `discovery/mdns` | mDNS service advertising via zeroconf |

## Test plan
- [x] `trunk check --all` passes with zero issues
- [x] Zero references to autostock, trading, market_hours, TSLA, BUY, SELL
- [ ] CI passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: feat: mount Slack on FastAPI, add file extraction and document tools
body:
## Summary

Mounts the Slack bot directly into the FastAPI app (previously standalone Socket Mode only), enabling deployment on Vercel and other FaaS platforms. Also adds file extraction from sandboxes and pre-installs document processing tools.

### New capabilities
- **FastAPI mount** (`slack_routes.py`) — Slack Bolt runs as a sub-app on `/slack/events` with `process_before_response=True` for FaaS compatibility
- **File extraction** — Agent-generated files are diffed against the initial sandbox state, extracted, and uploaded back to the Slack thread (up to 10 files, 25 MB each, 50 MB total)
- **File upload limit** bumped from 10 MB to 50 MB for incoming Slack files
- **Document tools** — Sandbox template ships with `poppler-utils`, `qpdf`, `pypdf`, `pdfplumber`, `reportlab`, and `markitdown`

### Config changes
- `timeout` and `system_prompt_append` moved from env vars (`SANDSTORM_SLACK_MODEL`, `SANDSTORM_SLACK_TIMEOUT`) to `sandstorm.json` — single source of truth for all entry points
- `system_prompt_append` supports both string and dict prompt formats
- `LINEAR_API_KEY` forwarded to sandbox when present (for MCP integrations)

### Fixes
- Prevent double run on sandbox reuse failure (generate fresh `run_id` on retry)
- Remove unauthenticated early returns in Slack event handling
- Slack deps (`slack-bolt`, `slack-sdk`, `aiohttp`) moved to core — required for Vercel where optional extras aren't installed

### Deployment
- Vercel `maxDuration` bumped to 300s
- Routes consolidated for the new `/slack/events` endpoint

## Test plan
- [x] `test_slack_routes.py` — event dispatch, file handling, error responses
- [x] `test_sandbox_skills.py` — system prompt append for string/dict prompts
- [x] `test_slack.py` — updated for config refactor
- [x] All 154 tests pass, lint and format clean

title: Add Wikipedia command, structured logging, proper error handling, and message splitting to GopalBot
body:
GopalBot used a bare `discord.Client` with `print()` logging, silent 2000-char response truncation, no prefix commands, and no Wikipedia support. This rewrites `bot.py` to a production-ready structure.

## Core changes

- **`discord.ext.commands.Bot`** — migrated from raw client; all features now use `@bot.command` decorators with docstrings surfaced via `!help`
- **`!wiki <query>`** — Wikipedia integration returning a 3-sentence summary as a rich embed (title, URL, footer); handles `DisambiguationError` and `PageError` with user-friendly messages
- **`!ask <question>`** — explicit prefix command for Grok AI queries, decoupled from mention/name/DM triggers (which are preserved)
- **Message splitting** — replaces `reply[:1997] + "..."` with `split_message()` that breaks on newlines and sends multiple messages
- **Structured logging** — `logging.basicConfig` with timestamp + level + logger name, replacing all `print()` calls
- **Error handling** — `on_command_error` for `MissingRequiredArgument` (names the missing param), `CommandNotFound` (echoes the attempted command), and a generic fallback; env var validation raises `EnvironmentError` at startup if either token is absent
- **Bot presence** — sets "Listening to !help" on `on_ready`
- **Case-insensitive name stripping** — uses `re.sub(..., flags=re.IGNORECASE)` instead of two sequential `.replace()` calls

## Files changed

- `bot.py` — full rewrite
- `requirements.txt` — adds `wikipedia==1.4.0`
- `README.md` — updated feature list, commands table, dependencies
- `.gitignore` — added (excludes `__pycache__`, `*.pyc`, `.env`)

```python
# Before: silent truncation
if len(reply) > 2000:
    reply = reply[:1997] + "..."

# After: split at newlines, send multiple messages
def split_message(text: str, limit: int = DISCORD_MAX_LENGTH) -> list[str]:
    chunks = []
    while len(text) > limit:
        split_at = text.rfind("\n", 0, limit)
        if split_at == -1:
            split_at = limit
        chunks.append(text[:split_at])
        text = text[split_at:].lstrip("\n")
    if text:
        chunks.append(text)
    return chunks
```

title: Bump pyasn1 from 0.4.2 to 0.6.3
body:
Bumps [pyasn1](https://github.com/pyasn1/pyasn1) from 0.4.2 to 0.6.3.
Release notes

Sourced from pyasn1's releases.

Release 0.6.3

It's a minor release.

  • Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (CVE-2026-30922).
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field.
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes.
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.2

It's a minor release.

  • Fixed continuation octet limits in OID/RELATIVE-OID decoder (CVE-2026-23490).
  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Added SECURITY.md policy.
  • Migrated to pyproject.toml packaging.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for Python 3.13.
  • Cleaned Python 2-related code.
  • Removed bdist_wheel universal flag from setup.cfg.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.0

It's a major release where we drop Python 2 support entirely. The most significant changes are:

  • Removed support for EOL Python 2.7, 3.6, 3.7
  • Added support for previously missing RELATIVE-OID construct
  • Updated link to Layman's Guide

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.5.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for PyPy 3.10 and Python 3.12
  • Updated RTD configuration to include a dummy index.rst redirecting to contents.html, ensuring compatibility with third-party documentation and search indexes.
  • Fixed the API breakage wih decoder.decode(substrateFun=...). A substrateFun passed to decoder.decode() can now be either v0.4 Non-Streaming or v0.5 Streaming. pyasn1 will detect and handle both cases transparently. A substrateFun passed to one of the new streaming decoders is still expected to be v0.5 Streaming only.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from pyasn1's changelog.

Revision 0.6.3, released 16-03-2026

  • CVE-2026-30922 (GHSA-jr27-m4p2-rc6r): Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (thanks for reporting, romanticpragmatism)
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field [issue #54](pyasn1/pyasn1#54) [pr #100](pyasn1/pyasn1#100)
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes [issue #86](pyasn1/pyasn1#86) [pr #101](pyasn1/pyasn1#101)
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing [issue #81](pyasn1/pyasn1#81) [pr #102](pyasn1/pyasn1#102)

Revision 0.6.2, released 16-01-2026

  • CVE-2026-23490 (GHSA-63vm-454h-vhhq): Fixed continuation octet limits in OID/RELATIVE-OID decoder (thanks to tsigouris007)
  • Added support for Python 3.14 [pr #97](pyasn1/pyasn1#97)
  • Added SECURITY.md policy
  • Fixed unit tests failing due to missing code [issue #91](pyasn1/pyasn1#91) [pr #92](pyasn1/pyasn1#92)
  • Migrated to pyproject.toml packaging [pr #90](pyasn1/pyasn1#90)

Revision 0.6.1, released 10-09-2024

  • Added support for Python 3.13 and updated GitHub Actions [pr #73](pyasn1/pyasn1#73)
  • Removed Python 2 support and related code [pr #62](pyasn1/pyasn1#62) [pr #61](pyasn1/pyasn1#61) [pr #60](pyasn1/pyasn1#60)
  • Improved error handling and consistency [pr #71](pyasn1/pyasn1#71) [pr #70](pyasn1/pyasn1#70)
  • Runtime deprecation of tagMap and typeMap aliases [pr #72](pyasn1/pyasn1#72)

  • Cleaned documentation and comments [pr #63](pyasn1/pyasn1#63)
  • Removed bdist_wheel universal flag from setup.cfg [pr #69](pyasn1/pyasn1#69)

title: Bump protobuf from 6.33.2 to 6.33.5 in /python
body:
Bumps [protobuf](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf) from 6.33.2 to 6.33.5.
Release notes

Sourced from protobuf's releases.

Protocol Buffers v34.0-rc1

Announcements

  • This version includes breaking changes to: C++, Objective-C, PHP, Python.
  • [Bazel] Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • [C++] Make generator headers private (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3a2af3510f0d454dbe3e4dc281674b61c4d20b9e)
  • [C++] Add a debug check that the target of CopyFrom is not a descendant of the source. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/7a7589823d2cfaaf7994b050e98d5d553bc9b1c1)
  • [C++] Add [[nodiscard]] to many APIs. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/a70115f33f9af2c4b2202c800b84837e7fe0d738)
  • [C++] Make the arena-enabled constructors of RepeatedField, RepeatedPtrField, and Map private. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ef890c3d0c79398c70e047fe5dd893f460ba2336)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [C++] Removes proto2::util::MessageDifferencer::AddIgnoreCriteria that takes a raw pointer as an argument in favor of the overload that takes a unique_ptr. Remove macro PROTOBUF_FUTURE_REMOVE_ADD_IGNORE_CRITERIA (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b115358c64127896fed88b8b5ef5d91d86d8cbae)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::has_optional_keyword() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or has_presence() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/68346ec9348e932664e58c3ecdcd1478f95233a8)
  • [C++] Remove AddUnusedImportTrackFile() and ClearUnusedImportTrackFiles(). Remove PROTOBUF_FUTURE_RENAME_ADD_UNUSED_IMPORT (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/837a2cd1d6c75402b2503ffe7cd8aeaf25868536)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::is_optional() in OSS. Use (!is_required() && !is_repeated()) instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9dbc5d479a8e453921485d8d3de47fb3c005f1af)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • [C++] All entity names have length limit (2afb0dc)
  • [ObjC] Remove generate_minimal_imports generation option warning (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/45b1297fdaad5a9436d0e207422168c38dc45ac4)
  • [ObjC] Fix nullability annotations on some GPB*Dictionary types. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ea67d6d26a48478a567c404679e3bb99cf230d50)
  • [ObjC] Remove -[GPBFieldDescriptor optional] (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3414dc151eb4dcbdb2ca952e2589993bf7af75c4)
  • [Other] Remove deprecated flag for enabling MSVC support (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/97c979be6e0907e1051bee62584dac4594e73fa7)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9c45014099a4f7004fab6dd1278de2f4f2a393c5)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs FieldDescriptor getLabel, use IsRepeated or isRequired instead. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cd76e675b14d00dda5623b30835d2bc7105fccc6, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345)
  • [PHP] Add PHP typehints for setters and remove redundant GPBUtil checks (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25296) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/aee03b78929c02461a5f9d8e136a2a016359b0cd)
  • [PHP] support default values for editions/proto2 (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25161) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b01099d56350551bae3da88b97bf3027274c9f17)
  • [Python] Raise errors in OSS when assign bool to int/enum field in Python Proto. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/5b116fe2f14f49dd0cc3b76089983717f211025c)
  • [Python] Remove float_format/double_format from python proto text_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/e4854a186e0bfa867d5bfa5cd850608a948fd488)
  • [Python] Raise TypeError when convert non-timedelta to Duration, or convert non-datetime to Timestamp in python proto. (Original code may raise ArributeError) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/00aaca1b4d98954bc2933d7c8a5379ba6088124c)
  • [Python] Remove float_precision from python proto json_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f027f1fcd52b9d080b7ee79f4024f53cf54e0dc5)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)

  • [Python] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)

C++

title: feat: add Prometheus metrics and Grafana monitoring
body:
## Summary
- Add `prometheus_client` dependency and expose `/metrics` Prometheus endpoint on port 8201
- Instrument all MCP tool functions, upload processing, RAG/LLM helpers, and MinIO storage operations with counters, histograms, and gauges
- 12 new tests covering metric increments across all instrumented layers (27 total tests pass)

## Metrics exposed
- `pageindex_tool_calls_total`, `pageindex_tool_errors_total`, `pageindex_tool_duration_seconds` (by tool)
- `pageindex_uploads_total` (by status), `pageindex_upload_duration_seconds`, `pageindex_active_uploads`
- `pageindex_rag_searches_total`, `pageindex_rag_duration_seconds`, `pageindex_llm_calls_total`, `pageindex_llm_duration_seconds`
- `pageindex_minio_operations_total`, `pageindex_minio_duration_seconds` (by operation)
- `pageindex_documents_total`

## Infra changes (separate repo)
Prometheus scrape job and Grafana dashboard JSON already added to `hetzner-deployment-service` configmap.

## Test plan
- [x] All 27 tests pass (`uv run pytest tests/ -v`)
- [x] All 14 custom metric names present in `/metrics` output
- [x] `uv lock --check` confirms lock file is up to date
- [ ] Deploy to staging and verify Prometheus scrapes `/metrics` successfully
- [ ] Verify Grafana dashboard loads with live data

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds end-to-end Prometheus metrics for PageIndex MCP and exposes a `/metrics` endpoint on port 8201 for scraping. Integrates with the existing Grafana stack for visibility into tools, uploads, RAG/LLM, and MinIO.

- **New Features**
  - Expose `/metrics` via `prometheus_client` on the Starlette app.
  - Instrumentation added for: MCP tools (calls, errors, duration), uploads (status, duration, active), RAG/LLM (calls/searches, duration), MinIO (ops, duration), and a documents gauge.
  - 12 new tests for metric increments; all 27 tests pass.
  - Implementation plan and design spec added under `docs/superpowers/`. Prometheus scrape job and Grafana dashboard JSON are provisioned in the infra repo.

- **Dependencies**
  - Added `prometheus_client` to `pyproject.toml`.

<sup>Written for commit ea7cdb42a3108c03070ab6dc8415a972ae639342. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

title: Python 3.10+ Migration (Phases 1-4)
body:
## Overview

This PR implements **Phases 1-4** of the comprehensive Python 3.10+ migration plan for Trigger, upgrading from the legacy Python 2.7 codebase to modern Python 3.10+.

## 🎯 Migration Scope

- **Target:** Python 3.10+ (released Oct 2021)
- **Current Phase:** 4 of 12 phases complete
- **Files Modified:** 115+ files
- **Commits:** 3 clean, focused commits

## ✅ Completed Work

### Phase 1: Foundation Setup
- ✨ Created modern `pyproject.toml` to replace `setup.py`
- 📦 Updated all dependencies to Python 3 compatible versions:
  - Twisted: 15.x → 22.10.0+
  - cryptography: 1.4 → 41.0.0+
  - crochet: 1.5.0 → 2.0.0+
  - pyparsing: ~2.2.0 → 3.1.0+
  - redis: → 5.0.0+
- 🏷️ Created backup tag `v1.6.0-py2-final`

### Phase 2: Automated Syntax Migration  
- 🔧 Ran `pyupgrade --py310-plus` on entire codebase
- 🖨️ Converted **200+ print statements** to `print()` functions
- 📚 Fixed **37 dictionary methods** (`.iteritems()` → `.items()`, etc.)
- ⚠️ Fixed **14 old exception syntax** (`except X, e:` → `except X as e:`)
- 🔢 Fixed **15 `.has_key()` calls** to `in` operator
- 📝 Fixed **2 xrange** → `range`
- 📂 Fixed **4 file()** → `open()`
- 🎨 Applied **black** formatting for consistent code style

### Phase 3: Manual Syntax Fixes
- 🔤 Fixed **basestring** → `str` (3 instances)
- ⌨️ Fixed **raw_input()** → `input()` (3 instances)  
- 🔄 Fixed **reload()** → `importlib.reload()`
- 📥 Fixed **StringIO imports** (5 files)
- ⚙️ Fixed **ConfigParser** → `configparser`
- 🔗 Fixed **urlparse** → `urllib.parse`
- 🎒 Fixed **cPickle** → `pickle`
- 🔧 Fixed **itertools.izip_longest** → `zip_longest`
- 📖 Fixed **UserDict.DictMixin** → `collections.abc.MutableMapping`
- ∞ Fixed **sys.maxint** → `sys.maxsize`
- 🆎 Added **unicode compatibility** for Python 3

### Phase 4: Update Vendored Packages
- 📦 **peewee:** v2.1.4 (2013) → v3.17.0 (Python 3 compatible)
  - Removed deprecated `threadlocals` parameter
  - Updated `trigger/acl/models.py` for compatibility
- 📡 **tftpy:** Fixed all Python 2 syntax issues
  - Fixed **30+ old raise syntax** statements
  - Fixed **6 old exception syntax** instances
  - Fixed **6 dict.has_key()** calls
  - Fixed **types.IntType** references
  - All files now compile with Python 3

## 🔍 Key Changes

### Build System Modernization
- Replaced `setup.py` with modern `pyproject.toml`
- CLI tools will use entry points (configured, not yet migrated)
- Removed deprecated test commands

### Syntax Conversions
```python
# Print statements
print "hello"  →  print("hello")
print >>sys.stderr, "error"  →  print("error", file=sys.stderr)

# Dictionary methods
for k, v in dict.iteritems():  →  for k, v in dict.items():
if dict.has_key('x'):  →  if 'x' in dict:

# Exceptions
except Error, e:  →  except Error as e:
raise Error, "msg"  →  raise Error("msg")

# Builtins
raw_input("prompt")  →  input("prompt")
basestring  →  str
file('path')  →  open('path')

# Imports
from StringIO import StringIO  →  from io import StringIO
import ConfigParser  →  import configparser
from urlparse import parse  →  from urllib.parse import parse

# Keywords
async = value  →  async_result = value  # 'async' is reserved in Python 3.7+

# Octal literals
os.chmod(path, 0644)  →  os.chmod(path, 0o644)
```

## ⏭️ Remaining Phases (5-12)

**Not included in this PR:**
- Phase 5: Convert bin/* scripts to trigger.bin package
- Phase 6: Migrate test suite to pytest
- Phase 7: Update dependencies and test compatibility
- Phase 8: Fix string/bytes handling issues
- Phase 9: Setup CI/CD with GitHub Actions
- Phase 10: Update documentation
- Phase 11: Run full test suite and verify
- Phase 12: Final cleanup and release preparation

## 🧪 Testing Status

- ✅ All modified files compile with Python 3.10+
- ✅ Vendored packages (peewee, tftpy) verified to import successfully
- ✅ Black formatting applied and passes
- ⚠️ Full test suite not yet run (Phase 11)
- ⚠️ String/bytes handling not yet audited (Phase 8)

## 📋 Review Checklist

- [ ] Review syntax conversion changes
- [ ] Verify pyproject.toml configuration
- [ ] Check vendored package updates (peewee v2→v3 has breaking changes)
- [ ] Decide whether to proceed with Phases 5-12 or iterate on Phases 1-4

## 🔗 Related

- Migration plan: See `CLAUDE.md` for detailed documentation
- Backup point: Tag `v1.6.0-py2-final` for Python 2.7 version

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes

This PR introduces **breaking changes** and requires Python 3.10+:
- Python 2.7 support completely removed
- Peewee ORM upgraded from v2 to v3 (API changes)
- All CLI tools require Python 3.10+ interpreter

---

**Note:** This is a work-in-progress migration. After review and approval of Phases 1-4, we can proceed with the remaining phases or iterate based on feedback.

title: feat: add extra_commands support to presets with odoo_version markers
body:
We have to handle a particular packaging case for old odoo.
- `vatnumber` is needed for odoo <= 13.0, see [here](https://github.com/odoo/odoo/blob/13.0/requirements.txt#L40)
- `vatnumber` package needs `2to3` to build which has been removed from recent setuptools
- in the past we had pinned "setuptools<58" as a workaround:
   - in [oca ci](https://github.com/OCA/maintainer-quality-tools/pull/695)
   - in [emoi](https://gitlab.trobz.com/packages/emoi/-/blob/py3/emoi/utils/helper.py#L322)
- then on oca ci, the workaround was moved to a dedicated container for legacy odoo: see [here](https://github.com/OCA/oca-ci/pull/98)

This PR is an attempt at handling it properly in odoo-venv: 

## Summary
- Add `extra_commands` field to presets.toml to run additional commands at specific stages during venv creation
- Commands can be conditional based on `odoo_version` markers (e.g., `odoo_version <= '13.0'`)
- Support for environment variables in commands (e.g., `UV_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION=1`)
- Verbose output shows condition and environment for each executed command

## Details
The new `extra_commands` feature allows running arbitrary commands at three stages:
- `after_venv` - after virtual environment creation
- `after_requirements` - after installing all requirements
- `after_odoo_install` - after installing Odoo in editable mode

Each command can have:
- `command`: list of command arguments
- `when`: marker expression (supports `odoo_version` and standard packaging markers)
- `stage`: one of the three stages above
- `env`: optional dict of environment variables

## Concretely for `vatnumber` 
```toml
[[common.extra_commands]]
command = ["uv", "pip", "install", "setuptools<58.0"]
when = "odoo_version <= '13.0'"
stage = "after_requirements"

[[common.extra_commands]]
command = ["uv", "pip", "install", "vatnumber==1.2"]
when = "odoo_version <= '13.0'"
stage = "after_requirements"
env = { UV_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION = "1" }
```

title: feat: auto-handle packages requiring --no-build-isolation
body:
## Summary

Moves hardcoded Odoo compatibility workarounds from `presets.toml` into the core `create_odoo_venv` logic, so they apply automatically regardless of preset.

### `_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION_PACKAGES`

A new dict mapping package names to an optional when-marker. When any such package is found in **any** requirement source (Odoo's `requirements.txt`, addons dirs, user sources, manifests) and its when-marker evaluates to True, it is automatically:
- excluded from the batch `uv pip install -r` run
- installed afterwards with `--no-build-isolation`

| Package | Condition |
|---|---|
| `vatnumber` | `odoo_version <= '13.0'` |
| `suds-jurko` | `odoo_version <= '13.0'` |
| `rfc6266-parser` | always (when present in any source) |

### Legacy build tools for Odoo <= 13.0

`setuptools<58.0` and `wheel` are now installed automatically when `odoo_version <= '13.0'` (setuptools 58 dropped 2to3 support required by vatnumber and suds-jurko). Runs before the `--no-build-isolation` installs so the build tools are ready.

### Preset cleanup

Removes from `presets.toml [common]`:
- `vatnumber` and `suds-jurko` from `ignore_from_odoo_requirements`
- `extra_commands` entries for vatnumber, suds-jurko, and `setuptools<58.0 wheel`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: fix(launcher): bypass broken shebang by invoking bin/odoo through python
body:
## Root Cause

`uv pip install -e` writes a broken shebang in `bin/odoo` pointing to a temporary build directory (e.g. `~/.cache/uv/builds-v0/.tmpdJaf0T/bin/python`) that is deleted after installation. The launcher script called `odoo` directly, hitting this broken interpreter.

## Fix

Replace `CMD=("odoo")` with `CMD=("python" "$VENV_DIR/bin/odoo")` in the launcher template. This runs the existing `bin/odoo` script through the venv's Python interpreter, bypassing the broken shebang while preserving each Odoo version's own import logic (which differs between v12 and v19).

title: fix(presets): pin psycopg2 to 2.8.3 for Odoo 12 on Python < 3.8
body: psycopg2==2.7.3.1 (shipped by Odoo 12) uses Python 2's `basestring`, causing a NameError on Python 3.

title: Bump requests from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0 in the pip group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the pip group with 1 update in the / directory: [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests). Updates `requests` from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865

WARNING: this is a security update.

Using a lot of recursively nested use tags could lead to long rendering times with relatively small inputs. CairoSVG now stops rendering when more than 100k use tags are rendered.

Using the --unsafe option allows to render larger documents.

title: docs: Add environment setup guide and fix README parity for 53-lesson curriculum
body:
Both READMEs lacked onboarding guidance for absolute beginners and were out of sync with the current 53-lesson curriculum (Chinese README said 52, English had duplicate Lesson 47–52 entries, neither listed Lesson 53).

## Changes

**Both READMEs**
- Added `🚀 Environment Setup` section covering Python installation (Windows/macOS/Linux), VS Code + Python/Pylance extensions, course download (ZIP vs git clone), and optional `uv` venv setup
- Added beginner tips: type code by hand, read error messages, experiment freely, use AI as a tutor
- Updated "Getting Started" to use correct directory names (`lesson_01_intro_to_python`), note `python3` on macOS/Linux, and mention `solution.py`
- Added Lesson 53 (Python for MCP and Skills)

**README.md (English)**
- Removed duplicate Lesson 47–52 block left over from a prior edit

**README_zh.md (Chinese)**
- Fixed lesson count 52 → 53
- Added link back to English README

title: fix: Remove Python 2 compat, fix bugs, modernize idioms
body:
## Summary

Comprehensive cleanup pass removing Python 2 compatibility code and fixing longstanding bugs across the Python client.

## Changes
- **Python 2 removal**: Remove all sys.version_info guards, raw_input shim
- **Bug fixes**: Fix print(print()) double-call, deprecated np.float, undefined parser.error, docstring-as-attribute in write_port, mutable default args, type() anti-pattern, duplicate get_camera_num, unused variables, debug print left in get_obj_scale
- **Modernization**: Remove old-style (object) bases, replace assert 0 with proper exceptions, replace bare except with except Exception, replace print warnings with logging, fix devnull file leak, add __all__

title: Update main fork or stochastic-benchmark from updates of local development
body:
## Summary

Major update bringing CI infrastructure, pandas/numpy compatibility, documentation improvements, new examples, and the initial extraction of experiment classes.

---

### CI & Build Infrastructure
- **GitHub Actions CI pipeline** (`ci.yml`): test matrix across Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 with unit tests, integration tests, and coverage reporting
- **Dependency management**: `requirements.txt`, `requirements-dev.txt`, `requirements-examples.txt` aligned; `setup.py` updated with `python_requires=">=3.10"` and full `install_requires`
- **Conda environment**: `environment-ci.yml` for reproducible local CI testing
- **Helper scripts**: `Makefile`, `run-ci-tests.sh`, `setup-ci-env.sh`, `test-all-python-versions.sh`, `quick-reference.sh`
- **CI documentation**: `CI-TESTING.md` with detailed testing guidelines

### Pandas 2.x / NumPy 2.x Compatibility
- `df.iteritems()` replaced with `df.items()` in `src/utils_ws.py` (removed in pandas 2.0)
- `np.issubdtype()` replaced with `pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype()` in `src/utils_ws.py` (handles pandas `StringDtype`)
- `include_groups=False` added to `groupby().apply()` calls in `src/cross_validation.py`
- `networkx>=3.0` (lowered from 3.5 for Python 3.10 compat)
- Removed `iteritems` monkeypatches from 7 test files

### Source Code Changes
- **`src/bootstrap.py`**: Refactored `BootstrapParameters.update_rule` to use explicit 2-arg signature `(self, df)` with `default_update` classmethod; `metric_args` default changed to `defaultdict(dict)`
- **`src/experiments.py`** (new): Initial extraction of experiment classes with `ExperimentParameters` dataclass for dependency injection (tracked in #49)
- **`src/stochastic_benchmark.py`**: Error-handling guards for `run_Interpolate` (`ValueError`/`TypeError`); code quality improvements
- **`src/sequential_exploration.py`**: Code improvements
- **`src/cross_validation.py`**: pandas `include_groups` compatibility

### Examples & Documentation
- **QAOA iterative** (`qaoa_demo.ipynb`, `generate_data_many_splits.py`): Expanded with detailed markdown documentation, fixed `update_rule` lambda signatures
- **QAOA multiple splits** (`qaoa_multiple_splits.ipynb`): Expanded documentation
- **Wishart** (`wishart_n_50_alpha_0.50.ipynb`): Expanded with checkpoints and documentation; `README.md` added
- **Simulated Annealing** (new): `simulated_annealing.ipynb` with pre-computed results
- **General workflow** (new): `examples/general_workflow.md` overview document
- **`.github/copilot-instructions.md`**: Updated coding guidelines
- **`.github/NAMING_CONVENTION_REFACTOR.md`**: Naming convention documentation

### Tests
- Expanded `test_bootstrap.py` with additional coverage
- Expanded `test_smoke.py` with `StochasticBenchmark` error-handling tests
- Fixed fragile `test_monotone_df_drops_level_columns` precondition in `test_df_utils.py`
- Removed deprecated pandas monkeypatches from all test files

---

### Follow-up
- #49 — Complete extraction of experiment classes from `stochastic_benchmark.py` into `experiments.py`

title: Fix Django admin static files and login 500 on Vercel
body:
Django admin renders unstyled on Vercel because `collectstatic` output (`staticfiles/`) is never bundled into the Lambda — WhiteNoise has nothing to serve. Additionally, `migrate` never ran on deploy, causing a 500 on `POST /api/auth/login/` due to missing `authtoken_token` table.

## Changes

- **`vercel.json`** — Add `"includeFiles": "staticfiles/**"` to the `@vercel/python` build config so collected assets are packaged into the Lambda; bump `maxLambdaSize` from 15 mb → 30 mb to accommodate them.

- **`build_files.sh`** — Add `python manage.py migrate --noinput` before `collectstatic`; without this, the auth token table never exists in production.

- **`settings.py`** — Switch `STATICFILES_STORAGE` from `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage` to `CompressedStaticFilesStorage`. The manifest variant requires `staticfiles.json` to be present and valid at startup; on serverless this is fragile. The non-manifest variant resolves files by name directly.

- **`backend/README.md`** — Document the static file strategy and verification steps:
  ```bash
  curl -I https://<domain>/static/admin/css/base.css
  curl -I https://<domain>/static/jazzmin/css/main.css
  # both should return HTTP 200
  ```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Goal
Fix Django admin UI styling on Vercel for `vansh-bit/pantry-pal` backend deployment. Admin page renders without CSS/JS (unstyled), which indicates static files are not being served correctly.

## Context
- Backend deployed on Vercel with Root Directory = `backend`.
- Django uses WhiteNoise and `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage`.
- `backend/build.sh` runs `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `python manage.py migrate`, `python manage.py collectstatic --noinput`.
- Current `backend/vercel.json` routes `/static/(.*)`, `/admin/(.*)`, and `/(.*)` to `pantry_pal/wsgi.py`.
- Admin remains unstyled in production.

## Requirements
Implement a robust static files strategy for Vercel so `/static/admin/...` and `/static/jazzmin/...` assets return 200 and admin renders correctly.

## Tasks
1. Update Django static settings to work reliably on Vercel serverless:
   - Ensure `STATIC_ROOT` is set to a directory that is present at runtime.
   - Prefer collecting into a directory that Vercel will serve as static output.
   - Consider using `STATICFILES_DIRS` if needed.
   - Ensure WhiteNoise settings are correct.

2. Update Vercel configuration so static files are served as real static assets:
   - In `backend/vercel.json`, add routes to serve static files from the collected directory before routing to WSGI.
   - If needed, use Vercel's `handle: "filesystem"` and/or explicit `src` to static directory.

3. Update build step to collect static into the chosen directory.

4. Add a quick doc snippet (e.g., in backend README) describing how static works on Vercel and how to verify with:
   - `curl -I /static/admin/css/base.css`
   - `curl -I /static/jazzmin/css/main.css`

## Acceptance criteria
- After deploying, requests to:
  - `/static/admin/css/base.css`
  - `/static/jazzmin/css/main.css`
  return HTTP 200.
- `/admin/` renders with proper CSS.

## Notes
Keep changes minimal and safe. Ensure existing API routes continue to work. If changing `STATIC_ROOT`, ensure it doesn't break local development.

title: Bump markdown from 3.6 to 3.8.1 in /python
body:
Bumps [markdown](https://github.com/Python-Markdown/markdown) from 3.6 to 3.8.1.
Release notes

Sourced from markdown's releases.

Release 3.8.1

Fixed

  • Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser (#1534).
  • Fixed dropped content in md_in_html (#1526).
  • Fixed HTML handling corner case that prevented some content from not being rendered (#1528).

Release 3.8

Changed

  • DRY fix in abbr extension by introducing method create_element (#1483).
  • Clean up test directory by removing some redundant tests and port non-redundant cases to the newer test framework.
  • Improved performance of the raw HTML post-processor (#1510).

Fixed

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

Release 3.7

Changed

Refactor abbr Extension

A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

A call to Markdown.reset() now clears all previously defined abbreviations.

Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying abbr tags to text without a title value.

Added an optional glossary configuration option to the abbreviations extension.

... (truncated)

Fixed

  • Ensure incomplete markup declaration in raw HTML doesn't crash parser (#1534).
  • Fixed dropped content in md_in_html (#1526).
  • Fixed HTML handling corner case that prevented some content from not being rendered (#1528).

  • Improved performance of the raw HTML post-processor (#1510).

Fixed

  • Backslash Unescape IDs set via attr_list on toc (#1493).
  • Ensure md_in_html processes content inside "markdown" blocks as they are parsed outside of "markdown" blocks to keep things more consistent for third-party extensions (#1503).
  • md_in_html handle tags within inline code blocks better (#1075).
  • md_in_html fix handling of one-liner block HTML handling (#1074).
  • Ensure <center> is treated like a block-level element (#1481).
  • Ensure that abbr extension respects AtomicString and does not process perceived abbreviations in these strings (#1512).
  • Ensure smarty extension correctly renders nested closing quotes (#1514).

[3.7.0] - 2024-08-16

Changed

  • Refactor abbr Extension

    A new AbbrTreeprocessor has been introduced, which replaces the now deprecated AbbrInlineProcessor. Abbreviation processing now happens after Attribute Lists, avoiding a conflict between the two extensions (#1460).

    The AbbrPreprocessor class has been renamed to AbbrBlockprocessor, which better reflects what it is. AbbrPreprocessor has been deprecated.

    Abbreviations are now sorted by length before executing AbbrTreeprocessor to ensure that multi-word abbreviations are implemented even if an abbreviation exists for one of those component words. (#1465)

    Abbreviations without a definition are now ignored. This avoids applying

title: Bump requests from 2.31.0 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.31.0 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

title: Port game and vendored pgu library to Python 3, add headless test suite
body:
## Summary

- **Python 3 port of game code**: fixed `has_key`, `iteritems`, integer division, pickle binary mode, and `dict.values()` wrapping in `eit.py`, `blockfield.py`, `playerfield.py`, `datamanager.py`, and `dialogs.py`
- **Python 3 port of vendored `_pgu` library**: converted all implicit relative imports to explicit relative imports, fixed `print` statements, `xrange`→`range`, integer division in `theme.py`/`table.py`, indentation bugs in `area.py`/`dialog.py`, and `None` comparison in `table.py`
- **89 headless pytest tests** covering imports, constants, `Scoretable` logic, block geometry, `BlockField` operations, `PlayerField` scoring/controls/targeting, pygame init, `Main` construction, menu init, event loop, and `calc_stats` integration — all passing
- **`requirements.txt`** with pinned dependencies for a Python 3.13 venv
- **`.gitignore`** excluding `__pycache__`, `.venv`, bytecode, and `scoretable.dat`

## Running tests

```
SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dummy LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 \
    .venv313/bin/pytest test_smoke.py -v
```

All 89 tests pass on a headless server with no display.

title: Port game and vendored pgu library to Python 3, add 89-test headless suite
body:
## Summary

- Ported all game source files (`eit.py`, `blockfield.py`, `playerfield.py`, `datamanager.py`, `dialogs.py`) from Python 2 to Python 3
- Fully ported the vendored `_pgu` GUI library to Python 3 (relative imports, `print` statements, `xrange`, integer division, indentation bugs, `None` comparisons)
- Added 89 pytest tests covering all major game systems, runnable headlessly via `SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dummy`

## How to run tests

```
SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dummy LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 .venv313/bin/pytest test_smoke.py -v
```

Requires Python 3.13 venv (see `requirements.txt`):
```
uv pip install --python .venv313/bin/python -r requirements.txt
```

title: Fix GA8 atlas packing and CELM envelope for macOS 13+
body:
Two bugs caused template (grayscale+alpha) images packed into atlases to
render incorrectly on macOS 14, manifesting as missing or garbled icons
in apps like Rectangle

Packer: GA8 sub-image positioning was using a fixed 4-bytes-per-pixel
stride, but vImage's deepmap2 encoder reads GA8 data at 2 bpp.
Sub-images placed at byte offset `x * 4` past the `width * 2` boundary
fell into the row padding region that vImage skipped, so most of the GA8
atlas content was never encoded.  Use the actual pixel size (2 for GA8,
4 for BGRA) for both the buffer stride and sub-image positioning

Compression envelope: Apple's actool emits CELM ver=0 (with the 16-byte
sub-header) for DMP2-compressed packed atlases when min_deploy is
macOS 13+.  We were unconditionally emitting ver=2.  CoreUI on macOS
14 decodes ver=2 GA8 atlases as kCGImageAlphaNoneSkipLast, dropping
the alpha channel entirely.  Switch to ver=0 for macOS >= 13.0; older
targets still use ver=2 to match Apple's behaviour there

title: [BugFix] anthropic/serving_messages: fix tool call arguments streaming
chore: switch from conda to uv
body:
Replaces all conda-based environment management with [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) across the project.

## Changes

- **`Dockerfile`**: Swap `continuumio/miniconda3` base for `python:3.11-slim`; replace `conda create`/`conda activate`/`pip install` with `uv venv` + `uv pip install`; expose venv via `PATH` so it's always active in the container
- **`docs/Installation.md`**: Replace `conda create`/`conda activate` setup steps with `uv venv --python 3.11` + `source .venv/bin/activate`; replace `pip install` with `uv pip install`; drop the conda-forge system-deps fallback block
- **`scripts/install/interactive_blender.sh`**: Replace `$CONDA_PREFIX` with `$VIRTUAL_ENV` in the `CFLAGS` include path
- **`.github/workflows/checks.yml`**: Replace `actions/setup-python` + bare `pip install` with `astral-sh/setup-uv@v5` + `uv pip install --system`
- **`.python-version`** *(new)*: Pin `3.11` for uv/pyenv tooling

> `docs/ExportingToSimulators.md` conda references are intentionally untouched — they reflect NVIDIA IsaacSim's own required setup, not this project's environment.

title: Bump protobuf from 3.19.3 to 5.29.6
body:
Bumps [protobuf](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf) from 3.19.3 to 5.29.6.
Release notes

Sourced from protobuf's releases.

Protocol Buffers v34.0-rc1

Announcements

  • This version includes breaking changes to: C++, Objective-C, PHP, Python.
  • [Bazel] Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • [C++] Make generator headers private (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3a2af3510f0d454dbe3e4dc281674b61c4d20b9e)
  • [C++] Add a debug check that the target of CopyFrom is not a descendant of the source. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/7a7589823d2cfaaf7994b050e98d5d553bc9b1c1)
  • [C++] Add [[nodiscard]] to many APIs. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/a70115f33f9af2c4b2202c800b84837e7fe0d738)
  • [C++] Make the arena-enabled constructors of RepeatedField, RepeatedPtrField, and Map private. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ef890c3d0c79398c70e047fe5dd893f460ba2336)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [C++] Removes proto2::util::MessageDifferencer::AddIgnoreCriteria that takes a raw pointer as an argument in favor of the overload that takes a unique_ptr. Remove macro PROTOBUF_FUTURE_REMOVE_ADD_IGNORE_CRITERIA (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b115358c64127896fed88b8b5ef5d91d86d8cbae)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::has_optional_keyword() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or has_presence() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/68346ec9348e932664e58c3ecdcd1478f95233a8)
  • [C++] Remove AddUnusedImportTrackFile() and ClearUnusedImportTrackFiles(). Remove PROTOBUF_FUTURE_RENAME_ADD_UNUSED_IMPORT (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/837a2cd1d6c75402b2503ffe7cd8aeaf25868536)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::is_optional() in OSS. Use (!is_required() && !is_repeated()) instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9dbc5d479a8e453921485d8d3de47fb3c005f1af)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • [C++] All entity names have length limit (2afb0dc)
  • [ObjC] Remove generate_minimal_imports generation option warning (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/45b1297fdaad5a9436d0e207422168c38dc45ac4)
  • [ObjC] Fix nullability annotations on some GPB*Dictionary types. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ea67d6d26a48478a567c404679e3bb99cf230d50)
  • [ObjC] Remove -[GPBFieldDescriptor optional] (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3414dc151eb4dcbdb2ca952e2589993bf7af75c4)
  • [Other] Remove deprecated flag for enabling MSVC support (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/97c979be6e0907e1051bee62584dac4594e73fa7)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9c45014099a4f7004fab6dd1278de2f4f2a393c5)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs FieldDescriptor getLabel, use IsRepeated or isRequired instead. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cd76e675b14d00dda5623b30835d2bc7105fccc6, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345)
  • [PHP] Add PHP typehints for setters and remove redundant GPBUtil checks (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25296) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/aee03b78929c02461a5f9d8e136a2a016359b0cd)
  • [PHP] support default values for editions/proto2 (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25161) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b01099d56350551bae3da88b97bf3027274c9f17)
  • [Python] Raise errors in OSS when assign bool to int/enum field in Python Proto. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/5b116fe2f14f49dd0cc3b76089983717f211025c)
  • [Python] Remove float_format/double_format from python proto text_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/e4854a186e0bfa867d5bfa5cd850608a948fd488)
  • [Python] Raise TypeError when convert non-timedelta to Duration, or convert non-datetime to Timestamp in python proto. (Original code may raise ArributeError) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/00aaca1b4d98954bc2933d7c8a5379ba6088124c)
  • [Python] Remove float_precision from python proto json_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f027f1fcd52b9d080b7ee79f4024f53cf54e0dc5)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor.label (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a8ff55518ea5874478ad5b26515b31d186045a9)
  • [Python] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • Protobuf News may include additional announcements or pre-announcements for upcoming changes.
  • Migration Guide may include additional guidance for breaking changes.

Bazel

  • Fix: cc_toolchain should prefer protoc when prebuilt flag is flipped. (#25168) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8c857c3a1c6a106b0a096f1c9fa504bfaca035a9)
  • Breaking change: Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • Feat(bazel): wire up prebuilt protoc toolchain (#24115) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cc23698b486e690ea2eb873cc7596a87c74a3ba6)
  • Migrate proto_descriptor_set (#23369) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8d4dfdd39a7a242a9ed631a6ab2192c57dd9b9c8)

Compiler

  • Ruby codegen: support generation of rbs files (#15633) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/6ebdf851ba78728f0aa145d38454ed9a316fb08d)
  • Avoid collision name problems between a message named Xyz and a direct sibling enum named XyzView (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/eba53e8f172b273d679759a72ce4250131ee3df1)
  • Generalizing and implementing ValidateFeatureSupport for both Options and Features during proto parsing (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ed3c57114d8e2b47cca7697ddaa50c1b3762a6b0)
  • Fix a bug with custom features outside of the pb package. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/872d3ce7a4da00d7dcec33ced20cfe45235935e8)
  • Fix import option handling when include_imports isn't set. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9ef9e80afd9bc8379d578fe67e5ab0738728c04e)
  • Fix a bug in STRICT check of namespaced enums to properly check for 'reserved 1 to max' (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/1229d4adba24c0952ab85ce96bc7b7f8a1fe6d0f)
  • Prevent accidental stripping of debug_redact options via import option. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/f58b098bffa7ca4045ef7773b09151a6af5d0c28)

C++

  • Add EnumerateEnumValues function. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/397d5d99db274b379d1384814074bf7df39d32f7)

title: Modernize littlefs_tools v1.2.0
body:
## Summary

Comprehensive modernization of littlefs_tools from a single-file legacy package to a well-structured, well-tested, PEP-compliant Python package.

- **Packaging:** Migrate from `setup.py` to `pyproject.toml` (PEP 517/518), pin dependency versions, drop Python 3.8 (EOL), add `__version__` to package
- **Core rewrite:** Chunked I/O (4096-byte reads vs byte-by-byte), proper exception hierarchy instead of `exit()`, snake_case naming, type hints, docstrings, input validation, `str.removeprefix()` (3.9+)
- **Unified CLI:** New `littlefs create|list|extract` subcommand interface alongside preserved legacy `littlefs_create/list/extract` entry points
- **Testing:** 37 pytest tests covering create, list, extract, CLI, validation, round-trip, and edge cases
- **CI:** Multi-OS matrix (Ubuntu/macOS/Windows), ruff linting, `python -m build`
- **Docs:** Added CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, updated README.md with new CLI and build instructions

## Test plan

- [x] `ruff check littlefs_tools/ tests/` — all checks passed
- [x] `pytest tests/ -v` — 37/37 passed
- [x] Unified CLI verified (`littlefs create/list/extract`)
- [x] Legacy CLI verified (`littlefs_create/list/extract`)
- [x] Legacy bash integration tests pass (`test/test.sh`, `test/test_large.sh`)
- [x] `littlefs --version` prints `1.2.0`
- [x] `python -m build` produces correct sdist + wheel
- [x] CI passes on all 3 OS x 6 Python versions

title: P2: six-layer architecture refactor (big-bang)
body:
## Summary

Big-bang рефакторинг монолитных `scripts/wisper_launcher.py` + `scripts/merge_whisperx.py` + `scripts/parse_fvtt_chat.py` в шестислойную архитектуру из `ARCHITECTURE.md`:

- `domain/` — чистые dataclass'ы (`SpeechEvent | ChatEvent | GameEvent`), `Timeline`, `speaker_map`
- `sources/` — `Source` ABC + `FasterWhisperSource`, `WhisperXSource`, `FvttChatSource`
- `mergers/` — `Merger` ABC + `ScriptMerger` (склейка same-speaker, speech-before-chat tiebreaker)
- `renderers/` — `Renderer` ABC + `PlainTextRenderer`
- `core/` — `pipeline.run` / `run_batch`, `PipelineParams` (frozen dataclass), `discovery`, `gpu_check`, `speaker_map`, `fvtt_helpers`, `cache` (stub)
- `ui/` — `cli.py` (argparse) + `gui.py` (tkinter, порт из `wisper_launcher.gui_main`) + `__main__.py` для `python -m ui`
- `launcher/` — bootstrap и PyInstaller spec переведены на `python -m ui`

Legacy-скрипты удалены (−1380 строк). Runtime-ссылок на них нет.

## Что сделано по шагам

- **P2.1–2.2**: scaffold + `domain/`
- **P2.3**: `sources/` (FasterWhisper, WhisperX с `beam_size=10`, FvttChat)
- **P2.4**: `mergers/` (`ScriptMerger` с glue + tiebreaker)
- **P2.5**: `renderers/` (`PlainTextRenderer`)
- **P2.6**: `core/pipeline.run` + `discovery` + `gpu_check` + `cache` stub
- **P2.7**: `ui/cli.py` с документированным исключением `ui → sources` для `argparse choices=`
- **P2.8**: `launcher/bootstrap.py` + `build.spec` на `python -m ui`, все шесть пакетов бандлятся
- **P2.9**: 3-tier e2e gate — tier 1 структурный (CI), tier 2 семантический (token overlap ≥ 0.90), tier 3 strict (manual)
- **P2.10**: удаление legacy-скриптов, `core/fvtt_helpers.detect_fvtt_tz_offset` для разрыва `ui → sources`, ренейминг `_parse_fvtt_log` → `parse_fvtt_log` и т.д. (reviewer finding)
- **Fix**: `_token_overlap` в tier2 тесте считал intersection над set но делил на длины list — идентичные тексты давали 0.77 вместо 1.0. Перешёл на Counter-based multiset intersection.

## Test plan

- [x] Tier 1 — 84 pytest green в 0.21s (`pytest -m "not slow"`)
- [x] Tier 2 — 2 pytest green в 102s, overlap **1.000** (byte-exact reproduction baseline на синтетическом FLAC-фикстуре, faster-whisper large-v3-ru-podlodka, CPU/int8/beam=1)
- [x] `python -m ui --help` работает
- [x] Полный import chain (`core.pipeline`, `core.fvtt_helpers`, `ui.cli`, `sources`, `mergers`, `renderers`, `domain.timeline`) — OK
- [ ] **Manual tier 3 smoke test** на реальной сессии (`games/bogomols/*`) — отложен в follow-up, покрыт tier 2 на синтетике

## Известные follow-up (не блокеры)

- Документация (`README.md`, `README.ru.md`, `00_README.md`, `01_Как_пользоваться.md`, `scripts/00_README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`) всё ещё содержит инструкции `python scripts/wisper_launcher.py ...` — требует отдельного `docs:` PR
- `resolve_speaker` edge case: `foo` vs `foo.json` коллизия в `domain/speaker_map.py`
- `load_speaker_map` warning logging
- Унификация registry contract: `sources` возвращает `type[Source]`, `mergers`/`renderers` возвращают instance
- ADR-009 для документированного исключения `ui/cli.py → sources` (`argparse choices=`)

## Breaking changes

- Entry point: `python scripts/wisper_launcher.py` → `python -m ui`
- `scripts/run_whisperx_gui.bat` обновлён автоматически
- PyInstaller build: теперь бандлит шесть пакетов (`ui`, `core`, `sources`, `mergers`, `renderers`, `domain`) вместо трёх старых скриптов
- Дропнутые CLI-флаги: `--vad_method`, `--hf_token`, `--diarize` (soft-drop с warning через `argparse.SUPPRESS`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Port PCS to Python 3.12
body:
## Summary

Port the entire PCS (Packet Construction Set) codebase from Python 2 to Python 3.12. Version bumped to 1.0.

## Changes

### Build System
- Replace distutils with setuptools (distutils removed in Python 3.12)
- Add multiarch library path for Debian/Ubuntu pcap detection
- Update Makefile to use python3 and cython3

### Core (pcs/__init__.py)
- Replace dl module with ctypes for if_nametoindex
- Replace itertools.ifilter with builtin filter
- Replace __div__ with __truediv__
- Use integer division (//) where int results are needed
- Use bytes literals (b'') for binary data
- Fix implicit relative imports to explicit

### Cython Extensions
- Replace PyBuffer_FromMemory with bytes slicing in pcap.pyx
- Replace Py_BEGIN/END_ALLOW_THREADS with Cython nogil blocks
- Fix integer division in clock.pyx

### All Python Files (84 files)
- Convert all print statements to print() function calls
- Fix raise syntax: raise ExcType, msg to raise ExcType(msg)
- Replace iteritems()/itervalues() with items()/values()
- Replace xrange with range
- Remove ord() on bytes indexing (returns int in Python 3)
- Fix tab/space indentation inconsistencies

title: Set web deployment default port to 3000
body:
### Motivation
- Make the lightweight WSGI web app ready for server deployment by using a sensible default port and allowing environment overrides for host/port configuration.

### Description
- Update `web_app.py` to read `HOST` and `PORT` from the environment with defaults `HOST=0.0.0.0` and `PORT=3000`, and print the runtime URL when starting the server.
- Update the `README.md` web usage section to document the new default `0.0.0.0:3000` and show how to override via `HOST`/`PORT` environment variables.

### Testing
- Ran the test suite with `python -m pytest -q` and all tests passed (`3 passed`).
- Executed the CLI verification `python mapping_system.py --date 2026-02-20` which produced the expected output (total score 9/10) and succeeded. 
- Launched the web app with `python web_app.py` and performed a browser smoke test (automated Playwright script submitted the date and captured a screenshot) which completed successfully.

title: Migrate to pyproject.toml and add headless CMS JSON API
body:
Replaces the legacy `setup.py` with `pyproject.toml` (PEP 517/518/621) and exposes the `Page` model via a REST-style JSON API to enable headless CMS usage.

## Packaging
- `pyproject.toml` replaces `setup.py` with `setuptools>=61.0` build backend
- Correct Python classifiers: drops Python 2, adds 3.8–3.12 and Django 4.0–4.2
- Fixes license classifier (was incorrectly `BSD`, now `LGPLv3`)
- Bumps `django` requirement from `>=3.0.7` to `>=4.0`
- Adds `manage.py` + `test_settings.py` for the test suite

## Headless CMS API

New endpoints served under `feedly/urls.py`:

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Blocks health check |
| GET | `/pages/` | List all pages |
| POST | `/pages/` | Create page (auth required) |
| GET | `/pages/<id>/` | Retrieve page |
| PUT | `/pages/<id>/` | Update page (owner only) |
| DELETE | `/pages/<id>/` | Delete page (owner only) |

```python
# Example: list pages
GET /feedly/pages/
# → {"pages": [{"id": 1, "name": "My Page", "content": "...", "date": "2024-..."}, ...]}

# Example: create a page (authenticated)
POST /feedly/pages/
{"name": "About", "content": "Hello world"}
# → 201 {"id": 2, "name": "About", "content": "Hello world", "date": "..."}
```

## Bug Fixes
- `feedly/apps.py`: `name` was `'feedly.feedly'` (no such subpackage), corrected to `'feedly'`
- `feedly/__init__.py`: removes stale `default_app_config` (deprecated Django 3.2+)
- `feedly/urls.py`: removes `from django.conf.urls import url` (removed in Django 4.0); switches to explicit view imports
- `feedly/views.py`: removes broken imports from non-existent `.feed` and `.core` modules

title: Migrate clean.py to Python 3
body:
`clean.py` used Python 2-only syntax, making it incompatible with Python 3.

## Changes

- **`raw_input` → `input`**: Python 3 removed `raw_input`
- **`print` statements → `print()` calls**: Python 3 print function syntax
- **Fix regex escape sequence**: Changed string literal to raw string (`r"..."`) to eliminate `SyntaxWarning` on `\[` in the pattern

```python
# Before
ans = raw_input("remove %s ([y]/n)? " % img)
print "all images in latex: ", len(all_used_img)
regex_string = "\\includegraphics(?:\[.*?\])?\{(.+?)\}"

# After
ans = input("remove %s ([y]/n)? " % img)
print("all images in latex: ", len(all_used_img))
regex_string = r"\\includegraphics(?:\[.*?\])?\{(.+?)\}"
```

title: Migrate codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
body:
Both `main.py` and `experimental.py` used Python 2-only constructs (`izip`, `print` statements, `raw_input`, unguarded `map()`). This migrates the codebase to Python 3.

### `main.py`
- Drop `izip` import; use built-in `zip`
- `print "..."` → `print(...)`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`
- `map(int, ...)` → `list(map(int, ...))` (map is lazy in Py3)
- Integer division: `/` → `//`

### `experimental.py`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`

### `README.md`
- Requirement updated from Python 2 → Python 3
- Commands updated from `python` → `python3`

title: Fix install failure on Intel Mac due to Python version constraints
body:
- Lower requires-python from >=3.13 to >=3.12 (no 3.13-specific features used)
- Fix install_service.sh: pass --python to uv pip install so it targets the .venv instead of falling back to a system interpreter
- Gracefully fall back to Python 3.12 if 3.13 is unavailable when creating venv

* Improved installation robustness with automatic version fallback handling and explicit environment configuration

title: Add weekday Azure Logic App deployment automation for GitHub JSON export
body:
### Motivation
- Provide an automated, repeatable way to deploy the containerized `/trending` service to Azure and schedule a Logic App that commits the JSON output to a GitHub repo every weekday at 3:55 PM ET.

### Description
- Update `README_AZURE.md` with a two-step workflow and examples for deploying the Container App and the weekday Logic App. 
- Improve `deploy_azure.sh` to use script-relative paths (`SCRIPT_DIR`), make the Docker build context correct, and support update-or-create behavior for an existing Container App. 
- Add `deploy_logicapp.sh` which deploys the ARM template (`templates/logicapp_arm_template.json`) with parameters for the container URL, GitHub repo, and token. 
- Change Logic App definitions in `templates/logicapp_workflow.json` and `templates/logicapp_arm_template.json` to run `Week` recurrence on Monday–Friday at `15:55` in `Eastern Standard Time`.

### Testing
- Verified shell syntax with `bash -n deploy_azure.sh` and `bash -n deploy_logicapp.sh`, both reported no syntax errors. 
- Validated JSON templates with `python -m json.tool templates/logicapp_workflow.json` and `python -m json.tool templates/logicapp_arm_template.json`, both succeeded. 
- Attempted `az account show` to validate live Azure operations but it failed in this environment because the Azure CLI is not installed (`/bin/bash: az: command not found`).

------
