title: Restructure project: migrate to `src/` app, remove legacy modules, update deps and docs
body: ### Motivation - Move the codebase to a slimmer packaged layout and make the runtime launcher simpler and more robust when `fastapi`/`uvicorn` are not installed. - Reduce surface area and external dependencies by removing legacy telephony/AI helper modules and feature code that were tightly coupled to the monolith. - Bump the project Python version to a newer patch release for consistency and bugfixes by updating `.python-version` to `3.11.14`. - Add a minimal `README` describing architecture, features and run instructions for the refactored project. ### Description - Updated runtime: `.python-version` changed from `3.11.9` to `3.11.14` and `README.md` was added with architecture and run instructions. - Rewrote `main.py` to act as a lightweight launcher that detects `fastapi`/`uvicorn` via `importlib.util.find_spec`, runs `src.app` with Uvicorn when available, and falls back to running `src.qa_validation.main` for local QA simulation otherwise. - Removed large set of legacy modules and features including `agent.py`, `call_handler.py`, `audio_utils.py`, `config.py`, `exotel_client.py`, `intent.py`, `lead_manager.py`, `phrase_cache.py`, `scheduler.py`, `scraper.py`, `sheets_manager.py`, and related files to reduce complexity and dependency footprint. - Trimmed `requirements.txt` to a smaller set of direct/runtime packages (removed many Google Sheets, Groq, scheduler and other heavy deps) and added only the essentials like `python-multipart` and `pdfplumber` used by the new layout. ### Testing - No automated tests were executed as part of this change. ------ [Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69d898fbde308322a099688874a25ae3) --- ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes * **New Features** * Added WebSocket-based voice call streaming with real-time audio processing * Implemented document learning system: upload PDFs and images to augment conversational knowledge * Introduced hybrid conversation routing combining pre-scripted responses with LLM fallback * Added end-of-speech detection for natural conversation pausing * **Documentation** * Added comprehensive README documentation for v3 architecture and features * **Chores** * Updated Python version to 3.11.14 * Simplified configuration structure and removed legacy integrations * Streamlined dependencies to core components only

title: feat: Implement Streamlit frontend and complete backend test script
body:
Adds the Streamlit UI layer and a self-contained backend test script to make the news aggregation + event extraction pipeline demonstrable end-to-end.

## Backend

- **`backend/test_news_extraction.py`** — Replaces the placeholder stub with a runnable script covering three test stages: news aggregation, event extraction, and confidence-score validation. Includes built-in sample data stubs so it produces meaningful output even without the real `app.data_ingestion` modules installed.
- **`backend/requirements.txt`** — Populated with the full dependency set: `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `spacy`, `feedparser`, `beautifulsoup4`, `streamlit≥1.28`, `plotly`, `pandas`, and supporting libraries.

## Frontend

- **`frontend/app.py`** — Four-page Streamlit app wired to the FastAPI backend via `BACKEND_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:8001/api/v1`):
  - 📰 **实时新闻** — article list with hours/count sliders and keyword search
  - 🔍 **事件监控** — extracted events filterable by type and severity, with entity display
  - 📊 **仪表板** — live metrics + Plotly charts for event-type and severity distribution
  - ⚙️ **系统状态** — backend connectivity check and news-source listing
- **`frontend/utils/api_client.py`** — Thin `requests`-based client covering all relevant endpoints (`/news/sources`, `/news/ingest`, `/news/latest`, `/news/search`, `/news/events/extracted`, `/events`, `/health`) with structured error returns instead of exceptions.
- **`frontend/.streamlit/config.toml`** — Theme and logger config.
- **`frontend/pages/__init__.py`**, **`frontend/utils/__init__.py`** — Package inits.

```bash
# Run test script (works without backend installed)
python backend/test_news_extraction.py

# Start backend + frontend
cd backend && uvicorn app.main:app --port 8001
streamlit run frontend/app.py
```

title: Ansible-to-Ansible Role Modernization
body:
## Ansible-to-Ansible Role Modernization

Related to: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/FLPATH-3397

### Summary

Adds a new input source — legacy Ansible roles — to the existing init → analyze → migrate pipeline. Users can now modernize outdated Ansible roles to current best practices using `--source-technology Ansible`, the same workflow already used for Chef and PowerShell.

### What's New

**Ansible analysis agent** (`src/inputs/ansible/`) — A structured analysis pipeline that scans legacy roles and produces a detailed modernization specification:
- Analyzes tasks, handlers, defaults, vars, meta, and templates using four specialized LLM services with Pydantic structured output
- Flags legacy patterns across 14 modernization categories (FQCN, `with_items` → `loop`, `sudo` → `become`, bare variables, fact access, handler casing, `.iteritems()` → `.items()`, TLS upgrades, etc.)
- Validates the analysis for completeness (dropped handlers, missing vars, incorrect dependencies)

**Migrate phase improvements** — Reliability fixes that affect all source technologies:
- Module path resolution now reads from `generated-project-metadata.json` (produced by init) instead of relying on LLM extraction, with LLM as fallback
- Write system prompt expanded with rules for vars/defaults preservation, handler preservation, privilege escalation consolidation, handler name capitalization, and application-domain value-type safety
- Collection manager filters out `ansible.builtin` before calling `ansible-galaxy install`, preventing install failures

**Documentation** — Usage and Docker guides unified into a single technology-agnostic workflow. Architecture, workflow, and input-agents docs updated to reflect the new pipeline and modernization categories.

### Test Plan

- [x] `uv run pytest -x -q` — 455 passed, 5 skipped
- [x] Full pipeline run (init → analyze → migrate) on [`bennojoy-nginx`](https://github.com/bennojoy/nginx) legacy role completes without manual intervention
- [x] Migrated role passes `ansible-role-check` validation
- [x] Handler names in `handlers/main.yml` match `notify:` references in `tasks/main.yml` exactly
- [x] All source tasks, handlers, vars, defaults, and templates preserved in migrated output

title: Bump actions/setup-python from 5 to 6
body:
Bumps [actions/setup-python](https://github.com/actions/setup-python) from 5 to 6.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/setup-python's releases.

v6.0.0

What's Changed

Breaking Changes

  • Upgrade to node 24 by @​salmanmkc in actions/setup-python#1164

Make sure your runner is on version v2.327.1 or later to ensure compatibility with this release. See Release Notes

Enhancements:

  • Add support for pip-version by @​priyagupta108 in actions/setup-python#1129
  • Enhance reading from .python-version by @​krystof-k in actions/setup-python#787
  • Add version parsing from Pipfile by @​aradkdj in actions/setup-python#1067

Bug fixes:

  • Clarify pythonLocation behaviour for PyPy and GraalPy in environment variables by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1183
  • Change missing cache directory error to warning by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1182
  • Add Architecture-Specific PATH Management for Python with --user Flag on Windows by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1122
  • Include python version in PyPy python-version output by @​cdce8p in actions/setup-python#1110
  • Update docs: clarification on pip authentication with setup-python by @​priya-kinthali in actions/setup-python#1156

Dependency updates:

  • Upgrade idna from 2.9 to 3.7 in /tests/data by @​dependabot[bot] in actions/setup-python#843
  • Upgrade form-data to fix critical vulnerabilities #182 & #183 by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1163
  • Upgrade setuptools to 78.1.1 to fix path traversal vulnerability in PackageIndex.download by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1165
  • Upgrade actions/checkout from 4 to 5 by @​dependabot[bot] in actions/setup-python#1181
  • Upgrade @​actions/tool-cache from 2.0.1 to 2.0.2 by @​dependabot[bot] in actions/setup-python#1095

New Contributors

  • @​krystof-k made their first contribution in actions/setup-python#787
  • @​cdce8p made their first contribution in actions/setup-python#1110
  • @​aradkdj made their first contribution in actions/setup-python#1067

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/setup-python/compare/v5...v6.0.0

v5.6.0

What's Changed

  • Workflow updates related to Ubuntu 20.04 by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1065
  • Fix for Candidate Not Iterable Error by @​aparnajyothi-y in actions/setup-python#1082
  • Upgrade semver and @​types/semver by @​dependabot in actions/setup-python#1091
  • Upgrade prettier from 2.8.8 to 3.5.3 by @​dependabot in actions/setup-python#1046
  • Upgrade ts-jest from 29.1.2 to 29.3.2 by @​dependabot in actions/setup-python#1081

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/setup-python/compare/v5...v5.6.0

v5.5.0

What's Changed

Enhancements:

  • Support free threaded Python versions like '3.13t' by @​colesbury in actions/setup-python#973
  • Enhance Workflows: Include ubuntu-arm runners, Add e2e Testing for free threaded and Upgrade @​action/cache from 4.0.0 to 4.0.3 by @​priya-kinthali in actions/setup-python#1056
  • Add support for .tool-versions file in setup-python by @​mahabaleshwars in actions/setup-python#1043

title: Implement config processing and update README
body:
This pull request introduces configuration file support for the deluge-export CLI application, allowing users to provide connection details via TOML files or environment variables. The CLI now prioritizes configuration from files and environment variables, improving usability and flexibility. Additionally, tests have been added for the new configuration logic, and the documentation has been significantly expanded.

Configuration support and enhancements:

* Added a new `config.py` module implementing the `load_config` function, which locates and parses configuration files from standard paths and supports TOML parsing for Python <3.11 via `tomli`.
* Modified `cli.py` to load connection details from configuration files and environment variables, providing fallback defaults and improved error handling for invalid ports.
* Updated dependencies in `pyproject.toml` to include `tomli` for compatibility with Python versions below 3.11.

Testing improvements:

* Added `test_config.py` with comprehensive tests for configuration loading, including cases for missing files, valid/invalid TOML, and missing `[deluge]` sections.
* Updated CLI tests to mock configuration loading, ensuring tests are isolated from actual config files.

Documentation updates:

* Expanded `README.md` with installation instructions, configuration details, environment variable support, and usage examples for listing and extracting torrents.

Other minor changes:

* Removed unused `pytest` import from `test_cli.py`.This pull request introduces configuration file support and environment variable integration for the deluge-export CLI, improving usability and flexibility. It also updates documentation and adds a dependency for TOML parsing on Python versions below 3.11.

Configuration support:

* Added a new `config.py` module that loads configuration from TOML files located at `~/.config/deluge-export/config.toml` or `~/.deluge-export.toml`, and exposes `DEFAULT_CONFIG` for use throughout the application.
* Updated `cli.py` so that CLI options for host, port, user, and password default to values from `DEFAULT_CONFIG`, and support environment variables (`DELUGE_HOST`, `DELUGE_PORT`, `DELUGE_USER`, `DELUGE_PASSWORD`).

Documentation:

* Expanded the `README.md` to include installation instructions, configuration file details, environment variable support, and usage examples.

Dependency management:

* Added the `tomli` dependency for TOML parsing on Python versions below 3.11 in `pyproject.toml`.

title: Enable Slack-based remote task control via Conversation service + document setup
body:
This PR implements end-to-end remote control from Slack by adding a Slack Events bridge into the existing Conversation service flow. It also updates docs so operators can configure the bot and use chat instructions to trigger Screen-Mind tasks.

- **Slack remote chat bridge (new service)**
  - Added `services/slack_adapter` (FastAPI, port `8014`) to handle Slack Events API traffic.
  - Supports:
    - `url_verification` challenge flow
    - Signed request validation (`X-Slack-Signature`, `X-Slack-Request-Timestamp`)
    - Message event intake (`event_callback`)
    - Channel → conversation session mapping
    - Forwarding user text to Conversation service
    - Optional direction auto-execution (`AUTO_EXECUTE_DIRECTIONS=true`)
    - Reply delivery back to Slack via `chat.postMessage`

- **Runtime/config wiring**
  - Added `slack-adapter` service to `docker-compose.yml`.
  - Added Slack-related env vars in `.env.example`:
    - `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`
    - `SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET`
    - `AUTO_EXECUTE_DIRECTIONS`
    - `CONVERSATION_URL`, `SLACK_ADAPTER_URL`

- **API and architecture docs**
  - Updated `README.md` with a concise remote Slack setup section and usage notes.
  - Added Conversation (`:8013`) and Slack Adapter (`:8014`) sections in `docs/api-reference.md`.
  - Added Slack remote chat flow in `docs/architecture.md` (signature verification, intent routing, optional execute path).

- **Focused test coverage**
  - Added `tests/test_slack_adapter.py` for:
    - health endpoint
    - URL verification
    - valid/invalid Slack signature behavior
    - message forwarding to Conversation
    - direction auto-execution behavior

- **Example event payload (implemented path)**

```json
{
  "type": "event_callback",
  "team_id": "T123",
  "event": {
    "type": "message",
    "channel": "C123",
    "user": "U123",
    "text": "open chrome and search weather in london"
  }
}
```

<screenshot>

![Slack adapter working screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/747c4c0c-873d-4ee6-925b-c4a39af96389)

</screenshot>

The implementation is clean and consistent with the pattern established by the prerequisite financial-analysis-agent-search unit. title: feat: compatibility support for xrange and izip
body:
Resolves the issue where `six.moves.xrange` and `itertools.izip` were generating unmapped `six.moves.xrange(10)` or `itertools.izip(...)` calls instead of utilizing V's native range constructs and sequence iterators. Both `visit_For` and comprehension generator visits were updated to look for these names in `ast.Call` nodes.

title: feat: Transform into full-stack interactive dashboard with React Force-Graph and FastAPI
body:
## Overview
This PR transforms the `agentic-knowledge-web` project from basic CLI scripts into a complete, interactive full-stack web application. It introduces a FastAPI backend layer to expose agent capabilities as REST endpoints and a modern React (Vite) frontend featuring a dynamic force-directed knowledge graph visualization.

## Key Changes

### 1. Backend (FastAPI)
- **API Layer**: Implemented full ASGI FastAPI application in `main.py` overriding the empty placeholder.
- **Routers**: Added modular REST endpoints under `src/api/` for `/chat`, `/ingest`, `/graph`, and `/reset`.
- **Dependency Injection**: Added `deps.py` for efficient Neo4j, Chroma, and LLM singleton management.
- **Graph Queries**: Enhanced `Neo4jClient` with frontend-ready graph serialization (`get_graph_data` & `expand_node`).
- **Resilience**: Added error handling around vector retrieval to gracefully handle Ollama `NaN` generation timeouts, allowing fallback to pure Graph search.
- **Model Config**: Switched local embedding model to `nomic-embed-text`.

### 2. Frontend (React + Vite)
- **Initialization**: Bootstrapped a new React + Vite project under `frontend/`.
- **Styling**: Configured deep dark themes, glassmorphism UI, and gradient components using Tailwind CSS v3.
- **State Management**: Implemented `Zustand` (`useStore.js`) for managing deeply nested graph and chat states globally.
- **Interactive Knowledge Web**: Integrated `react-force-graph-2d` to visualize RAG components.
  - **Dynamic Expansion**: Users can now right-click nodes to pull and merge localized neighbors incrementally.
  - **Auto Interaction**: Clicking nodes displays context data along with quick "Ask AI" integrations.
- **Integrations**: Fully wired up Markdown-supported chat window and drag-and-drop ingestion endpoints.

### 3. Build & Docs
- **Dependencies**: Updated `pyproject.toml` and lock files incorporating `fastapi` and `uvicorn`. 
- **Doc Rewrite**: Refactored `README.md` to reflect the new dual-server (FastAPI + Vite) startup flow and latest architectural diagram.

## How to Test
1. Start backend: `uv run python -m uvicorn main:app --reload`
2. Start frontend: `cd frontend && npm run dev`
3. Hit `http://localhost:5173`.
4. Drop a markdown file onto the bottom-left uploader to trigger knowledge ingestion.
5. Watch the Graph automatically reload, zoom across entities, right-click to expand edges, and interact with the Agent via chat.

## Notes
- **Breaking Change**: The embedding model was swapped to `nomic-embed-text` (dim: 768). If migrating an existing local environment, please press the danger "Reset" button on the UI (or manually delete `chroma_db/`) to wipe old 1024-dim `bge-m3` vectors.

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

title: feat: Oracle自动版本检测和连接模式切换
body:
## Summary

修复 Oracle 数据库连接版本兼容性问题，实现自动版本检测和连接模式切换。

- 自动检测 Oracle 版本，对于 12c+ 使用 thin mode
- 对于旧版本（如 11g）自动切换到 thick mode
- 自动查找 Oracle Instant Client 安装路径
- 添加 `oracle_client_lib` 和 `force_thick_mode` 参数
- 在 pyproject.toml 中添加 `datasource_oracle` 可选依赖

## 问题背景

连接旧版 Oracle 数据库（如 11g）时出现错误：
```
DPY-3010: connections to this database server version are not supported by python-oracledb in thin mode
```

python-oracledb 的 **thin mode** 仅支持 Oracle 12.1+，旧版本需要 **thick mode** + Oracle Instant Client。

## 解决方案

### 自动版本检测

```
用户发起连接 → 尝试 Thin Mode → 成功则直接使用
                              ↓ 失败(DPY-3010)
                        切换 Thick Mode → 自动查找 Instant Client → 连接
```

### 自动查找 Instant Client

按优先级查找：
1. `ORACLE_INSTANT_CLIENT_HOME` 环境变量
2. `ORACLE_HOME` 环境变量  
3. 常见路径 (`/opt/oracle`, `/usr/lib/oracle` 等)
4. `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` / `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH`

### 用户使用

**无需任何配置，直接连接即可**。

### Oracle Instant Client 安装（仅旧版本需要）

```bash
# Linux
mkdir -p /opt/oracle && cd /opt/oracle
unzip instantclient-basic-linux.x64-19.8.0.0.0dbru.zip
export ORACLE_INSTANT_CLIENT_HOME=/opt/oracle/instantclient_19_8
```

## 文件改动

- `packages/derisk-ext/src/derisk_ext/datasource/rdbms/conn_oracle.py` - 添加自动检测功能
- `packages/derisk-ext/pyproject.toml` - 添加 oracledb 依赖

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Direct ADB backup/restore tool for VMOS Pro cloud devices
body:
Adds a standalone backup/restore tool that operates entirely via direct ADB-over-TCP/IP connections — no VMOS API calls involved. Backs up and restores system identity, network proxies, apps with data, accounts/logins, and contacts. Each component is independently backupable and restorable. Supports cross-owner device access via HMAC-SHA256 signed permission tokens.

### New module: `vmospro_controller/backup/`

- **`adb_connector.py`** — ADB TCP/IP connection manager wrapping `adb connect/shell/pull/push` via subprocess
- **`direct_backup.py`** — Connects to device, reads properties via `getprop`, pulls APKs and data archives locally via `adb pull`
- **`direct_restore.py`** — Pushes APKs/data back via `adb push`, sets properties via `setprop`, fixes ownership/permissions
- **`permission.py`** — Offline cross-owner token system: owner signs a `PermissionGrant` with shared secret, grantee verifies before accessing device
- **`models.py`** — `BackupManifest`, `BackupComponent` enum, property categorization sets

### Backup components (each independent)

| Component | What's captured | Method |
|---|---|---|
| `system_identity` | All `ro.*`/`persist.*` props (IMEI, Android ID, fingerprint, etc.) | `getprop` parsing |
| `network_proxies` | Proxy mode/type/data, DNS, bypass rules | Property subset |
| `installed_apps` | Third-party APKs | `pm list packages -f -3` + `adb pull` |
| `app_data` | `/data/data/<pkg>` + `/sdcard/Android/data/<pkg>` | On-device `tar` + `adb pull` |
| `accounts` | `accounts.db` + `dumpsys account` metadata | Direct file pull |
| `contacts` | Contacts provider database | `tar` + `adb pull` |

### Integration

- 13 new skills in `agent/skills.py` including `grant_device_permission` for cross-owner tokens
- SKILL_CATALOG and policies updated
- CLI subcommands: `backup`, `restore`, `list-backups`, `grant-permission` — none require API credentials

### Usage

```bash
# Full backup (direct ADB, no API)
python main.py backup 192.168.1.100 --port 5555

# Restore to any device
python main.py restore 10.0.0.50 --backup-id backup_1713000000_abc12345

# Cross-owner: owner grants access
python main.py grant-permission 192.168.1.100 \
  --owner-id alice --grantee-id bob --secret shared-key --expires-hours 48

# Grantee uses token
python main.py backup 192.168.1.100 \
  --permission-token "<token>" --permission-secret shared-key
```

```python
from vmospro_controller.backup import DirectBackup, DirectRestore

backup = DirectBackup("192.168.1.100", 5555)
manifest = backup.full_backup()

restore = DirectRestore("10.0.0.50", 5555)
restore.full_restore(manifest)
```

### Security

- Shell arguments escaped via `_shell_escape()` (single-quote safe)
- Property keys validated against `^[a-zA-Z0-9._]+$`
- Package names validated before use in shell commands
- Permission tokens require explicit shared secret (no default key)
- Property override files written via base64 encoding to avoid heredoc injection

title: Harden PSD/stage analysis, avoid pandas interpolate deprecation, and make pipeline config/result handling more robust
body:
### Motivation
- Prevent runtime warnings and future breakage from `DataFrame.interpolate` on object dtypes. 
- Make PSD and stage analysis tolerant to partial/short recordings and missing analyzed artifacts to avoid crashes during merging. 
- Preserve per-dataset output layout and allow a sane default config location for Docker runs. 
- Improve path handling to support alternative FASTER2 result layouts and avoid NameError/XRange issues in stage-time plotting.

### Description
- Call `infer_objects(copy=False)` before `interpolate` in `faster2lib/summary_psd.py` to avoid the pandas deprecation warning and handle object dtypes. 
- Normalize mask/epoch handling and support `range`/`slice`/lists for `epoch_range`, trim ranges to available data, align mask lengths, and return empty `DataFrame`s for empty inputs in `faster2lib/summary_psd.py`. 
- Add fallbacks and existence checks in `analysis.py` to use alternative stat/PSD paths, warn and skip missing files, and return empty frames when no inputs are found. 
- Harden `pipeline_step2_analyze.py` by resolving result directories (`resolve_result_dir`), using `Path`, trimming stage arrays for hourly/circadian profiles and swtrans, making `extract_raw_EEG_n_EMG` resilient, and emitting warnings instead of crashing; also generate per-dataset `analyzed` outputs instead of flattening them. 
- Add `DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH` and `resolve_config_path` in `run_pipeline.py` and update CLI to default to `/data/config.json`, and update `README.md` Docker instructions accordingly.

### Testing
- No automated unit tests or CI were executed for these changes. 
- The change to call `infer_objects` was applied to silence the reported `FutureWarning` from `pandas.DataFrame.interpolate`. 
- Runtime-guarding additions were limited to existence checks and dtype conversions and thus require integration testing on real datasets. 
- Manual review/commit was performed but no automated verification was run.

------

title: Update pandas to support Python 3.13
body:
## Problem

The build fails during pandas compilation under Python 3.13.12 with the error "standard attributes in middle of decl-specifiers" in Cython-generated C++ code. This is a known incompatibility — pandas 2.2.2 predates Python 3.13 and its generated C extensions cannot be compiled against it.

## Solution

Bumped pandas from 2.2.2 to 2.3.0 in requirements.txt. Pandas 2.3.0 is the first release with official Python 3.13 support and ships pre-built wheels for it, eliminating the source compilation step that triggered the error.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: Add tool to strip Python function bodies while preserving docstrings
body:
### Motivation
- Provide a lightweight utility to prepare large sets of test assets by removing implementations from real Python code while keeping docstrings intact.

### Description
- Add `tools/strip_function_bodies.py`, an AST-driven script that rewrites each function/method body to keep the original docstring (if any) and replace the remainder with `pass`, using precise source offsets to preserve formatting.
- Support processing single files or directories and a `--dry-run` mode to preview changes without writing files.
- Add `tests/test_strip_function_bodies_tool.py` with focused tests for in-memory rewriting and on-disk updates; the test imports the tool via `importlib` so it can exercise the module functions directly.

### Testing
- Ran `uv run pytest -q tests/test_strip_function_bodies_tool.py` and the added tests passed.
- Ran the full suite with `uv run pytest -q` and all project tests passed (no failures).

------

title: py3.7升级3.11之后py本地版兼容性完善修改
body:
# QUANTAXIS PR 😇

感谢您对于QUANTAXIS的项目的参与~ 🛠请在此完善一下最后的信息~

## 注意

- 如果非第一次fork, 请先将quantaxis 库的内容PR进您的项目进行更新, 然后再执行对于quantaxis的pr
- 请完善[CHANGELOG](https://github.com/QUANTAXIS/QUANTAXIS/releases)再进行PR

## PR前必看

请使用如下代码对要PR的代码进行格式化:

使用以下代码来格式化
```
pip install https://github.com/google/yapf/archive/master.zip
yapf -i --style .style.yapf <file>
```

## 🛠该PR主要解决的问题🛠:

py3.7升级3.11之后兼容性完善修改
### 一、环境与依赖升级概况

#### 1.1 Python 版本要求提升

- 在 `setup.py` 中：
  - 检查并限制 Python 版本为 `>=3.9,<4.0`。
- 在 `QUANTAXIS/__init__.py` 中：
  - 明确版本信息，避免在低版本 Python 上运行。

#### 1.2 核心依赖升级（`requirements.txt`）

- **数据库层**
  - `pymongo>=4.10.0,<5.0.0`
  - `motor>=3.7.0,<4.0.0`
- **数据处理**
  - `pandas>=2.0.0,<3.0.0`
  - `numpy>=1.24.0,<2.0.0`
- **Web & Async**
  - `tornado>=6.4.0,<7.0.0`
  - `flask>=3.0.0,<4.0.0`
- **金融数据源**
  - `tushare>=1.4.0`
  - `pytdx>=1.72`

---

### 二、兼容性问题与代码修改

#### 2.1 pymongo 4.x 兼容性

**问题**：旧代码使用了 `insert()`、`update()`、`find().count()`、`collection_names()` 等已废弃 API。

**处理**：

- 统一替换为：
  - `insert()` → `insert_one()` / `insert_many()`
  - `update()` → `update_one()` / `update_many()`
  - `find().count()` → `count_documents({...})`
  - `collection_names()` → `list_collection_names()`
- 涉及模块包括：
  - `QACmd/__init__.py`
  - `QASU/save_*.py` 系列
  - `QAUtil/QAMongo.py`
  - `QIFI/QifiAccount.py`
  - `QAStrategy/qactabase.py`、`qamultibase.py` 等。

#### 2.2 pandas 2.0+ 兼容性

**问题**：`DataFrame.append()`、`.ix[]`、`iteritems()` 等旧接口在 pandas 2.0+ 中被移除或弃用。

**处理**：

- `DataFrame.append(...)` → `pd.concat([...], ignore_index=True)`，主要文件：
  - `QAFetch/QATdx.py`
  - `QAFetch/QATdx_adv.py`
  - `QAData/data_resample.py`
  - `QAUtil/QAcrypto.py`
  - `QAFetch/QAhuobi_realtime.py`
  - `QAData/base_datastruct.py` 等。
- `.ix[]` → `.loc[]`：
  - `QAFetch/QAQuery_Async.py` 中的选择列操作。
- `base_datastruct.py` 中：
  - `iteritems()` → `items()`，兼容 pandas 2.x。

#### 2.3 asyncio 3.10+ 行为变更

**问题**：`asyncio.get_event_loop()` 在 3.10+ 中行为变化，错误使用会触发异常或弃用警告。

**处理**：

- 在协程内部（已有事件循环）使用：

  ```python
  asyncio.get_running_loop()
  ```

  应用：

  - `QAEngine/QAAsyncThread.py`
  - `QAEngine/QAAsyncSchedule.py`

- 在同步代码中需要事件循环时，统一采用：

  ```python
  try:
      loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
  except RuntimeError:
      loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
      asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
  ```


作者信息:trbmilk
时间: 2026.2.25

title: Port GitFive from Python to Go
body:
## Summary

- Complete rewrite of GitFive from Python to Go, producing a single static binary per platform
- All Python code, assets, and config files removed — repository is now a pure Go project
- All 5 CLI commands implemented and verified: `login`, `user`, `email`, `emails`, `light`

## What's included

- **CLI** (cobra): `gitfive-go login|user|email|emails|light` with flags matching the original
- **Auth**: GitHub web login with full 2FA support (TOTP, device verification, GitHub Mobile)
- **API**: Multi-client GitHub API interface with automatic rate-limit switching
- **Scrapers**: Repos, social graph, organizations, commits, PEA detection (goquery)
- **Analysis**: XRAY (deep repo analysis), metamon (email impersonation), email generation, close friends, domain finder
- **Models**: Target, ContribEntry, StringSet with JSON export
- **Cross-compilation**: goreleaser config for linux/darwin/windows × amd64/arm64, `CGO_ENABLED=0`
- **Tests**: 46 tests (unit + E2E), all passing with `-race`

## Verified functionality

| Command | Test |
|---|---|
| `gitfive-go email <addr>` | Correctly resolves email → GitHub account |
| `gitfive-go user <name>` | XRAY finds emails, username history, near names |
| `gitfive-go user <name> --json out.json` | Full JSON export with all discovered data |
| `gitfive-go light <name>` | Quick email discovery from commit history |
| `gitfive-go login` | Interactive auth with session persistence |

## Test plan

- [x] `go test -race ./internal/... ./test/...` — 46 tests pass
- [x] `go build ./cmd/gitfive` — static binary builds
- [x] Manual testing of all 5 commands against real GitHub accounts

Closes #1

title: Modernize for Python 3 with modern build system and CI
body:
## Summary

- **Build system**: Replaced ancient `distutils` `setup.py` with modern `pyproject.toml` using setuptools backend. Added proper metadata, Python >=3.10 requirement, optional dependencies (scipy, mulpyplexer), and dev extras.
- **Python 3 compatibility**: Fixed all Python 2 constructs across the entire codebase (`blob/`, `tests/`, `blob/pyecm/`):
  - `bytes` vs `str` handling — data is now proper `bytes` objects with `latin-1` encoding bridge for string convenience
  - Integer division (`/` → `//`) throughout
  - `xrange` → `range`, `itertools.izip` → `zip`, `tostring()` → `tobytes()`
  - `.encode('hex')` / `.decode('hex')` → `.hex()` / `bytes.fromhex()`
  - `print` statements → `print()` functions
  - Long literals (`0L`, `1L`), `raise` syntax, `raw_input`, `reduce` import, `type(1L)`
- **Tests**: Rewrote test suite for pytest with **149 tests** (up from ~36) covering all Blob operations: construction, size, equality, bitwise ops, concatenation, rotation, block sizes, splitting (by size/count/separator/bits), mp_split, offset, truncation, struct unpacking, bit/byte conversion, indexing (int/float/slice), entropy, chi-square, rotating XORs, element counting, justification, edge cases, and all utility functions.
- **CI**: Added GitHub Actions workflow running tests on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 with ruff linting.
- **Cleanup**: Updated `.gitignore` for modern Python, removed empty `requirements.txt` and `setup.py`.

## Test plan
- [x] All 149 tests pass locally on Python 3.12
- [x] Ruff linting passes with no errors
- [ ] CI runs on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Migrate Cython extensions from Python 2 to Python 3
body:
The three Cython files in `src/cy_ext` (CSeqDict, gem_qa, Delta) were written for Python 2 and fail to compile under Python 3/Cython 3.x.

## Python 2 → 3 syntax fixes

- **Print statements**: `print 'msg'` → `print('msg')`
- **Dict iteration**: `.iteritems()` → `.items()`, `.iterkeys()` → `.keys()`
- **String encoding**: Added UTF-8 encoding for `fopen()` since Python 3 strings are unicode by default

## Cython 3.x compatibility

- **CSeqDict.pxd**: Fixed `cpdef cppclass` → `cdef cppclass` (invalid syntax in modern Cython)
- **CSeqDict.pyx**: Added inline `cdef` attribute declarations and `cdef extern` struct definition for `SeqIDLL` to satisfy stricter type checking

## Build system modernization

- **setup.py**: Updated to use modern Python 3 build tools
  - Replaced deprecated `distutils.core` with `setuptools`
  - Replaced deprecated `Cython.Distutils.build_ext` with `Cython.Build.cythonize`
  - Removed invalid `include_path` parameter
  - Added `language_level: "3"` compiler directive
  - Improved code formatting

## Build artifacts

Updated `.gitignore` to exclude Cython-generated C/C++ files.

---

All modules now compile and import successfully under Python 3.12.

title: Decouple literature/molecule tools and planning mode reliability fixes
body:
## Summary


Extracts literature search and molecule query from `PlanningAgent` internals into standalone orchestrator-level tools, enabling flexible composition with any planning tool. Also, includes a series of fixes to the planning orchestrator discovered during end-to-end campaign testing to improve knowledge synthesis reliability, literature context handling, HTML report quality, and scalarizer script reuse.

## Changes

### 1. Extract literature search and molecule query as orchestrator tools (`orchestrator_tools.py`, `planning_agent.py`, `planning_orchestrator.py`)

**Problem:** Literature search (FutureHouse/Edison) and molecule query tools were embedded inside `PlanningAgent`, tightly coupled to the initial plan generation flow. They couldn't be called independently, reused across tools, or composed flexibly by the orchestrator.

**Fix:** Extracted `search_literature` and `query_molecules` as standalone orchestrator tools. Their outputs (saved as `.md` files in the session directory) can now be passed as `literature_context` or `molecule_context` parameters to `generate_initial_plan`, `refine_plan_with_results`, and `run_economic_analysis`. The orchestrator system prompt guides the LLM to call `search_literature` first, then pass the returned `file_path` to downstream tools.

**Result:** Literature and molecule context is decoupled from plan generation — the orchestrator can search, then decide which tool to feed the results to.

### 2. Chat history compression and compression-safe knowledge synthesis (`planning_orchestrator.py`, `planning_agent.py`, `orchestrator_tools.py`)

**Chat history compression (new):** Long planning campaigns accumulate large tool results in chat history, eventually exceeding LLM context limits. Added `_compress_large_tool_results()` which triggers when total history exceeds 100K chars, truncating individual tool results >30K chars down to 5K chars while preserving the 2 most recent messages. A hint directs the LLM to use `read_file` to re-read full content if needed.

**Knowledge synthesis hardening:** The `synthesize_knowledge` tool previously relied on chat history for experimental outcome details — which compression could truncate. Two changes make synthesis independent of chat history:
- `planning_agent.py`: `experimental_results` now stores the fully parsed content (`consolidated_feedback` from `parse_multimodal_results`) instead of `str(results)` (which was often just a file path). The original raw input is preserved in a new `raw_input` field.
- `orchestrator_tools.py`: `synthesize_knowledge` now pulls experimental outcomes directly from `planner_state["experimental_results"]` and includes them in the synthesis prompt alongside plan designs and human feedback.

**Result:** Knowledge synthesis is fully self-contained — works correctly regardless of whether chat history has been compressed.


### 3. Fix source_documents inheritance in refined plans (`rag_engine.py`)

**Problem:** When `refine_plan_with_feedback` produced a refined plan, it passed the full original plan JSON (including `source_documents`) to the LLM. The LLM simply copied the original plan's reference list forward, making it appear that the refined plan cited the same literature even if it used different sources during refinement.

**Fix:**
- Strip `source_documents` from the plan JSON before passing to the refinement prompt
- Add a constraint telling the LLM to only cite references actually present in the provided literature context

**Result:** Refined plans now list only references consulted during refinement. Confirmed in testing — iteration 2 plans show different, refinement-specific source documents.

### 4. Fix HTML report results rendering (`html_generator.py`)

**Problem (three issues):**
- Results were matched to plan cards by list index, causing experimental results to attach to the TEA card instead of the experiment card
- The full parsed CSV content (hundreds of rows) was dumped verbatim into the HTML report
- Results appeared on TEA cards where they don't belong

**Fix:**
- Match results to plan cards by iteration number instead of list index
- Skip TEA cards entirely (results only appear on experiment cards)
- Show only a minimal filename reference (`📊 Results received: ./data/file.csv`) using the `raw_input` field

### 5. Restrict proactive constraint adjustment (`planning_orchestrator.py`)

**Problem:** After the user approved a refined plan, the orchestrator LLM proactively called `adjust_plan_for_constraints` with self-generated constraints, modifying the plan without user input. This violates co-pilot mode expectations.

**Fix:** Added system prompt guidance:
- **CO_PILOT mode:** Only call `adjust_plan_for_constraints` when the user explicitly provides constraints
- **SUPERVISED/AUTONOMOUS mode:** May be called proactively when clear incompatibilities are identified

**Result:** Confirmed in testing — approved plans proceed without unsolicited constraint adjustments.

### 6. Prioritize locked scalarizer script over LLM schema hints (`orchestrator_tools.py`)

**Problem:** When `analyze_file` was called on a new file with the same data format as previously batch-analyzed files, the LLM passed explicit `inputs`/`targets` arguments. If target names didn't exactly match stored names (e.g., `Integrated_Absorbance` vs `Integrated_Absorbance_Area` — due to non-deterministic LLM naming), the code detected a "schema change" and generated a new script, creating duplicate rows with inconsistent metric names.

**Fix:**
- If a locked script exists and `force_regenerate` is not set, always use the locked script — ignore LLM-supplied schema arguments
- Schema hints (`role_hints`, `_schema_requirements`) are only injected when generating a new script (no locked script exists)
- If the locked script fails on genuinely different data, return a clear error with a hint to use `force_regenerate=True`

**Result:** Same-format files are always processed with the same locked script. Schema transitions require explicit action (`force_regenerate=True` or `reset_analysis_logic`).

### 7. UI model update (`config.py`)

Updated default GPT model from `gpt-5.2` to `gpt-5.4`.


## Known issues not addressed in this PR

- **BO log-transform not implemented:** The BO agent claims to apply log-transforms to skewed targets but the underlying `bo_tools.py` only supports `Standardize(m=1)`. The acquisition function improvement came from kernel/noise changes, not the requested log-transform.
- **Scalarizer naming non-determinism:** The LLM-generated analysis scripts produce slightly different target names across runs (e.g., `Integrated_Absorbance` vs `Integrated_Absorbance_Area`). The locked script mechanism works around this, but the root cause remains.

## Files changed

## Files changed

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `orchestrator_tools.py` | Extract lit/molecule tools, auto-load lit context during refinement, experimental outcomes in synthesis, scalarizer script reuse fix |
| `planning_agent.py` | Decouple lit/molecule search, store parsed experimental content in `data_summary`, add `raw_input` field |
| `planning_orchestrator.py` | System prompt for extracted tools, chat history compression, `adjust_plan_for_constraints` guidance |
| `rag_engine.py` | Strip `source_documents` from refinement prompt, add citation constraint |
| `html_generator.py` | Match results by iteration, skip TEA, show filename only |
| `ui/config.py` | Update GPT model to 5.4 |
| `ui/components/file_viewer.py` | Bump preview limits from 5K/10K to 100K chars |
| `tests/test_planning_campaigns.py` | Campaign test coverage |

title: chore(deps): bump requests from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0
body:
Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.5 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

  • @​M0d3v1 made their first contribution in psf/requests#6865
  • @​aminvakil made their first contribution in psf/requests#7220
  • @​E8Price made their first contribution in psf/requests#6960
  • @​mitre88 made their first contribution in psf/requests#7244
  • @​magsen made their first contribution in psf/requests#6553
  • @​Rohan5commit made their first contribution in psf/requests#7227

Full Changelog: https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/main/HISTORY.md#2330-2026-03-25
